1	"""
2	Step 2 — Chunking & metadata tagging.
3	Converts all raw documents into data/chunks/chunks.jsonl
4	Uses multiprocessing to parallelize across files.
5	"""
6	
7	import json, pathlib, re, hashlib, sys
8	from multiprocessing import Pool, cpu_count
9	from typing import Iterator
10	
11	# ── config ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
12	CHUNK_SIZE  = 1_600
13	OVERLAP     = 200
14	MIN_CHUNK   = 120
15	WORKERS     = min(4, max(1, cpu_count() - 3))
16	
17	RAW_ROOT    = pathlib.Path(__file__).parent / "data/raw"
18	TEXT_ROOT   = pathlib.Path(__file__).parent / "data/text"
19	OUT_DIR     = pathlib.Path(__file__).parent / "data/chunks"
20	OUT_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
21	CHUNKS_FILE = OUT_DIR / "chunks.jsonl"
22	
23	# Pre-compiled regexes
24	_RE_NULL   = re.compile(r'\x00')
25	_RE_SPACES = re.compile(r'[ \t]{3,}')
26	_RE_LINES  = re.compile(r'\n{4,}')
27	_RE_HYPHEN = re.compile(r'([a-z])-\n([a-z])')
28	
29	
30	# ── helpers ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
31	
32	def tokens_approx(text: str) -> int:
33	    return max(1, len(text) // 4)
34	
35	def make_id(source_type: str, doc_name: str, page, chunk_idx: int) -> str:
36	    return f"{source_type}__{doc_name}__p{page}__c{chunk_idx}"
37	
38	def clean_text(text: str) -> str:
39	    text = _RE_NULL.sub('', text)
40	    text = _RE_SPACES.sub('  ', text)
41	    text = _RE_LINES.sub('\n\n\n', text)
42	    text = _RE_HYPHEN.sub(r'\1\2', text)
43	    return text.strip()
44	
45	def sliding_window(text: str) -> Iterator[str]:
46	    text = text.strip()
47	    if not text:
48	        return
49	    if len(text) <= CHUNK_SIZE:
50	        if len(text) >= MIN_CHUNK:
51	            yield text
52	        return
53	    start = 0
54	    while start < len(text):
55	        end   = start + CHUNK_SIZE
56	        chunk = text[start:end]
57	        if end < len(text):
58	            bp = max(chunk.rfind(". ", len(chunk) - 200),
59	                     chunk.rfind("\n",  len(chunk) - 200))
60	            if bp > len(chunk) // 2:
61	                chunk = chunk[:bp + 1]
62	        chunk = chunk.strip()
63	        if len(chunk) >= MIN_CHUNK:
64	            yield chunk
65	        start += len(chunk) - OVERLAP
66	
67	
68	# ── metadata builders ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
69	
70	def usgs_meta(p: pathlib.Path) -> dict:
71	    m    = re.search(r'(\d{4})', p.stem)
72	    year = int(m.group(1)) if m else None
73	    el   = next((e for e in ["lithium","cobalt","rare-earths","nickel",
74	                              "graphite","manganese","tungsten","tin"]
75	                 if e.replace("-","_") in p.stem or e in p.stem), None)
76	    label = f"USGS Mineral Commodity Summary {year}" + (f" — {el.title()}" if el else "")
77	    return {"source": label, "source_type": "usgs", "year": year, "element": el,
78	            "url": f"https://pubs.usgs.gov/periodicals/mcs{year}/mcs{year}.pdf",
79	            "doc_path": str(p.relative_to(RAW_ROOT))}
80	
81	def usgs_text_meta(p: pathlib.Path) -> dict:
82	    return usgs_meta(pathlib.Path(str(p).replace("data/text/", "data/raw/").replace(".txt", ".pdf")))
83	
84	def sec_meta(p: pathlib.Path) -> dict:
85	    company = p.parent.name
86	    return {"source": f"SEC Form SD — Conflict Minerals Disclosure ({company.upper()})",
87	            "source_type": "sec_edgar", "year": None, "company": company,
88	            "url": "https://www.sec.gov/cgi-bin/browse-edgar",
89	            "doc_path": str(p.relative_to(RAW_ROOT))}
90	
91	def comtrade_meta(p: pathlib.Path) -> dict:
92	    parts = p.stem.split("_")
93	    year  = int(parts[-1]) if parts[-1].isdigit() else None
94	    flow  = "Exports" if "X" in parts else "Imports"
95	    return {"source": f"UN Comtrade — HS {parts[0]} {flow} {year}",
96	            "source_type": "comtrade", "year": year, "hs_code": parts[0], "flow": flow,
97	            "url": "https://comtradeplus.un.org/",
98	            "doc_path": str(p.relative_to(RAW_ROOT))}
99	
100	def misc_meta(p: pathlib.Path) -> dict:
101	    raw_str = str(p).replace("/data/text/misc/", "/data/raw/misc/").replace(".txt", ".pdf")
102	    try:
103	        doc_path = str(pathlib.Path(raw_str).relative_to(RAW_ROOT))
104	    except ValueError:
105	        doc_path = p.name
106	    return {"source": f"External Report — {p.stem.replace('_',' ').title()}",
107	            "source_type": "misc", "year": None, "url": "", "doc_path": doc_path}
108	
109	
110	# ── worker (runs in subprocess) ───────────────────────────────────────────────
111	
112	def process_file(args: tuple) -> list[dict]:
113	    path_str, meta_fn_name, is_pdf = args
114	    path     = pathlib.Path(path_str)
115	    meta_fn  = globals()[meta_fn_name]
116	    meta     = meta_fn(path)
117	
118	    if is_pdf:
119	        # should not happen — all PDFs pre-extracted, but keep as safety net
120	        try:
121	            import pdfplumber
122	            chunks = []
123	            with pdfplumber.open(path) as pdf:
124	                for pg_num, pg in enumerate(pdf.pages, 1):
125	                    raw = pg.extract_text()
126	                    if not raw:
127	                        continue
128	                    text = clean_text(raw)
129	                    for idx, chunk in enumerate(sliding_window(text)):
130	                        chunks.append({**meta,
131	                            "id": make_id(meta["source_type"], path.stem, pg_num, idx),
132	                            "text": chunk, "page": pg_num, "chunk_index": idx,
133	                            "tokens_approx": tokens_approx(chunk)})
134	            return chunks
135	        except Exception as e:
136	            print(f"  ✗ PDF {path.name}: {e}", flush=True)
137	            return []
138	
139	    try:
140	        raw  = path.read_text(encoding="utf-8", errors="ignore")
141	        text = clean_text(raw)
142	        return [{**meta,
143	                 "id": make_id(meta["source_type"], path.stem, 0, idx),
144	                 "text": chunk, "page": None, "chunk_index": idx,
145	                 "tokens_approx": tokens_approx(chunk)}
146	                for idx, chunk in enumerate(sliding_window(text))]
147	    except Exception as e:
148	        print(f"  ✗ {path.name}: {e}", flush=True)
149	        return []
150	
151	
152	# ── file collection ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
153	
154	_misc_pre = {p.stem for p in (TEXT_ROOT/"misc").glob("*.txt")} \
155	            if (TEXT_ROOT/"misc").exists() else set()
156	
157	SOURCES = [
158	    (TEXT_ROOT/"usgs",        "*.txt",    False, "usgs_text_meta"),
159	    (TEXT_ROOT/"misc",        "*.txt",    False, "misc_meta"),
160	    (RAW_ROOT/"sec_edgar",    "**/*.txt", False, "sec_meta"),
161	    (RAW_ROOT/"comtrade_text","*.txt",    False, "comtrade_meta"),
162	    (RAW_ROOT/"misc",         "**/*.txt", False, "misc_meta"),
163	]
164	
165	def collect_files() -> list[tuple]:
166	    files = []
167	    seen  = set()
168	    for folder, glob, is_pdf, fn_name in SOURCES:
169	        if not folder.exists():
170	            continue
171	        for p in sorted(folder.glob(glob)):
172	            if not p.is_file() or p.name == "_manifest.json":
173	                continue
174	            key = p.stem
175	            if key in seen:          # skip raw/misc txt if already in text/misc
176	                continue
177	            seen.add(key)
178	            files.append((str(p), fn_name, is_pdf))
179	    # any misc PDFs not yet pre-extracted
180	    misc_pdf = RAW_ROOT / "misc"
181	    if misc_pdf.exists():
182	        for p in sorted(misc_pdf.glob("**/*.pdf")):
183	            if p.stem not in _misc_pre:
184	                files.append((str(p), "misc_meta", True))
185	    return files
186	
187	
188	# ── main ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
189	
190	def main():
191	    files = collect_files()
192	    n     = len(files)
193	    print(f"\n✓ {n} source files  •  {WORKERS} workers\n")
194	
195	    seen_hashes : set[str] = set()
196	    total_chunks = total_tokens = skipped = 0
197	
198	    with open(CHUNKS_FILE, "w", encoding="utf-8") as out, \
199	         Pool(processes=WORKERS) as pool:
200	
201	        for i, chunks in enumerate(pool.imap_unordered(process_file, files, chunksize=1), 1):
202	            name = pathlib.Path(files[i-1][0]).name if i <= n else "?"
203	            pct  = int(i / n * 100)
204	            print(f"  [{i:>3}/{n}] {pct:>3}%  +{len(chunks):>4} chunks  {name}", flush=True)
205	
206	            for chunk in chunks:
207	                h = hashlib.md5(chunk["text"].encode()).hexdigest()[:8]
208	                if h in seen_hashes:
209	                    skipped += 1
210	                    continue
211	                seen_hashes.add(h)
212	                out.write(json.dumps(chunk, ensure_ascii=False) + "\n")
213	                total_chunks += 1
214	                total_tokens += chunk["tokens_approx"]
215	
216	    # breakdown
217	    by_type: dict[str, int] = {}
218	    with open(CHUNKS_FILE, encoding="utf-8") as f:
219	        for line in f:
220	            st = json.loads(line).get("source_type", "?")
221	            by_type[st] = by_type.get(st, 0) + 1
222	
223	    mx = max(by_type.values()) if by_type else 1
224	    print(f"\n{'═'*55}")
225	    print(f"  Chunks written : {total_chunks:,}  →  {CHUNKS_FILE}")
226	    print(f"  Approx tokens  : {total_tokens:,}  (~{total_tokens//1000}k)")
227	    print(f"  Duplicates     : {skipped:,}")
228	    print(f"\n  By source type:")
229	    for st, c in sorted(by_type.items(), key=lambda x: -x[1]):
230	        print(f"    {st:<18} {c:>6}  {'█' * (c // max(1, mx // 30))}")
231	    print(f"{'═'*55}")
232	    print(f"\n  ✓ Ready →  python 06_embed.py")

112	def process_file(args: tuple) -> list[dict]:

118	    if is_pdf:

144	                 "text": chunk, "page": None, "chunk_index": idx,
145	                 "tokens_approx": tokens_approx(chunk)}
146	                for idx, chunk in enumerate(sliding_window(text))]

154	_misc_pre = {p.stem for p in (TEXT_ROOT/"misc").glob("*.txt")} \

157	SOURCES = [

160	    (RAW_ROOT/"sec_edgar",    "**/*.txt", False, "sec_meta"),

162	    (RAW_ROOT/"misc",         "**/*.txt", False, "misc_meta"),
163	]

172	            if not p.is_file() or p.name == "_manifest.json":
173	                continue

179	    # any misc PDFs not yet pre-extracted

181	    if misc_pdf.exists():

183	            if p.stem not in _misc_pre:

198	    with open(CHUNKS_FILE, "w", encoding="utf-8") as out, \
199	         Pool(processes=WORKERS) as pool:
200

202	            name = pathlib.Path(files[i-1][0]).name if i <= n else "?"
203	            pct  = int(i / n * 100)
204	            print(f"  [{i:>3}/{n}] {pct:>3}%  +{len(chunks):>4} chunks  {name}", flush=True)

206	            for chunk in chunks:

210	                    continue

212	                out.write(json.dumps(chunk, ensure_ascii=False) + "\n")
213	                total_chunks += 1

215	
216	    # breakdown

218	    with open(CHUNKS_FILE, encoding="utf-8") as f:
219	        for line in f:

221	            by_type[st] = by_type.get(st, 0) + 1
222	
223	    mx = max(by_type.values()) if by_type else 1
224	    print(f"\n{'═'*55}")
225	    print(f"  Chunks written : {total_chunks:,}  →  {CHUNKS_FILE}")
226	    print(f"  Approx tokens  : {total_tokens:,}  (~{total_tokens//1000}k)")
227	    print(f"  Duplicates     : {skipped:,}")
228	    print(f"\n  By source type:")
229	    for st, c in sorted(by_type.items(), key=lambda x: -x[1]):
230	        print(f"    {st:<18} {c:>6}  {'█' * (c // max(1, mx // 30))}")
231	    print(f"{'═'*55}")
232	    print(f"\n  ✓ Ready →  python 06_embed.py")
233	
234	
235	if __name__ == "__main__":
236	    main()

query:
The script might be stuck in `collect_files()` — specifically the `**/*.txt` recursive glob on `sec_edgar` can take minutes if the directory tree is deep. Nothing prints until that returns.

1	"""
2	Step 2 — Chunking & metadata tagging.
3	Converts all raw documents into data/chunks/chunks.jsonl
4	Uses multiprocessing to parallelize across files.
5	"""
6	
7	import json, pathlib, re, hashlib, sys
8	from multiprocessing import Pool, cpu_count
9	from typing import Iterator
10	
11	# ── config ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
12	CHUNK_SIZE  = 1_600
13	OVERLAP     = 200
14	MIN_CHUNK   = 120
15	WORKERS     = min(4, max(1, cpu_count() - 3))
16	
17	RAW_ROOT    = pathlib.Path(__file__).parent / "data/raw"
18	TEXT_ROOT   = pathlib.Path(__file__).parent / "data/text"
19	OUT_DIR     = pathlib.Path(__file__).parent / "data/chunks"
20	OUT_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
21	CHUNKS_FILE = OUT_DIR / "chunks.jsonl"
22	
23	# Pre-compiled regexes
24	_RE_NULL   = re.compile(r'\x00')
25	_RE_SPACES = re.compile(r'[ \t]{3,}')
26	_RE_LINES  = re.compile(r'\n{4,}')
27	_RE_HYPHEN = re.compile(r'([a-z])-\n([a-z])')
28	
29	
30	# ── helpers ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
31	
32	def tokens_approx(text: str) -> int:
33	    return max(1, len(text) // 4)
34	
35	def make_id(source_type: str, doc_name: str, page, chunk_idx: int) -> str:
36	    return f"{source_type}__{doc_name}__p{page}__c{chunk_idx}"
37	
38	def clean_text(text: str) -> str:
39	    text = _RE_NULL.sub('', text)
40	    text = _RE_SPACES.sub('  ', text)
41	    text = _RE_LINES.sub('\n\n\n', text)
42	    text = _RE_HYPHEN.sub(r'\1\2', text)
43	    return text.strip()
44	
45	def sliding_window(text: str) -> Iterator[str]:
46	    text = text.strip()
47	    if not text:
48	        return
49	    if len(text) <= CHUNK_SIZE:
50	        if len(text) >= MIN_CHUNK:
51	            yield text
52	        return
53	    start = 0
54	    while start < len(text):
55	        end   = start + CHUNK_SIZE
56	        chunk = text[start:end]
57	        if end < len(text):
58	            bp = max(chunk.rfind(". ", len(chunk) - 200),
59	                     chunk.rfind("\n",  len(chunk) - 200))
60	            if bp > len(chunk) // 2:
61	                chunk = chunk[:bp + 1]
62	        chunk = chunk.strip()
63	        if len(chunk) >= MIN_CHUNK:
64	            yield chunk
65	        start += len(chunk) - OVERLAP
66	
67	
68	# ── metadata builders ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
69	
70	def usgs_meta(p: pathlib.Path) -> dict:
71	    m    = re.search(r'(\d{4})', p.stem)
72	    year = int(m.group(1)) if m else None
73	    el   = next((e for e in ["lithium","cobalt","rare-earths","nickel",
74	                              "graphite","manganese","tungsten","tin"]
75	                 if e.replace("-","_") in p.stem or e in p.stem), None)
76	    label = f"USGS Mineral Commodity Summary {year}" + (f" — {el.title()}" if el else "")
77	    return {"source": label, "source_type": "usgs", "year": year, "element": el,
78	            "url": f"https://pubs.usgs.gov/periodicals/mcs{year}/mcs{year}.pdf",
79	            "doc_path": str(p.relative_to(RAW_ROOT))}
80	
81	def usgs_text_meta(p: pathlib.Path) -> dict:
82	    return usgs_meta(pathlib.Path(str(p).replace("data/text/", "data/raw/").replace(".txt", ".pdf")))
83	
84	def sec_meta(p: pathlib.Path) -> dict:
85	    company = p.parent.name
86	    return {"source": f"SEC Form SD — Conflict Minerals Disclosure ({company.upper()})",
87	            "source_type": "sec_edgar", "year": None, "company": company,
88	            "url": "https://www.sec.gov/cgi-bin/browse-edgar",
89	            "doc_path": str(p.relative_to(RAW_ROOT))}
90	
91	def comtrade_meta(p: pathlib.Path) -> dict:
92	    parts = p.stem.split("_")
93	    year  = int(parts[-1]) if parts[-1].isdigit() else None
94	    flow  = "Exports" if "X" in parts else "Imports"
95	    return {"source": f"UN Comtrade — HS {parts[0]} {flow} {year}",
96	            "source_type": "comtrade", "year": year, "hs_code": parts[0], "flow": flow,
97	            "url": "https://comtradeplus.un.org/",
98	            "doc_path": str(p.relative_to(RAW_ROOT))}
99	
100	def misc_meta(p: pathlib.Path) -> dict:
101	    raw_str = str(p).replace("/data/text/misc/", "/data/raw/misc/").replace(".txt", ".pdf")
102	    try:
103	        doc_path = str(pathlib.Path(raw_str).relative_to(RAW_ROOT))
104	    except ValueError:
105	        doc_path = p.name
106	    return {"source": f"External Report — {p.stem.replace('_',' ').title()}",
107	            "source_type": "misc", "year": None, "url": "", "doc_path": doc_path}
108	
109	
110	# ── worker (runs in subprocess) ───────────────────────────────────────────────
111	
112	def process_file(args: tuple) -> list[dict]:
113	    path_str, meta_fn_name, is_pdf = args
114	    path     = pathlib.Path(path_str)
115	    meta_fn  = globals()[meta_fn_name]
116	    meta     = meta_fn(path)
117	
118	    if is_pdf:
119	        # should not happen — all PDFs pre-extracted, but keep as safety net
120	        try:
121	            import pdfplumber
122	            chunks = []
123	            with pdfplumber.open(path) as pdf:
124	                for pg_num, pg in enumerate(pdf.pages, 1):
125	                    raw = pg.extract_text()
126	                    if not raw:
127	                        continue
128	                    text = clean_text(raw)
129	                    for idx, chunk in enumerate(sliding_window(text)):
130	                        chunks.append({**meta,
131	                            "id": make_id(meta["source_type"], path.stem, pg_num, idx),
132	                            "text": chunk, "page": pg_num, "chunk_index": idx,
133	                            "tokens_approx": tokens_approx(chunk)})
134	            return chunks
135	        except Exception as e:
136	            print(f"  ✗ PDF {path.name}: {e}", flush=True)
137	            return []
138	
139	    try:
140	        raw  = path.read_text(encoding="utf-8", errors="ignore")
141	        text = clean_text(raw)
142	        return [{**meta,
143	                 "id": make_id(meta["source_type"], path.stem, 0, idx),
144	                 "text": chunk, "page": None, "chunk_index": idx,
145	                 "tokens_approx": tokens_approx(chunk)}
146	                for idx, chunk in enumerate(sliding_window(text))]
147	    except Exception as e:
148	        print(f"  ✗ {path.name}: {e}", flush=True)
149	        return []
150	
151	
152	# ── file collection ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
153	
154	_misc_pre = {p.stem for p in (TEXT_ROOT/"misc").glob("*.txt")} \
155	            if (TEXT_ROOT/"misc").exists() else set()
156	
157	SOURCES = [
158	    (TEXT_ROOT/"usgs",        "*.txt",    False, "usgs_text_meta"),
159	    (TEXT_ROOT/"misc",        "*.txt",    False, "misc_meta"),
160	    (RAW_ROOT/"sec_edgar",    "**/*.txt", False, "sec_meta"),
161	    (RAW_ROOT/"comtrade_text","*.txt",    False, "comtrade_meta"),
162	    (RAW_ROOT/"misc",         "**/*.txt", False, "misc_meta"),
163	]
164	
165	def collect_files() -> list[tuple]:
166	    files = []
167	    seen  = set()
168	    for folder, glob, is_pdf, fn_name in SOURCES:
169	        if not folder.exists():
170	            continue
171	        for p in sorted(folder.glob(glob)):
172	            if not p.is_file() or p.name == "_manifest.json":
173	                continue
174	            key = p.stem
175	            if key in seen:          # skip raw/misc txt if already in text/misc
176	                continue
177	            seen.add(key)
178	            files.append((str(p), fn_name, is_pdf))
179	    # any misc PDFs not yet pre-extracted
180	    misc_pdf = RAW_ROOT / "misc"
181	    if misc_pdf.exists():
182	        for p in sorted(misc_pdf.glob("**/*.pdf")):
183	            if p.stem not in _misc_pre:
184	                files.append((str(p), "misc_meta", True))
185	    return files
186	
187	
188	# ── main ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
189	
190	def main():
191	    files = collect_files()
192	    n     = len(files)
193	    print(f"\n✓ {n} source files  •  {WORKERS} workers\n")
194	
195	    seen_hashes : set[str] = set()
196	    total_chunks = total_tokens = skipped = 0
197	
198	    with open(CHUNKS_FILE, "w", encoding="utf-8") as out, \
199	         Pool(processes=WORKERS) as pool:
200	
201	        for i, chunks in enumerate(pool.imap_unordered(process_file, files, chunksize=1), 1):
202	            name = pathlib.Path(files[i-1][0]).name if i <= n else "?"
203	            pct  = int(i / n * 100)
204	            print(f"  [{i:>3}/{n}] {pct:>3}%  +{len(chunks):>4} chunks  {name}", flush=True)
205	
206	            for chunk in chunks:
207	                h = hashlib.md5(chunk["text"].encode()).hexdigest()[:8]
208	                if h in seen_hashes:
209	                    skipped += 1
210	                    continue
211	                seen_hashes.add(h)
212	                out.write(json.dumps(chunk, ensure_ascii=False) + "\n")
213	                total_chunks += 1
214	                total_tokens += chunk["tokens_approx"]
215	
216	    # breakdown
217	    by_type: dict[str, int] = {}
218	    with open(CHUNKS_FILE, encoding="utf-8") as f:
219	        for line in f:
220	            st = json.loads(line).get("source_type", "?")
221	            by_type[st] = by_type.get(st, 0) + 1
222	
223	    mx = max(by_type.values()) if by_type else 1
224	    print(f"\n{'═'*55}")
225	    print(f"  Chunks written : {total_chunks:,}  →  {CHUNKS_FILE}")
226	    print(f"  Approx tokens  : {total_tokens:,}  (~{total_tokens//1000}k)")
227	    print(f"  Duplicates     : {skipped:,}")
228	    print(f"\n  By source type:")
229	    for st, c in sorted(by_type.items(), key=lambda x: -x[1]):
230	        print(f"    {st:<18} {c:>6}  {'█' * (c // max(1, mx // 30))}")
231	    print(f"{'═'*55}")
232	    print(f"\n  ✓ Ready →  python 06_embed.py")
233	
234	
235	if __name__ == "__main__":
236	    main()

1	"""
2	Step 2 — Chunking & metadata tagging.
3	Converts all raw documents into data/chunks/chunks.jsonl
4	Uses multiprocessing to parallelize across files.
5	"""
6	
7	import json, pathlib, re, hashlib, sys
8	from multiprocessing import Pool, cpu_count
9	from typing import Iterator
10	
11	# ── config ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
12	CHUNK_SIZE  = 1_600
13	OVERLAP     = 200
14	MIN_CHUNK   = 120
15	WORKERS     = min(4, max(1, cpu_count() - 3))
16	
17	RAW_ROOT    = pathlib.Path(__file__).parent / "data/raw"
18	TEXT_ROOT   = pathlib.Path(__file__).parent / "data/text"
19	OUT_DIR     = pathlib.Path(__file__).parent / "data/chunks"
20	OUT_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
21	CHUNKS_FILE = OUT_DIR / "chunks.jsonl"
22	
23	# Pre-compiled regexes
24	_RE_NULL   = re.compile(r'\x00')
25	_RE_SPACES = re.compile(r'[ \t]{3,}')
26	_RE_LINES  = re.compile(r'\n{4,}')
27	_RE_HYPHEN = re.compile(r'([a-z])-\n([a-z])')
28	
29	
30	# ── helpers ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
31	
32	def tokens_approx(text: str) -> int:
33	    return max(1, len(text) // 4)
34	
35	def make_id(source_type: str, doc_name: str, page, chunk_idx: int) -> str:
36	    return f"{source_type}__{doc_name}__p{page}__c{chunk_idx}"
37	
38	def clean_text(text: str) -> str:
39	    text = _RE_NULL.sub('', text)
40	    text = _RE_SPACES.sub('  ', text)
41	    text = _RE_LINES.sub('\n\n\n', text)
42	    text = _RE_HYPHEN.sub(r'\1\2', text)
43	    return text.strip()
44	
45	def sliding_window(text: str) -> Iterator[str]:
46	    text = text.strip()
47	    if not text:
48	        return
49	    if len(text) <= CHUNK_SIZE:
50	        if len(text) >= MIN_CHUNK:
51	            yield text
52	        return
53	    start = 0
54	    while start < len(text):
55	        end   = start + CHUNK_SIZE
56	        chunk = text[start:end]
57	        if end < len(text):
58	            bp = max(chunk.rfind(". ", len(chunk) - 200),
59	                     chunk.rfind("\n",  len(chunk) - 200))
60	            if bp > len(chunk) // 2:
61	                chunk = chunk[:bp + 1]
62	        chunk = chunk.strip()
63	        if len(chunk) >= MIN_CHUNK:
64	            yield chunk
65	        start += len(chunk) - OVERLAP
66	
67	
68	# ── metadata builders ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
69	
70	def usgs_meta(p: pathlib.Path) -> dict:
71	    m    = re.search(r'(\d{4})', p.stem)
72	    year = int(m.group(1)) if m else None
73	    el   = next((e for e in ["lithium","cobalt","rare-earths","nickel",
74	                              "graphite","manganese","tungsten","tin"]
75	                 if e.replace("-","_") in p.stem or e in p.stem), None)
76	    label = f"USGS Mineral Commodity Summary {year}" + (f" — {el.title()}" if el else "")
77	    return {"source": label, "source_type": "usgs", "year": year, "element": el,
78	            "url": f"https://pubs.usgs.gov/periodicals/mcs{year}/mcs{year}.pdf",
79	            "doc_path": str(p.relative_to(RAW_ROOT))}
80	
81	def usgs_text_meta(p: pathlib.Path) -> dict:
82	    return usgs_meta(pathlib.Path(str(p).replace("data/text/", "data/raw/").replace(".txt", ".pdf")))
83	
84	def sec_meta(p: pathlib.Path) -> dict:
85	    company = p.parent.name
86	    return {"source": f"SEC Form SD — Conflict Minerals Disclosure ({company.upper()})",
87	            "source_type": "sec_edgar", "year": None, "company": company,
88	            "url": "https://www.sec.gov/cgi-bin/browse-edgar",
89	            "doc_path": str(p.relative_to(RAW_ROOT))}
90	
91	def comtrade_meta(p: pathlib.Path) -> dict:
92	    parts = p.stem.split("_")
93	    year  = int(parts[-1]) if parts[-1].isdigit() else None
94	    flow  = "Exports" if "X" in parts else "Imports"
95	    return {"source": f"UN Comtrade — HS {parts[0]} {flow} {year}",
96	            "source_type": "comtrade", "year": year, "hs_code": parts[0], "flow": flow,
97	            "url": "https://comtradeplus.un.org/",
98	            "doc_path": str(p.relative_to(RAW_ROOT))}
99	
100	def misc_meta(p: pathlib.Path) -> dict:
101	    raw_str = str(p).replace("/data/text/misc/", "/data/raw/misc/").replace(".txt", ".pdf")
102	    try:
103	        doc_path = str(pathlib.Path(raw_str).relative_to(RAW_ROOT))
104	    except ValueError:
105	        doc_path = p.name
106	    return {"source": f"External Report — {p.stem.replace('_',' ').title()}",
107	            "source_type": "misc", "year": None, "url": "", "doc_path": doc_path}
108	
109	
110	# ── worker (runs in subprocess) ───────────────────────────────────────────────
111	
112	def process_file(args: tuple) -> list[dict]:
113	    path_str, meta_fn_name, is_pdf = args
114	    path     = pathlib.Path(path_str)
115	    meta_fn  = globals()[meta_fn_name]
116	    meta     = meta_fn(path)
117	
118	    if is_pdf:
119	        # should not happen — all PDFs pre-extracted, but keep as safety net
120	        try:
121	            import pdfplumber
122	            chunks = []
123	            with pdfplumber.open(path) as pdf:
124	                for pg_num, pg in enumerate(pdf.pages, 1):
125	                    raw = pg.extract_text()
126	                    if not raw:
127	                        continue
128	                    text = clean_text(raw)
129	                    for idx, chunk in enumerate(sliding_window(text)):
130	                        chunks.append({**meta,
131	                            "id": make_id(meta["source_type"], path.stem, pg_num, idx),
132	                            "text": chunk, "page": pg_num, "chunk_index": idx,
133	                            "tokens_approx": tokens_approx(chunk)})
134	            return chunks
135	        except Exception as e:
136	            print(f"  ✗ PDF {path.name}: {e}", flush=True)
137	            return []
138	
139	    try:
140	        raw  = path.read_text(encoding="utf-8", errors="ignore")
141	        text = clean_text(raw)
142	        return [{**meta,
143	                 "id": make_id(meta["source_type"], path.stem, 0, idx),
144	                 "text": chunk, "page": None, "chunk_index": idx,
145	                 "tokens_approx": tokens_approx(chunk)}
146	                for idx, chunk in enumerate(sliding_window(text))]
147	    except Exception as e:
148	        print(f"  ✗ {path.name}: {e}", flush=True)
149	        return []
150	
151	
152	# ── file collection ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
153	
154	_misc_pre = {p.stem for p in (TEXT_ROOT/"misc").glob("*.txt")} \
155	            if (TEXT_ROOT/"misc").exists() else set()
156	
157	SOURCES = [
158	    (TEXT_ROOT/"usgs",        "*.txt",    False, "usgs_text_meta"),
159	    (TEXT_ROOT/"misc",        "*.txt",    False, "misc_meta"),
160	    (RAW_ROOT/"sec_edgar",    "**/*.txt", False, "sec_meta"),
161	    (RAW_ROOT/"comtrade_text","*.txt",    False, "comtrade_meta"),
162	    (RAW_ROOT/"misc",         "**/*.txt", False, "misc_meta"),
163	]
164	
165	def collect_files() -> list[tuple]:
166	    files = []
167	    seen  = set()
168	    for folder, glob, is_pdf, fn_name in SOURCES:
169	        if not folder.exists():
170	            continue
171	        for p in sorted(folder.glob(glob)):
172	            if not p.is_file() or p.name == "_manifest.json":
173	                continue
174	            key = p.stem
175	            if key in seen:          # skip raw/misc txt if already in text/misc
176	                continue
177	            seen.add(key)
178	            files.append((str(p), fn_name, is_pdf))
179	    # any misc PDFs not yet pre-extracted
180	    misc_pdf = RAW_ROOT / "misc"
181	    if misc_pdf.exists():
182	        for p in sorted(misc_pdf.glob("**/*.pdf")):
183	            if p.stem not in _misc_pre:
184	                files.append((str(p), "misc_meta", True))
185	    return files
186	
187	
188	# ── main ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
189	
190	def main():
191	    files = collect_files()
192	    n     = len(files)
193	    print(f"\n✓ {n} source files  •  {WORKERS} workers\n")
194	
195	    seen_hashes : set[str] = set()
196	    total_chunks = total_tokens = skipped = 0
197	
198	    with open(CHUNKS_FILE, "w", encoding="utf-8") as out, \
199	         Pool(processes=WORKERS) as pool:
200	
201	        for i, chunks in enumerate(pool.imap_unordered(process_file, files, chunksize=1), 1):
202	            name = pathlib.Path(files[i-1][0]).name if i <= n else "?"
203	            pct  = int(i / n * 100)
204	            print(f"  [{i:>3}/{n}] {pct:>3}%  +{len(chunks):>4} chunks  {name}", flush=True)
205	
206	            for chunk in chunks:
207	                h = hashlib.md5(chunk["text"].encode()).hexdigest()[:8]
208	                if h in seen_hashes:
209	                    skipped += 1
210	                    continue
211	                seen_hashes.add(h)
212	                out.write(json.dumps(chunk, ensure_ascii=False) + "\n")
213	                total_chunks += 1
214	                total_tokens += chunk["tokens_approx"]
215	
216	    # breakdown
217	    by_type: dict[str, int] = {}
218	    with open(CHUNKS_FILE, encoding="utf-8") as f:
219	        for line in f:
220	            st = json.loads(line).get("source_type", "?")
221	            by_type[st] = by_type.get(st, 0) + 1
222	
223	    mx = max(by_type.values()) if by_type else 1
224	    print(f"\n{'═'*55}")
225	    print(f"  Chunks written : {total_chunks:,}  →  {CHUNKS_FILE}")
226	    print(f"  Approx tokens  : {total_tokens:,}  (~{total_tokens//1000}k)")
227	    print(f"  Duplicates     : {skipped:,}")
228	    print(f"\n  By source type:")
229	    for st, c in sorted(by_type.items(), key=lambda x: -x[1]):
230	        print(f"    {st:<18} {c:>6}  {'█' * (c // max(1, mx // 30))}")
231	    print(f"{'═'*55}")
232	    print(f"\n  ✓ Ready →  python 06_embed.py")
233	
234	
235	if __name__ == "__main__":
236	    main()
237

query:
Found the root cause. `path.read_text()` loads the whole file, then `clean_text` makes a second copy, then all chunks are materialized into a list simultaneously. For large SEC filings this can be 200MB+ per file.

The fix: convert `process_file` to a generator that streams chunks one at a time, and explicitly free the raw text before yielding:
original_content:

2	Step 2 — Chunking & metadata tagging.

4	Uses multiprocessing to parallelize across files.

45	def sliding_window(text: str) -> Iterator[str]:
46	    text = text.strip()
47	    if not text:
48	        return
49	    if len(text) <= CHUNK_SIZE:
50	        if len(text) >= MIN_CHUNK:
51	            yield text
52	        return
53	    start = 0
54	    while start < len(text):
55	        end   = start + CHUNK_SIZE
56	        chunk = text[start:end]

58	            bp = max(chunk.rfind(". ", len(chunk) - 200),
59	                     chunk.rfind("\n",  len(chunk) - 200))
60	            if bp > len(chunk) // 2:
61	                chunk = chunk[:bp + 1]
62	        chunk = chunk.strip()
63	        if len(chunk) >= MIN_CHUNK:
64	            yield chunk
65	        start += len(chunk) - OVERLAP

92	    parts = p.stem.split("_")
93	    year  = int(parts[-1]) if parts[-1].isdigit() else None
94	    flow  = "Exports" if "X" in parts else "Imports"

100	def misc_meta(p: pathlib.Path) -> dict:
101	    raw_str = str(p).replace("/data/text/misc/", "/data/raw/misc/").replace(".txt", ".pdf")
102	    try:
103	        doc_path = str(pathlib.Path(raw_str).relative_to(RAW_ROOT))
104	    except ValueError:
105	        doc_path = p.name
106	    return {"source": f"External Report — {p.stem.replace('_',' ').title()}",
107	            "source_type": "misc", "year": None, "url": "", "doc_path": doc_path}
108	
109	
110	# ── worker (runs in subprocess) ───────────────────────────────────────────────
111	
112	def process_file(args: tuple) -> list[dict]:
113	    path_str, meta_fn_name, is_pdf = args
114	    path     = pathlib.Path(path_str)
115	    meta_fn  = globals()[meta_fn_name]
116	    meta     = meta_fn(path)
117	
118	    if is_pdf:
119	        # should not happen — all PDFs pre-extracted, but keep as safety net
120	        try:
121	            import pdfplumber
122	            chunks = []
123	            with pdfplumber.open(path) as pdf:
124	                for pg_num, pg in enumerate(pdf.pages, 1):
125	                    raw = pg.extract_text()
126	                    if not raw:
127	                        continue
128	                    text = clean_text(raw)
129	                    for idx, chunk in enumerate(sliding_window(text)):
130	                        chunks.append({**meta,
131	                            "id": make_id(meta["source_type"], path.stem, pg_num, idx),
132	                            "text": chunk, "page": pg_num, "chunk_index": idx,
133	                            "tokens_approx": tokens_approx(chunk)})
134	            return chunks
135	        except Exception as e:
136	            print(f"  ✗ PDF {path.name}: {e}", flush=True)
137	            return []
138	
139	    try:
140	        raw  = path.read_text(encoding="utf-8", errors="ignore")
141	        text = clean_text(raw)
142	        return [{**meta,
143	                 "id": make_id(meta["source_type"], path.stem, 0, idx),
144	                 "text": chunk, "page": None, "chunk_index": idx,
145	                 "tokens_approx": tokens_approx(chunk)}
146	                for idx, chunk in enumerate(sliding_window(text))]
147	    except Exception as e:
148	        print(f"  ✗ {path.name}: {e}", flush=True)
149	        return []

152	# ── file collection ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
153	
154	_misc_pre = {p.stem for p in (TEXT_ROOT/"misc").glob("*.txt")} \
155	            if (TEXT_ROOT/"misc").exists() else set()
156	
157	SOURCES = [
158	    (TEXT_ROOT/"usgs",        "*.txt",    False, "usgs_text_meta"),
159	    (TEXT_ROOT/"misc",        "*.txt",    False, "misc_meta"),
160	    (RAW_ROOT/"sec_edgar",    "**/*.txt", False, "sec_meta"),
161	    (RAW_ROOT/"comtrade_text","*.txt",    False, "comtrade_meta"),
162	    (RAW_ROOT/"misc",         "**/*.txt", False, "misc_meta"),
163	]
164	
165	def collect_files() -> list[tuple]:
166	    files = []
167	    seen  = set()
168	    for folder, glob, is_pdf, fn_name in SOURCES:
169	        if not folder.exists():
170	            continue
171	        for p in sorted(folder.glob(glob)):
172	            if not p.is_file() or p.name == "_manifest.json":
173	                continue
174	            key = p.stem
175	            if key in seen:          # skip raw/misc txt if already in text/misc
176	                continue
177	            seen.add(key)
178	            files.append((str(p), fn_name, is_pdf))
179	    # any misc PDFs not yet pre-extracted
180	    misc_pdf = RAW_ROOT / "misc"
181	    if misc_pdf.exists():
182	        for p in sorted(misc_pdf.glob("**/*.pdf")):
183	            if p.stem not in _misc_pre:
184	                files.append((str(p), "misc_meta", True))
185	    return files
186	
187	
188	# ── main ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
189	
190	def main():
191	    files = collect_files()
192	    n     = len(files)
193	    print(f"\n✓ {n} source files  •  {WORKERS} workers\n")
194	
195	    seen_hashes : set[str] = set()
196	    total_chunks = total_tokens = skipped = 0
197	
198	    with open(CHUNKS_FILE, "w", encoding="utf-8") as out, \
199	         Pool(processes=WORKERS) as pool:
200	
201	        for i, chunks in enumerate(pool.imap_unordered(process_file, files, chunksize=1), 1):
202	            name = pathlib.Path(files[i-1][0]).name if i <= n else "?"
203	            pct  = int(i / n * 100)
204	            print(f"  [{i:>3}/{n}] {pct:>3}%  +{len(chunks):>4} chunks  {name}", flush=True)
205	
206	            for chunk in chunks:
207	                h = hashlib.md5(chunk["text"].encode()).hexdigest()[:8]
208	                if h in seen_hashes:
209	                    skipped += 1
210	                    continue
211	                seen_hashes.add(h)
212	                out.write(json.dumps(chunk, ensure_ascii=False) + "\n")
213	                total_chunks += 1
214	                total_tokens += chunk["tokens_approx"]
215	
216	    # breakdown
217	    by_type: dict[str, int] = {}
218	    with open(CHUNKS_FILE, encoding="utf-8") as f:
219	        for line in f:
220	            st = json.loads(line).get("source_type", "?")
221	            by_type[st] = by_type.get(st, 0) + 1
222	
223	    mx = max(by_type.values()) if by_type else 1
224	    print(f"\n{'═'*55}")
225	    print(f"  Chunks written : {total_chunks:,}  →  {CHUNKS_FILE}")
226	    print(f"  Approx tokens  : {total_tokens:,}  (~{total_tokens//1000}k)")
227	    print(f"  Duplicates     : {skipped:,}")
228	    print(f"\n  By source type:")
229	    for st, c in sorted(by_type.items(), key=lambda x: -x[1]):
230	        print(f"    {st:<18} {c:>6}  {'█' * (c // max(1, mx // 30))}")
231	    print(f"{'═'*55}")
232	    print(f"\n  ✓ Ready →  python 06_embed.py")
233	
234	
235	if __name__ == "__main__":
236	    main()
237

query:
Found the root cause. `path.read_text()` loads the whole file, then `clean_text` makes a second copy, then all chunks are materialized into a list simultaneously. For large SEC filings this can be 200MB+ per file.

The fix: convert `process_file` to a generator that streams chunks one at a time, and explicitly free the raw text before yielding:

query:
Found the root cause. `path.read_text()` loads the whole file, then `clean_text` makes a second copy, then all chunks are materialized into a list simultaneously. For large SEC filings this can be 200MB+ per file.

The fix: convert `process_file` to a generator that streams chunks one at a time, and explicitly free the raw text before yielding:

4	Uses multiprocessing to parallelize across files.

12	CHUNK_SIZE  = 1_600
13	OVERLAP     = 200
14	MIN_CHUNK   = 120
15	WORKERS     = min(4, max(1, cpu_count() - 3))

23	# Pre-compiled regexes
24	_RE_NULL   = re.compile(r'\x00')
25	_RE_SPACES = re.compile(r'[ \t]{3,}')
26	_RE_LINES  = re.compile(r'\n{4,}')
27	_RE_HYPHEN = re.compile(r'([a-z])-\n([a-z])')

query:
Found the root cause. `path.read_text()` loads the whole file, then `clean_text` makes a second copy, then all chunks are materialized into a list simultaneously. For large SEC filings this can be 200MB+ per file.

The fix: convert `process_file` to a generator that streams chunks one at a time, and explicitly free the raw text before yielding:

query:
Found the root cause. `path.read_text()` loads the whole file, then `clean_text` makes a second copy, then all chunks are materialized into a list simultaneously. For large SEC filings this can be 200MB+ per file.

The fix: convert `process_file` to a generator that streams chunks one at a time, and explicitly free the raw text before yielding:

1	"""
2	Step 2 — Chunking & metadata tagging.
3	Converts all raw documents into data/chunks/chunks.jsonl
4	Uses multiprocessing to parallelize across files.
5	"""
6	
7	import json, pathlib, re, hashlib, sys
8	from multiprocessing import Pool, cpu_count
9	from typing import Iterator
10	
11	# ── config ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
12	CHUNK_SIZE  = 1_600
13	OVERLAP     = 200
14	MIN_CHUNK   = 120
15	WORKERS     = min(4, max(1, cpu_count() - 3))
16	
17	RAW_ROOT    = pathlib.Path(__file__).parent / "data/raw"
18	TEXT_ROOT   = pathlib.Path(__file__).parent / "data/text"
19	OUT_DIR     = pathlib.Path(__file__).parent / "data/chunks"
20	OUT_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
21	CHUNKS_FILE = OUT_DIR / "chunks.jsonl"
22	
23	# Pre-compiled regexes
24	_RE_NULL   = re.compile(r'\x00')
25	_RE_SPACES = re.compile(r'[ \t]{3,}')
26	_RE_LINES  = re.compile(r'\n{4,}')
27	_RE_HYPHEN = re.compile(r'([a-z])-\n([a-z])')
28	
29	
30	# ── helpers ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
31	
32	def tokens_approx(text: str) -> int:
33	    return max(1, len(text) // 4)
34	
35	def make_id(source_type: str, doc_name: str, page, chunk_idx: int) -> str:
36	    return f"{source_type}__{doc_name}__p{page}__c{chunk_idx}"
37	
38	def clean_text(text: str) -> str:
39	    text = _RE_NULL.sub('', text)
40	    text = _RE_SPACES.sub('  ', text)
41	    text = _RE_LINES.sub('\n\n\n', text)
42	    text = _RE_HYPHEN.sub(r'\1\2', text)
43	    return text.strip()
44	
45	def sliding_window(text: str) -> Iterator[str]:
46	    text = text.strip()
47	    if not text:
48	        return
49	    if len(text) <= CHUNK_SIZE:
50	        if len(text) >= MIN_CHUNK:
51	            yield text
52	        return
53	    start = 0
54	    while start < len(text):
55	        end   = start + CHUNK_SIZE
56	        chunk = text[start:end]
57	        if end < len(text):
58	            bp = max(chunk.rfind(". ", len(chunk) - 200),
59	                     chunk.rfind("\n",  len(chunk) - 200))
60	            if bp > len(chunk) // 2:
61	                chunk = chunk[:bp + 1]
62	        chunk = chunk.strip()
63	        if len(chunk) >= MIN_CHUNK:
64	            yield chunk
65	        start += len(chunk) - OVERLAP
66	
67	
68	# ── metadata builders ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
69	
70	def usgs_meta(p: pathlib.Path) -> dict:
71	    m    = re.search(r'(\d{4})', p.stem)
72	    year = int(m.group(1)) if m else None
73	    el   = next((e for e in ["lithium","cobalt","rare-earths","nickel",
74	                              "graphite","manganese","tungsten","tin"]
75	                 if e.replace("-","_") in p.stem or e in p.stem), None)
76	    label = f"USGS Mineral Commodity Summary {year}" + (f" — {el.title()}" if el else "")
77	    return {"source": label, "source_type": "usgs", "year": year, "element": el,
78	            "url": f"https://pubs.usgs.gov/periodicals/mcs{year}/mcs{year}.pdf",
79	            "doc_path": str(p.relative_to(RAW_ROOT))}
80	
81	def usgs_text_meta(p: pathlib.Path) -> dict:
82	    return usgs_meta(pathlib.Path(str(p).replace("data/text/", "data/raw/").replace(".txt", ".pdf")))
83	
84	def sec_meta(p: pathlib.Path) -> dict:
85	    company = p.parent.name
86	    return {"source": f"SEC Form SD — Conflict Minerals Disclosure ({company.upper()})",
87	            "source_type": "sec_edgar", "year": None, "company": company,
88	            "url": "https://www.sec.gov/cgi-bin/browse-edgar",
89	            "doc_path": str(p.relative_to(RAW_ROOT))}
90	
91	def comtrade_meta(p: pathlib.Path) -> dict:
92	    parts = p.stem.split("_")
93	    year  = int(parts[-1]) if parts[-1].isdigit() else None
94	    flow  = "Exports" if "X" in parts else "Imports"
95	    return {"source": f"UN Comtrade — HS {parts[0]} {flow} {year}",
96	            "source_type": "comtrade", "year": year, "hs_code": parts[0], "flow": flow,
97	            "url": "https://comtradeplus.un.org/",
98	            "doc_path": str(p.relative_to(RAW_ROOT))}
99	
100	def misc_meta(p: pathlib.Path) -> dict:
101	    raw_str = str(p).replace("/data/text/misc/", "/data/raw/misc/").replace(".txt", ".pdf")
102	    try:
103	        doc_path = str(pathlib.Path(raw_str).relative_to(RAW_ROOT))
104	    except ValueError:
105	        doc_path = p.name
106	    return {"source": f"External Report — {p.stem.replace('_',' ').title()}",
107	            "source_type": "misc", "year": None, "url": "", "doc_path": doc_path}
108	
109	
110	# ── worker (runs in subprocess) ───────────────────────────────────────────────
111	
112	def process_file(args: tuple) -> list[dict]:
113	    path_str, meta_fn_name, is_pdf = args

116	    meta     = meta_fn(path)
117	
118	    if is_pdf:
119	        # should not happen — all PDFs pre-extracted, but keep as safety net
120	        try:
121	            import pdfplumber
122	            chunks = []

query:
Found the root cause. `path.read_text()` loads the whole file, then `clean_text` makes a second copy, then all chunks are materialized into a list simultaneously. For large SEC filings this can be 200MB+ per file.

The fix: convert `process_file` to a generator that streams chunks one at a time, and explicitly free the raw text before yielding:

1	"""
2	Step 2 — Chunking & metadata tagging.
3	Converts all raw documents into data/chunks/chunks.jsonl
4	Uses multiprocessing to parallelize across files.
5	"""
6	
7	import json, pathlib, re, hashlib, sys
8	from multiprocessing import Pool, cpu_count
9	from typing import Iterator
10	
11	# ── config ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
12	CHUNK_SIZE  = 1_600
13	OVERLAP     = 200
14	MIN_CHUNK   = 120
15	WORKERS     = min(4, max(1, cpu_count() - 3))
16	
17	RAW_ROOT    = pathlib.Path(__file__).parent / "data/raw"
18	TEXT_ROOT   = pathlib.Path(__file__).parent / "data/text"
19	OUT_DIR     = pathlib.Path(__file__).parent / "data/chunks"
20	OUT_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
21	CHUNKS_FILE = OUT_DIR / "chunks.jsonl"
22	
23	# Pre-compiled regexes
24	_RE_NULL   = re.compile(r'\x00')
25	_RE_SPACES = re.compile(r'[ \t]{3,}')
26	_RE_LINES  = re.compile(r'\n{4,}')
27	_RE_HYPHEN = re.compile(r'([a-z])-\n([a-z])')
28	
29	
30	# ── helpers ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
31	
32	def tokens_approx(text: str) -> int:
33	    return max(1, len(text) // 4)
34	
35	def make_id(source_type: str, doc_name: str, page, chunk_idx: int) -> str:
36	    return f"{source_type}__{doc_name}__p{page}__c{chunk_idx}"
37	
38	def clean_text(text: str) -> str:
39	    text = _RE_NULL.sub('', text)
40	    text = _RE_SPACES.sub('  ', text)
41	    text = _RE_LINES.sub('\n\n\n', text)
42	    text = _RE_HYPHEN.sub(r'\1\2', text)
43	    return text.strip()
44	
45	def sliding_window(text: str) -> Iterator[str]:
46	    text = text.strip()
47	    if not text:
48	        return
49	    if len(text) <= CHUNK_SIZE:
50	        if len(text) >= MIN_CHUNK:
51	            yield text
52	        return
53	    start = 0
54	    while start < len(text):
55	        end   = start + CHUNK_SIZE
56	        chunk = text[start:end]
57	        if end < len(text):
58	            bp = max(chunk.rfind(". ", len(chunk) - 200),
59	                     chunk.rfind("\n",  len(chunk) - 200))
60	            if bp > len(chunk) // 2:
61	                chunk = chunk[:bp + 1]
62	        chunk = chunk.strip()
63	        if len(chunk) >= MIN_CHUNK:
64	            yield chunk
65	        start += len(chunk) - OVERLAP
66	
67	
68	# ── metadata builders ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
69	
70	def usgs_meta(p: pathlib.Path) -> dict:
71	    m    = re.search(r'(\d{4})', p.stem)
72	    year = int(m.group(1)) if m else None
73	    el   = next((e for e in ["lithium","cobalt","rare-earths","nickel",
74	                              "graphite","manganese","tungsten","tin"]
75	                 if e.replace("-","_") in p.stem or e in p.stem), None)
76	    label = f"USGS Mineral Commodity Summary {year}" + (f" — {el.title()}" if el else "")
77	    return {"source": label, "source_type": "usgs", "year": year, "element": el,
78	            "url": f"https://pubs.usgs.gov/periodicals/mcs{year}/mcs{year}.pdf",
79	            "doc_path": str(p.relative_to(RAW_ROOT))}
80	
81	def usgs_text_meta(p: pathlib.Path) -> dict:
82	    return usgs_meta(pathlib.Path(str(p).replace("data/text/", "data/raw/").replace(".txt", ".pdf")))
83	
84	def sec_meta(p: pathlib.Path) -> dict:
85	    company = p.parent.name
86	    return {"source": f"SEC Form SD — Conflict Minerals Disclosure ({company.upper()})",
87	            "source_type": "sec_edgar", "year": None, "company": company,
88	            "url": "https://www.sec.gov/cgi-bin/browse-edgar",
89	            "doc_path": str(p.relative_to(RAW_ROOT))}
90	
91	def comtrade_meta(p: pathlib.Path) -> dict:
92	    parts = p.stem.split("_")
93	    year  = int(parts[-1]) if parts[-1].isdigit() else None
94	    flow  = "Exports" if "X" in parts else "Imports"
95	    return {"source": f"UN Comtrade — HS {parts[0]} {flow} {year}",
96	            "source_type": "comtrade", "year": year, "hs_code": parts[0], "flow": flow,
97	            "url": "https://comtradeplus.un.org/",
98	            "doc_path": str(p.relative_to(RAW_ROOT))}
99	
100	def misc_meta(p: pathlib.Path) -> dict:
101	    raw_str = str(p).replace("/data/text/misc/", "/data/raw/misc/").replace(".txt", ".pdf")
102	    try:
103	        doc_path = str(pathlib.Path(raw_str).relative_to(RAW_ROOT))
104	    except ValueError:
105	        doc_path = p.name
106	    return {"source": f"External Report — {p.stem.replace('_',' ').title()}",
107	            "source_type": "misc", "year": None, "url": "", "doc_path": doc_path}
108	
109	
110	# ── worker (runs in subprocess) ───────────────────────────────────────────────
111	
112	def process_file(args: tuple) -> list[dict]:
113	    path_str, meta_fn_name, is_pdf = args
114	    path     = pathlib.Path(path_str)
115	    meta_fn  = globals()[meta_fn_name]
116	    meta     = meta_fn(path)
117	
118	    if is_pdf:
119	        # should not happen — all PDFs pre-extracted, but keep as safety net
120	        try:
121	            import pdfplumber
122	            chunks = []
123	            with pdfplumber.open(path) as pdf:
124	                for pg_num, pg in enumerate(pdf.pages, 1):
125	                    raw = pg.extract_text()
126	                    if not raw:
127	                        continue
128	                    text = clean_text(raw)
129	                    for idx, chunk in enumerate(sliding_window(text)):
130	                        chunks.append({**meta,
131	                            "id": make_id(meta["source_type"], path.stem, pg_num, idx),
132	                            "text": chunk, "page": pg_num, "chunk_index": idx,
133	                            "tokens_approx": tokens_approx(chunk)})
134	            return chunks
135	        except Exception as e:
136	            print(f"  ✗ PDF {path.name}: {e}", flush=True)
137	            return []
138	
139	    try:
140	        raw  = path.read_text(encoding="utf-8", errors="ignore")
141	        text = clean_text(raw)
142	        return [{**meta,
143	                 "id": make_id(meta["source_type"], path.stem, 0, idx),
144	                 "text": chunk, "page": None, "chunk_index": idx,
145	                 "tokens_approx": tokens_approx(chunk)}
146	                for idx, chunk in enumerate(sliding_window(text))]
147	    except Exception as e:
148	        print(f"  ✗ {path.name}: {e}", flush=True)
149	        return []
150	
151	
152	# ── file collection ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
153	
154	_misc_pre = {p.stem for p in (TEXT_ROOT/"misc").glob("*.txt")} \
155	            if (TEXT_ROOT/"misc").exists() else set()
156	
157	SOURCES = [
158	    (TEXT_ROOT/"usgs",        "*.txt",    False, "usgs_text_meta"),
159	    (TEXT_ROOT/"misc",        "*.txt",    False, "misc_meta"),
160	    (RAW_ROOT/"sec_edgar",    "**/*.txt", False, "sec_meta"),
161	    (RAW_ROOT/"comtrade_text","*.txt",    False, "comtrade_meta"),
162	    (RAW_ROOT/"misc",         "**/*.txt", False, "misc_meta"),
163	]
164	
165	def collect_files() -> list[tuple]:
166	    files = []
167	    seen  = set()
168	    for folder, glob, is_pdf, fn_name in SOURCES:
169	        if not folder.exists():
170	            continue
171	        for p in sorted(folder.glob(glob)):
172	            if not p.is_file() or p.name == "_manifest.json":
173	                continue
174	            key = p.stem
175	            if key in seen:          # skip raw/misc txt if already in text/misc
176	                continue
177	            seen.add(key)
178	            files.append((str(p), fn_name, is_pdf))
179	    # any misc PDFs not yet pre-extracted
180	    misc_pdf = RAW_ROOT / "misc"
181	    if misc_pdf.exists():
182	        for p in sorted(misc_pdf.glob("**/*.pdf")):
183	            if p.stem not in _misc_pre:
184	                files.append((str(p), "misc_meta", True))
185	    return files
186	
187	
188	# ── main ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
189	
190	def main():
191	    files = collect_files()
192	    n     = len(files)
193	    print(f"\n✓ {n} source files  •  {WORKERS} workers\n")
194	
195	    seen_hashes : set[str] = set()
196	    total_chunks = total_tokens = skipped = 0
197	
198	    with open(CHUNKS_FILE, "w", encoding="utf-8") as out, \
199	         Pool(processes=WORKERS) as pool:
200	
201	        for i, chunks in enumerate(pool.imap_unordered(process_file, files, chunksize=1), 1):
202	            name = pathlib.Path(files[i-1][0]).name if i <= n else "?"
203	            pct  = int(i / n * 100)
204	            print(f"  [{i:>3}/{n}] {pct:>3}%  +{len(chunks):>4} chunks  {name}", flush=True)
205	
206	            for chunk in chunks:
207	                h = hashlib.md5(chunk["text"].encode()).hexdigest()[:8]
208	                if h in seen_hashes:
209	                    skipped += 1
210	                    continue
211	                seen_hashes.add(h)
212	                out.write(json.dumps(chunk, ensure_ascii=False) + "\n")
213	                total_chunks += 1
214	                total_tokens += chunk["tokens_approx"]
215	
216	    # breakdown
217	    by_type: dict[str, int] = {}
218	    with open(CHUNKS_FILE, encoding="utf-8") as f:
219	        for line in f:
220	            st = json.loads(line).get("source_type", "?")
221	            by_type[st] = by_type.get(st, 0) + 1
222	
223	    mx = max(by_type.values()) if by_type else 1
224	    print(f"\n{'═'*55}")
225	    print(f"  Chunks written : {total_chunks:,}  →  {CHUNKS_FILE}")
226	    print(f"  Approx tokens  : {total_tokens:,}  (~{total_tokens//1000}k)")
227	    print(f"  Duplicates     : {skipped:,}")
228	    print(f"\n  By source type:")
229	    for st, c in sorted(by_type.items(), key=lambda x: -x[1]):
230	        print(f"    {st:<18} {c:>6}  {'█' * (c // max(1, mx // 30))}")
231	    print(f"{'═'*55}")
232	    print(f"\n  ✓ Ready →  python 06_embed.py")
233	
234	
235	if __name__ == "__main__":
236	    main()
237

1	"""
2	Step 2 — Chunking & metadata tagging.
3	Converts all raw documents into data/chunks/chunks.jsonl
4	Uses multiprocessing to parallelize across files.
5	"""
6	
7	import json, pathlib, re, hashlib, sys
8	from multiprocessing import Pool, cpu_count
9	from typing import Iterator
10	
11	# ── config ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
12	CHUNK_SIZE  = 1_600
13	OVERLAP     = 200
14	MIN_CHUNK   = 120
15	WORKERS     = min(4, max(1, cpu_count() - 3))
16	
17	RAW_ROOT    = pathlib.Path(__file__).parent / "data/raw"
18	TEXT_ROOT   = pathlib.Path(__file__).parent / "data/text"
19	OUT_DIR     = pathlib.Path(__file__).parent / "data/chunks"
20	OUT_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
21	CHUNKS_FILE = OUT_DIR / "chunks.jsonl"
22	
23	# Pre-compiled regexes
24	_RE_NULL   = re.compile(r'\x00')
25	_RE_SPACES = re.compile(r'[ \t]{3,}')
26	_RE_LINES  = re.compile(r'\n{4,}')
27	_RE_HYPHEN = re.compile(r'([a-z])-\n([a-z])')
28	
29	
30	# ── helpers ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
31	
32	def tokens_approx(text: str) -> int:
33	    return max(1, len(text) // 4)
34	
35	def make_id(source_type: str, doc_name: str, page, chunk_idx: int) -> str:
36	    return f"{source_type}__{doc_name}__p{page}__c{chunk_idx}"
37	
38	def clean_text(text: str) -> str:
39	    text = _RE_NULL.sub('', text)
40	    text = _RE_SPACES.sub('  ', text)
41	    text = _RE_LINES.sub('\n\n\n', text)
42	    text = _RE_HYPHEN.sub(r'\1\2', text)
43	    return text.strip()
44	
45	def sliding_window(text: str) -> Iterator[str]:
46	    text = text.strip()
47	    if not text:
48	        return
49	    if len(text) <= CHUNK_SIZE:
50	        if len(text) >= MIN_CHUNK:
51	            yield text
52	        return
53	    start = 0
54	    while start < len(text):
55	        end   = start + CHUNK_SIZE
56	        chunk = text[start:end]
57	        if end < len(text):
58	            bp = max(chunk.rfind(". ", len(chunk) - 200),
59	                     chunk.rfind("\n",  len(chunk) - 200))
60	            if bp > len(chunk) // 2:
61	                chunk = chunk[:bp + 1]
62	        chunk = chunk.strip()
63	        if len(chunk) >= MIN_CHUNK:
64	            yield chunk
65	        start += len(chunk) - OVERLAP
66	
67	
68	# ── metadata builders ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
69	
70	def usgs_meta(p: pathlib.Path) -> dict:
71	    m    = re.search(r'(\d{4})', p.stem)
72	    year = int(m.group(1)) if m else None
73	    el   = next((e for e in ["lithium","cobalt","rare-earths","nickel",
74	                              "graphite","manganese","tungsten","tin"]
75	                 if e.replace("-","_") in p.stem or e in p.stem), None)
76	    label = f"USGS Mineral Commodity Summary {year}" + (f" — {el.title()}" if el else "")
77	    return {"source": label, "source_type": "usgs", "year": year, "element": el,
78	            "url": f"https://pubs.usgs.gov/periodicals/mcs{year}/mcs{year}.pdf",
79	            "doc_path": str(p.relative_to(RAW_ROOT))}
80	
81	def usgs_text_meta(p: pathlib.Path) -> dict:
82	    return usgs_meta(pathlib.Path(str(p).replace("data/text/", "data/raw/").replace(".txt", ".pdf")))
83	
84	def sec_meta(p: pathlib.Path) -> dict:
85	    company = p.parent.name
86	    return {"source": f"SEC Form SD — Conflict Minerals Disclosure ({company.upper()})",
87	            "source_type": "sec_edgar", "year": None, "company": company,
88	            "url": "https://www.sec.gov/cgi-bin/browse-edgar",
89	            "doc_path": str(p.relative_to(RAW_ROOT))}
90	
91	def comtrade_meta(p: pathlib.Path) -> dict:
92	    parts = p.stem.split("_")
93	    year  = int(parts[-1]) if parts[-1].isdigit() else None
94	    flow  = "Exports" if "X" in parts else "Imports"
95	    return {"source": f"UN Comtrade — HS {parts[0]} {flow} {year}",
96	            "source_type": "comtrade", "year": year, "hs_code": parts[0], "flow": flow,
97	            "url": "https://comtradeplus.un.org/",
98	            "doc_path": str(p.relative_to(RAW_ROOT))}
99	
100	def misc_meta(p: pathlib.Path) -> dict:
101	    raw_str = str(p).replace("/data/text/misc/", "/data/raw/misc/").replace(".txt", ".pdf")
102	    try:
103	        doc_path = str(pathlib.Path(raw_str).relative_to(RAW_ROOT))
104	    except ValueError:
105	        doc_path = p.name
106	    return {"source": f"External Report — {p.stem.replace('_',' ').title()}",
107	            "source_type": "misc", "year": None, "url": "", "doc_path": doc_path}
108	
109	
110	# ── worker (runs in subprocess) ───────────────────────────────────────────────
111	
112	def process_file(args: tuple) -> list[dict]:
113	    path_str, meta_fn_name, is_pdf = args
114	    path     = pathlib.Path(path_str)
115	    meta_fn  = globals()[meta_fn_name]
116	    meta     = meta_fn(path)
117	
118	    if is_pdf:
119	        # should not happen — all PDFs pre-extracted, but keep as safety net
120	        try:
121	            import pdfplumber
122	            chunks = []
123	            with pdfplumber.open(path) as pdf:
124	                for pg_num, pg in enumerate(pdf.pages, 1):
125	                    raw = pg.extract_text()
126	                    if not raw:
127	                        continue
128	                    text = clean_text(raw)
129	                    for idx, chunk in enumerate(sliding_window(text)):
130	                        chunks.append({**meta,
131	                            "id": make_id(meta["source_type"], path.stem, pg_num, idx),
132	                            "text": chunk, "page": pg_num, "chunk_index": idx,
133	                            "tokens_approx": tokens_approx(chunk)})
134	            return chunks
135	        except Exception as e:
136	            print(f"  ✗ PDF {path.name}: {e}", flush=True)
137	            return []
138	
139	    try:
140	        raw  = path.read_text(encoding="utf-8", errors="ignore")
141	        text = clean_text(raw)
142	        return [{**meta,
143	                 "id": make_id(meta["source_type"], path.stem, 0, idx),
144	                 "text": chunk, "page": None, "chunk_index": idx,
145	                 "tokens_approx": tokens_approx(chunk)}
146	                for idx, chunk in enumerate(sliding_window(text))]
147	    except Exception as e:
148	        print(f"  ✗ {path.name}: {e}", flush=True)
149	        return []
150	
151	
152	# ── file collection ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
153	
154	_misc_pre = {p.stem for p in (TEXT_ROOT/"misc").glob("*.txt")} \
155	            if (TEXT_ROOT/"misc").exists() else set()
156	
157	SOURCES = [
158	    (TEXT_ROOT/"usgs",        "*.txt",    False, "usgs_text_meta"),
159	    (TEXT_ROOT/"misc",        "*.txt",    False, "misc_meta"),
160	    (RAW_ROOT/"sec_edgar",    "**/*.txt", False, "sec_meta"),
161	    (RAW_ROOT/"comtrade_text","*.txt",    False, "comtrade_meta"),
162	    (RAW_ROOT/"misc",         "**/*.txt", False, "misc_meta"),
163	]
164	
165	def collect_files() -> list[tuple]:
166	    files = []
167	    seen  = set()
168	    for folder, glob, is_pdf, fn_name in SOURCES:
169	        if not folder.exists():
170	            continue
171	        for p in sorted(folder.glob(glob)):
172	            if not p.is_file() or p.name == "_manifest.json":
173	                continue
174	            key = p.stem
175	            if key in seen:          # skip raw/misc txt if already in text/misc
176	                continue
177	            seen.add(key)
178	            files.append((str(p), fn_name, is_pdf))
179	    # any misc PDFs not yet pre-extracted
180	    misc_pdf = RAW_ROOT / "misc"
181	    if misc_pdf.exists():
182	        for p in sorted(misc_pdf.glob("**/*.pdf")):
183	            if p.stem not in _misc_pre:
184	                files.append((str(p), "misc_meta", True))
185	    return files
186	
187	
188	# ── main ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
189	
190	def main():
191	    files = collect_files()
192	    n     = len(files)
193	    print(f"\n✓ {n} source files  •  {WORKERS} workers\n")
194	
195	    seen_hashes : set[str] = set()
196	    total_chunks = total_tokens = skipped = 0
197	
198	    with open(CHUNKS_FILE, "w", encoding="utf-8") as out, \
199	         Pool(processes=WORKERS) as pool:
200	
201	        for i, chunks in enumerate(pool.imap_unordered(process_file, files, chunksize=1), 1):
202	            name = pathlib.Path(files[i-1][0]).name if i <= n else "?"
203	            pct  = int(i / n * 100)
204	            print(f"  [{i:>3}/{n}] {pct:>3}%  +{len(chunks):>4} chunks  {name}", flush=True)
205	
206	            for chunk in chunks:
207	                h = hashlib.md5(chunk["text"].encode()).hexdigest()[:8]
208	                if h in seen_hashes:
209	                    skipped += 1
210	                    continue
211	                seen_hashes.add(h)
212	                out.write(json.dumps(chunk, ensure_ascii=False) + "\n")
213	                total_chunks += 1
214	                total_tokens += chunk["tokens_approx"]
215	
216	    # breakdown
217	    by_type: dict[str, int] = {}
218	    with open(CHUNKS_FILE, encoding="utf-8") as f:
219	        for line in f:
220	            st = json.loads(line).get("source_type", "?")
221	            by_type[st] = by_type.get(st, 0) + 1
222	
223	    mx = max(by_type.values()) if by_type else 1
224	    print(f"\n{'═'*55}")
225	    print(f"  Chunks written : {total_chunks:,}  →  {CHUNKS_FILE}")
226	    print(f"  Approx tokens  : {total_tokens:,}  (~{total_tokens//1000}k)")
227	    print(f"  Duplicates     : {skipped:,}")
228	    print(f"\n  By source type:")
229	    for st, c in sorted(by_type.items(), key=lambda x: -x[1]):
230	        print(f"    {st:<18} {c:>6}  {'█' * (c // max(1, mx // 30))}")
231	    print(f"{'═'*55}")
232	    print(f"\n  ✓ Ready →  python 06_embed.py")
233	
234	
235	if __name__ == "__main__":
236	    main()
237

107	            "source_type": "misc", "year": None, "url": "", "doc_path": doc_path}
108	
109	
110	# ── worker (runs in subprocess) ───────────────────────────────────────────────
111	
112	def process_file(args: tuple) -> list[dict]:
113	    path_str, meta_fn_name, is_pdf = args
114	    path     = pathlib.Path(path_str)
115	    meta_fn  = globals()[meta_fn_name]
116	    meta     = meta_fn(path)
117	
118	    if is_pdf:
119	        # should not happen — all PDFs pre-extracted, but keep as safety net
120	        try:
121	            import pdfplumber
122	            chunks = []
123	            with pdfplumber.open(path) as pdf:
124	                for pg_num, pg in enumerate(pdf.pages, 1):
125	                    raw = pg.extract_text()
126	                    if not raw:
127	                        continue
128	                    text = clean_text(raw)
129	                    for idx, chunk in enumerate(sliding_window(text)):
130	                        chunks.append({**meta,
131	                            "id": make_id(meta["source_type"], path.stem, pg_num, idx),
132	                            "text": chunk, "page": pg_num, "chunk_index": idx,
133	                            "tokens_approx": tokens_approx(chunk)})
134	            return chunks
135	        except Exception as e:
136	            print(f"  ✗ PDF {path.name}: {e}", flush=True)
137	            return []
138	
139	    try:
140	        raw  = path.read_text(encoding="utf-8", errors="ignore")
141	        text = clean_text(raw)
142	        return [{**meta,
143	                 "id": make_id(meta["source_type"], path.stem, 0, idx),
144	                 "text": chunk, "page": None, "chunk_index": idx,
145	                 "tokens_approx": tokens_approx(chunk)}
146	                for idx, chunk in enumerate(sliding_window(text))]
147	    except Exception as e:
148	        print(f"  ✗ {path.name}: {e}", flush=True)
149	        return []
150	
151	
152	# ── file collection ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
153	
154	_misc_pre = {p.stem for p in (TEXT_ROOT/"misc").glob("*.txt")} \
155	            if (TEXT_ROOT/"misc").exists() else set()
156	
157	SOURCES = [
158	    (TEXT_ROOT/"usgs",        "*.txt",    False, "usgs_text_meta"),
159	    (TEXT_ROOT/"misc",        "*.txt",    False, "misc_meta"),
160	    (RAW_ROOT/"sec_edgar",    "**/*.txt", False, "sec_meta"),
161	    (RAW_ROOT/"comtrade_text","*.txt",    False, "comtrade_meta"),
162	    (RAW_ROOT/"misc",         "**/*.txt", False, "misc_meta"),
163	]
164	
165	def collect_files() -> list[tuple]:
166	    files = []
167	    seen  = set()
168	    for folder, glob, is_pdf, fn_name in SOURCES:
169	        if not folder.exists():
170	            continue
171	        for p in sorted(folder.glob(glob)):
172	            if not p.is_file() or p.name == "_manifest.json":
173	                continue
174	            key = p.stem
175	            if key in seen:          # skip raw/misc txt if already in text/misc
176	                continue
177	            seen.add(key)
178	            files.append((str(p), fn_name, is_pdf))
179	    # any misc PDFs not yet pre-extracted
180	    misc_pdf = RAW_ROOT / "misc"
181	    if misc_pdf.exists():
182	        for p in sorted(misc_pdf.glob("**/*.pdf")):
183	            if p.stem not in _misc_pre:
184	                files.append((str(p), "misc_meta", True))
185	    return files
186	
187	
188	# ── main ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
189	
190	def main():
191	    files = collect_files()
192	    n     = len(files)
193	    print(f"\n✓ {n} source files  •  {WORKERS} workers\n")
194	
195	    seen_hashes : set[str] = set()
196	    total_chunks = total_tokens = skipped = 0
197	
198	    with open(CHUNKS_FILE, "w", encoding="utf-8") as out, \
199	         Pool(processes=WORKERS) as pool:
200	
201	        for i, chunks in enumerate(pool.imap_unordered(process_file, files, chunksize=1), 1):
202	            name = pathlib.Path(files[i-1][0]).name if i <= n else "?"
203	            pct  = int(i / n * 100)
204	            print(f"  [{i:>3}/{n}] {pct:>3}%  +{len(chunks):>4} chunks  {name}", flush=True)
205	
206	            for chunk in chunks:
207	                h = hashlib.md5(chunk["text"].encode()).hexdigest()[:8]
208	                if h in seen_hashes:
209	                    skipped += 1
210	                    continue
211	                seen_hashes.add(h)
212	                out.write(json.dumps(chunk, ensure_ascii=False) + "\n")
213	                total_chunks += 1
214	                total_tokens += chunk["tokens_approx"]
215	
216	    # breakdown
217	    by_type: dict[str, int] = {}
218	    with open(CHUNKS_FILE, encoding="utf-8") as f:
219	        for line in f:
220	            st = json.loads(line).get("source_type", "?")
221	            by_type[st] = by_type.get(st, 0) + 1
222	
223	    mx = max(by_type.values()) if by_type else 1
224	    print(f"\n{'═'*55}")
225	    print(f"  Chunks written : {total_chunks:,}  →  {CHUNKS_FILE}")
226	    print(f"  Approx tokens  : {total_tokens:,}  (~{total_tokens//1000}k)")
227	    print(f"  Duplicates     : {skipped:,}")
228	    print(f"\n  By source type:")
229	    for st, c in sorted(by_type.items(), key=lambda x: -x[1]):
230	        print(f"    {st:<18} {c:>6}  {'█' * (c // max(1, mx // 30))}")
231	    print(f"{'═'*55}")
232	    print(f"\n  ✓ Ready →  python 06_embed.py")
233	
234	
235	if __name__ == "__main__":
236	    main()
