query: Now let me check the webapp structure and look for API/RAG code:
original_content:
1	/**
2	 * REEtrieve Intelligence — chat API server
3	 * POST /api/chat  →  SSE stream of Ollama responses grounded in RAG corpus
4	 *
5	 * Run alongside Vite:  npm run dev:all
6	 * Requires:  Ollama running locally (ollama serve) with a model pulled.
7	 *
8	 * Quick setup:
9	 *   brew install ollama
10	 *   ollama serve          # or open Ollama.app
11	 *   ollama pull llama3.2  # ~2 GB — good quality/speed balance
12	 *
13	 * Override model or URL via .env:
14	 *   OLLAMA_MODEL=mistral
15	 *   OLLAMA_URL=http://localhost:11434
16	 */
17	
18	import express from 'express'
19	import { readFileSync, existsSync } from 'fs'
20	import path from 'path'
21	import { fileURLToPath } from 'url'
22	import 'dotenv/config'
23	
24	const __dirname = path.dirname(fileURLToPath(import.meta.url))
25	const app = express()
26	app.use(express.json({ limit: '1mb' }))
27	
28	const PORT         = process.env.PORT        ?? 3001
29	const OLLAMA_URL   = process.env.OLLAMA_URL  ?? 'http://localhost:11434'
30	const OLLAMA_MODEL = process.env.OLLAMA_MODEL ?? 'llama3.2'
31	
32	// ── Load RAG corpus once at startup ──────────────────────────────────────────
33	const CHUNKS_PATH = process.env.CHUNKS_PATH
34	  ?? path.resolve(__dirname, '../../rag_prep/data/chunks/chunks.jsonl')
35	
36	let CHUNKS = []
37	if (existsSync(CHUNKS_PATH)) {
38	  const lines = readFileSync(CHUNKS_PATH, 'utf8').trim().split('\n').filter(Boolean)
39	  CHUNKS = lines.map(l => { try { return JSON.parse(l) } catch { return null } }).filter(Boolean)
40	  console.log(`✓ Loaded ${CHUNKS.length} RAG chunks from ${path.basename(path.dirname(CHUNKS_PATH))}/chunks.jsonl`)
41	} else {
42	  console.warn(`⚠ chunks.jsonl not found at:\n  ${CHUNKS_PATH}\n  Running in entity-data-only mode (RAG disabled).\n  Run: cd rag_prep && python 05_chunk.py`)
43	}
44	
45	// ── Keyword-based chunk retrieval ─────────────────────────────────────────────
46	const STOPWORDS = new Set([
47	  'the','and','for','that','this','with','are','from','into','what',
48	  'which','have','been','will','when','where','how','does','can','its',
49	  'their','about','used','also','over','more','than','such',
50	])
51	
52	function retrieveChunks(entityName, query, topK = 7) {
53	  if (!CHUNKS.length) return []
54	
55	  // Build token set: entity name tokens + meaningful query tokens
56	  const entityTokens = entityName.toLowerCase().split(/\W+/).filter(t => t.length > 2)
57	  const queryTokens  = query.toLowerCase().split(/\W+/)
58	    .filter(t => t.length > 3 && !STOPWORDS.has(t))
59	  const allTokens    = [...new Set([...entityTokens, ...queryTokens])]
60	
61	  return CHUNKS
62	    .map(chunk => {
63	      const haystack = (chunk.text || chunk.content || '').toLowerCase()
64	      // Entity tokens worth 2 pts each (high precision), query tokens 1 pt
65	      const score =
66	        entityTokens.reduce((s, t) => s + (haystack.includes(t) ? 2 : 0), 0) +
67	        queryTokens.reduce((s, t)  => s + (haystack.includes(t) ? 1 : 0), 0)
68	      return { ...chunk, _score: score }
69	    })
70	    .filter(c => c._score > 0)
71	    .sort((a, b) => b._score - a._score)
72	    .slice(0, topK)
73	}
74	
75	// ── System prompt ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
76	function buildSystemPrompt(entity, chunks) {
77	  const chunkBlock = chunks.length
78	    ? chunks.map((c, i) => [
79	        `[DOC ${i + 1}] ${c.source ?? c.source_type ?? 'Unknown source'} | Year: ${c.year ?? '?'} | Page: ${c.page ?? '?'}`,
80	        c.url ? `URL: ${c.url}` : null,
81	        '',
82	        c.text ?? c.content ?? '',
83	      ].filter(x => x !== null).join('\n'))
84	      .join('\n\n' + '─'.repeat(60) + '\n\n')
85	    : 'No RAG corpus available for this session. Base your analysis on the entity signal data only and be explicit about this limitation.'
86	
87	  const signalLegend = [
88	    'cl1 = Unexplained Production Spike (USGS / EITI)',
89	    'cl2 = Regional Child Labour Prevalence (ILO ILOSTAT)',
90	    'cl3 = Low School Enrolment + Literacy (UNESCO)',
91	    'cl4 = Nighttime Luminosity Anomaly (NASA VIIRS)',
92	    'fl1 = Armed Group / Mine Proximity (IPIS / ACLED)',
93	    'fl2 = ILO Forced Labour Indicator (ILO 2022)',
94	    'fl3 = Debt Bondage / Wage Withholding (Amnesty Intl)',
95	    'fl4 = AIS Dark Shipping Events (MarineTraffic)',
96	    'df1 = Comtrade Mirror-Trade Gap (UN Comtrade)',
97	    'df2 = AIS Dark Events at Transit Port (MarineTraffic)',
98	    'df3 = Certificate Temporal Fraud (RMI RMAP / EITI)',
99	    'df4 = Mine Area vs Concession Mismatch (Sentinel-2)',
100	    'df5 = Invoice Price Below Market Floor (LME / Fastmarkets)',
101	    'mr1 = RMI AMRT Non-Compliance (RMI 2024)',
102	    'mr2 = GISTM Tailings Extreme/Very High (Global Tailings Portal 2025)',
103	    'mr3 = BHRRC Allegation Density (BHRRC TMT 2024)',
104	    'mr4 = Global Witness Investigative Flag (GW 2024)',
105	    'mr5 = BMI Carbon & Waste ESG Below Median (BMI 2024)',
106	  ].join('\n  ')
107	
108	  const preset = entity.signalPreset ?? {}
109	  const signalLines = Object.entries(preset)
110	    .map(([k, v]) => `  ${k}: ${v.toFixed(2)}`)
111	    .join('\n')
112	
113	  return `You are REEtrieve Intelligence, an investigative AI embedded in the REEtrieve Compliance Platform. You specialise in critical mineral supply chains — specifically rare earth elements (REE), cobalt, nickel, tin, and tungsten.
114	
115	Your audience is compliance officers, regulators, journalists, and auditors who need rigorous, cited, probabilistic analysis — not reassurance.
116	
117	${'═'.repeat(65)}
118	ENTITY UNDER REVIEW
119	${'═'.repeat(65)}
120	Name:            ${entity.name}
121	Country:         ${entity.flag ?? ''} ${entity.country}
122	Industry:        ${entity.industry}
123	Risk Score:      ${entity.riskScore}/100  (Bayesian composite, 4 dimensions)
124	Cert Status:     ${entity.certStatus ?? 'unknown'}
125	Last Audit:      ${entity.lastAudit ?? 'never'}
126	Risk Flags:      ${(entity.riskFlags ?? []).join(' | ') || 'none'}
127	Intel Note:      ${entity.note ?? ''}
128	
129	SIGNAL ACTIVATIONS (0 = no signal, 1 = fully active):
130	${signalLines}
131	
132	SIGNAL LEGEND:
133	  ${signalLegend}
134	
135	${'═'.repeat(65)}
136	RELEVANT DOCUMENT EXCERPTS  (ranked by relevance to your query)
137	${'═'.repeat(65)}
138	${chunkBlock}
139	
140	${'═'.repeat(65)}
141	ANALYTICAL RULES — APPLY TO EVERY RESPONSE
142	${'═'.repeat(65)}
143	1. CITATION REQUIRED: Every factual claim must end with its source in parentheses.
144	   Use exact names: (USGS MCS 2024), (UN Comtrade 2023), (ILO ILOSTAT 2022),
145	   (RMI 2024), (BHRRC TMT 2024), (Global Witness 2024), (BMI 2024),
146	   or (REEtrieve signal data) for values derived from signal activations above.
147	
148	2. TWO-SIDED ANALYSIS: After each risk finding, state the strongest
149	   counter-argument or benign alternative interpretation, then explain
150	   why you accept or reject it given the available evidence.
151	
152	3. RELATIVIZE SCORES: Interpret numbers in context.
153	   The 13-entity database range is roughly 38–95. Quartile breakpoints:
154	   ≥82 = top quartile (critical), 68–81 = high, 52–67 = elevated, <52 = lower.
155	   Always say where this entity sits.
156	
157	4. PROBABILISTIC FRAMING: Use "the evidence is consistent with...",
158	   "with high/moderate/low confidence...", "this cannot be confirmed but...".
159	   Never state certainties. Supply chains are inherently uncertain.
160	
161	5. MISSING DATA IS A SIGNAL: If a dataset doesn't cover this entity,
162	   name the gap explicitly and explain what inference is still possible.
163	   E.g., absence from GISTM disclosures after the 2025 mandatory deadline
164	   is itself non-compliance, not a neutral absence.
165	
166	6. TEMPORAL LAG: Materials extracted today appear in products in 2–3 years.
167	   A battery sold in 2025 contains ore from 2022–2023. Always anchor
168	   downstream product claims to the correct extraction window.
169	
170	7. DOWNSTREAM TRACING: Trace material flows step by step using the document
171	   corpus: mine → smelter → refiner → manufacturer → brand. State each
172	   inference link explicitly. Only name companies that appear in cited documents.
173	   If a company cannot be confirmed, say so rather than speculating.
174	
175	8. SCOPE GUARD: You cover REE, cobalt, nickel, tin, tungsten only.
176	   If asked about other minerals, note this is outside your current dataset.
177	
178	9. ADVERSARIAL LENS: Always ask — could this data have been falsified?
179	   Who benefits from the official narrative? Flag where document fraud
180	   signals (df1–df5) suggest the numbers themselves may be unreliable.`
181	}
182	
183	// ── Chat endpoint ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
184	app.post('/api/chat', async (req, res) => {
185	  const { entity, messages } = req.body
186	
187	  if (!entity || !Array.isArray(messages) || !messages.length) {
188	    return res.status(400).json({ error: 'entity and messages[] required' })
189	  }
190	
191	  // Extract last user message for retrieval
192	  const lastUser = [...messages].reverse().find(m => m.role === 'user')?.content ?? ''
193	  const chunks   = retrieveChunks(entity.name, lastUser)
194	
195	  const systemPrompt = buildSystemPrompt(entity, chunks)
196	
197	  // SSE headers
198	  res.setHeader('Content-Type', 'text/event-stream')
199	  res.setHeader('Cache-Control', 'no-cache')
200	  res.setHeader('Connection', 'keep-alive')
201	  res.setHeader('X-Accel-Buffering', 'no')
202	  res.flushHeaders()
203	
204	  try {
205	    // Ollama OpenAI-compatible chat endpoint with streaming
206	    const model = req.body.model ?? OLLAMA_MODEL
207	    const ollamaRes = await fetch(`${OLLAMA_URL}/api/chat`, {
208	      method: 'POST',
209	      headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
210	      body: JSON.stringify({
211	        model,
212	        stream: true,
213	        options: { num_predict: 1500, temperature: 0.3 },
214	        messages: [
215	          { role: 'system', content: systemPrompt },
216	          ...messages.map(m => ({ role: m.role, content: m.content })),
217	        ],
218	      }),
219	    })
220	
221	    if (!ollamaRes.ok) {
222	      const err = await ollamaRes.text()
223	      throw new Error(`Ollama ${ollamaRes.status}: ${err}`)
224	    }
225	
226	    const reader  = ollamaRes.body.getReader()
227	    const decoder = new TextDecoder()
228	    let   buffer  = ''
229	
230	    while (true) {
231	      const { done, value } = await reader.read()
232	      if (done) break
233	      buffer += decoder.decode(value, { stream: true })
234	      const lines = buffer.split('\n')
235	      buffer = lines.pop()
236	      for (const line of lines) {
237	        if (!line.trim()) continue
238	        try {
239	          const json = JSON.parse(line)
240	          const text = json.message?.content
241	          if (text) res.write(`data: ${JSON.stringify({ text })}\n\n`)
242	          if (json.done) {
243	            res.write('data: [DONE]\n\n')
244	            res.end()
245	            return
246	          }
247	        } catch { /* partial JSON — skip */ }
248	      }
249	    }
250	
251	    res.write('data: [DONE]\n\n')
252	    res.end()
253	  } catch (err) {
254	    console.error('Ollama error:', err.message)
255	    if (!res.headersSent) {
256	      res.status(500).json({ error: err.message })
257	    } else {
258	      res.write(`data: ${JSON.stringify({ error: err.message })}\n\n`)
259	      res.end()
260	    }
261	  }
262	})
263

52	function retrieveChunks(entityName, query, topK = 7) {
53	  if (!CHUNKS.length) return []
54	
55	  // Build token set: entity name tokens + meaningful query tokens
56	  const entityTokens = entityName.toLowerCase().split(/\W+/).filter(t => t.length > 2)

58	    .filter(t => t.length > 3 && !STOPWORDS.has(t))

63	      const haystack = (chunk.text || chunk.content || '').toLowerCase()
64	      // Entity tokens worth 2 pts each (high precision), query tokens 1 pt

original_content:
1	# REEtrieve — Critical Mineral Supply Chain Intelligence
2	
3	> *Can a battery's "ethically sourced" claim survive a 10-second check?*
4	
5	REEtrieve is an open-data PoC that traces rare earth, cobalt, nickel, tin, and tungsten supply chains — flags laundering hubs, mass-balance gaps, and moral risk — and lets auditors interrogate the evidence via an on-device AI chat grounded in real documents.
6	
7	---
8	
9	## What it does
10	
11	| Layer | What it does |
12	|---|---|
13	| **Geo-Forensic Map** | Plots 200+ mines, refineries, and transit hubs. Infers missing coordinates at state level. Flags AIS dark-shipping corridors. |
14	| **Bayesian Risk Engine** | 18 signals across 4 dimensions: child labour, forced labour, document fraud, moral risk. Non-independence product model — no hardcoded scores, everything derived from public data. |
15	| **High-Risk Audit Dashboard** | 13 flagged entities across 6 continents. OECD 5-step compliance checklist. Grade assignment (A–F) with OECD §4.1–4.4 citations. Inspector workflow with file upload and audit history. |
16	| **RAG + Local AI Chat** | Investigators ask natural-language questions grounded in USGS, UN Comtrade, OECD, and RMI documents. Two-sided analysis enforced. All claims cited. Runs 100% on-device — no API key. |
17	
18	---
19	
20	## Data sources
21	
22	- **USGS** Mineral Commodity Summaries 2019–2024 + element fact sheets
23	- **UN Comtrade** trade flow API — HS codes for REE, Co, Ni, Sn, W (2018–2024)
24	- **OECD** Due Diligence Guidance 3rd Ed. — checklist and grade multipliers
25	- **RMI AMRT** — smelter/refiner audit lists
26	- **GISTM** Global Tailings Portal — TSF consequence classifications
27	- **BHRRC** Transition Minerals Tracker — allegation density
28	- **Global Witness** — "The Sacrifice Zone" 2024 hyperspectral satellite investigation
29	- **Benchmark Mineral Intelligence** — REE carbon & waste ESG scores
30	
31	---
32	
33	## Risk signals (18 total)
34	
35	| Dimension | Signals |
36	|---|---|
37	| Child Labour | Production spike (USGS/EITI), ILO child labour rate, UNESCO enrolment gap, NASA VIIRS night luminosity |
38	| Forced Labour | Armed group proximity (IPIS/ACLED), ILO forced labour indicator, debt bondage (Amnesty), AIS dark shipping |
39	| Document & Trade Fraud | Comtrade mirror-trade gap, AIS dark port events, certificate temporal fraud, satellite concession mismatch, invoice below LME floor |
40	| Moral Risk | RMI AMRT absence, GISTM tailings class, BHRRC allegation density, Global Witness investigation flag, BMI ESG below median |
41	
42	---
43	
44	## Tech stack
45	
46	- **Frontend** — React 18 + Vite, MapLibre GL, Deck.gl
47	- **Risk engine** — Bayesian non-independence product (`scoring.js`) — pure JS, no library
48	- **RAG pipeline** — Python: `pdfplumber` → sliding-window chunker → JSONL corpus
49	- **AI chat** — Ollama (local, free, no API key) · Express SSE proxy · keyword-ranked chunk retrieval
50	- **Models** — Llama 3.2 (fast) / Mistral (stronger reasoning) — switchable in UI
51	
52	---
53	
54	## Run it
55	
56	```bash
57	# 1. Install Ollama and pull models (one-time, ~4 GB total)
58	brew install ollama
59	ollama serve
60	ollama pull llama3.2    # fast
61	ollama pull mistral     # stronger reasoning — switchable in UI
62	
63	# 2. Start the app
64	cd webapp
65	cp .env.example .env
66	npm install
67	npm run dev:all          # starts Vite + Express API concurrently
68	
69	# 3. Build the RAG corpus (one-time, ~15 min)
70	cd rag_prep
71	pip install pdfplumber tqdm requests
72	python 01_scrape_usgs.py        # USGS PDFs (26 documents)
73	python 01b_extract_usgs_text.py # PDF → inspectable text
74	python 02_scrape_comtrade.py    # UN Comtrade trade flows
75	python 03_scrape_oecd_sec.py    # OECD guidance + SEC CMR exhibits
76	python 05_chunk.py              # sliding-window chunker → chunks.jsonl
77	```
78	
79	Open `http://localhost:5173` → Audit Dashboard → click any entity → **Ask AI**
80	
81	---
82	
83	## Uninstall
84	
85	### Remove npm packages added for this project
86	```bash
87	cd webapp
88	npm uninstall @anthropic-ai/sdk express dotenv
89	npm uninstall --save-dev concurrently
90	```
91	
92	### Remove Ollama models
93	```bash
94	ollama rm llama3.2
95	ollama rm mistral
96	```
97	
98	### Remove Ollama itself
99	```bash
100	# Stop the service
101	pkill ollama
102	
103	# Homebrew install
104	brew uninstall ollama
105	
106	# Remove model data (~4 GB)
107	rm -rf ~/.ollama
108	
109	# Remove the app if you installed via .dmg
110	rm -rf /Applications/Ollama.app
111	```
112	
113	### Remove Python RAG dependencies
114	```bash
115	pip uninstall pdfplumber tqdm requests ocrmypdf pytesseract -y
116	```
117	

<system-reminder>
Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.
</system-reminder>

The following skills are available for use with the Skill tool:

- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- savings: Show Context Gateway compression savings report
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch
</system-reminder>

original_content:
1	/**
2	 * REEtrieve Intelligence — chat API server
3	 * POST /api/chat  →  SSE stream of Ollama responses grounded in RAG corpus
4	 *
5	 * Run alongside Vite:  npm run dev:all
6	 * Requires:  Ollama running locally (ollama serve) with a model pulled.
7	 *
8	 * Quick setup:
9	 *   brew install ollama
10	 *   ollama serve          # or open Ollama.app
11	 *   ollama pull llama3.2  # ~2 GB — good quality/speed balance
12	 *
13	 * Override model or URL via .env:
14	 *   OLLAMA_MODEL=mistral
15	 *   OLLAMA_URL=http://localhost:11434
16	 */
17	
18	import express from 'express'
19	import { readFileSync, existsSync } from 'fs'
20	import path from 'path'
21	import { fileURLToPath } from 'url'
22	import 'dotenv/config'
23	
24	const __dirname = path.dirname(fileURLToPath(import.meta.url))
25	const app = express()
26	app.use(express.json({ limit: '1mb' }))
27	
28	const PORT         = process.env.PORT        ?? 3001
29	const OLLAMA_URL   = process.env.OLLAMA_URL  ?? 'http://localhost:11434'
30	const OLLAMA_MODEL = process.env.OLLAMA_MODEL ?? 'llama3.2'
31	
32	// ── Load RAG corpus once at startup ──────────────────────────────────────────
33	const CHUNKS_PATH = process.env.CHUNKS_PATH
34	  ?? path.resolve(__dirname, '../../rag_prep/data/chunks/chunks.jsonl')
35	
36	let CHUNKS = []
37	if (existsSync(CHUNKS_PATH)) {
38	  const lines = readFileSync(CHUNKS_PATH, 'utf8').trim().split('\n').filter(Boolean)
39	  CHUNKS = lines.map(l => { try { return JSON.parse(l) } catch { return null } }).filter(Boolean)
40	  console.log(`✓ Loaded ${CHUNKS.length} RAG chunks from ${path.basename(path.dirname(CHUNKS_PATH))}/chunks.jsonl`)
41	} else {
42	  console.warn(`⚠ chunks.jsonl not found at:\n  ${CHUNKS_PATH}\n  Running in entity-data-only mode (RAG disabled).\n  Run: cd rag_prep && python 05_chunk.py`)
43	}
44	
45	// ── Keyword-based chunk retrieval ─────────────────────────────────────────────
46	const STOPWORDS = new Set([
47	  'the','and','for','that','this','with','are','from','into','what',
48	  'which','have','been','will','when','where','how','does','can','its',
49	  'their','about','used','also','over','more','than','such',
50	])
51	
52	function retrieveChunks(entityName, query, topK = 7) {
53	  if (!CHUNKS.length) return []
54	
55	  // Build token set: entity name tokens + meaningful query tokens
56	  const entityTokens = entityName.toLowerCase().split(/\W+/).filter(t => t.length > 2)
57	  const queryTokens  = query.toLowerCase().split(/\W+/)
58	    .filter(t => t.length > 3 && !STOPWORDS.has(t))
59	  const allTokens    = [...new Set([...entityTokens, ...queryTokens])]
60	
61	  return CHUNKS
62	    .map(chunk => {
63	      const haystack = (chunk.text || chunk.content || '').toLowerCase()
64	      // Entity tokens worth 2 pts each (high precision), query tokens 1 pt
65	      const score =
66	        entityTokens.reduce((s, t) => s + (haystack.includes(t) ? 2 : 0), 0) +
67	        queryTokens.reduce((s, t)  => s + (haystack.includes(t) ? 1 : 0), 0)
68	      return { ...chunk, _score: score }
69	    })
70	    .filter(c => c._score > 0)
71	    .sort((a, b) => b._score - a._score)
72	    .slice(0, topK)
73	}
74	
75	// ── System prompt ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
76	function buildSystemPrompt(entity, chunks) {
77	  const chunkBlock = chunks.length
78	    ? chunks.map((c, i) => [
79	        `[DOC ${i + 1}] ${c.source ?? c.source_type ?? 'Unknown source'} | Year: ${c.year ?? '?'} | Page: ${c.page ?? '?'}`,
80	        c.url ? `URL: ${c.url}` : null,
81	        '',
82	        c.text ?? c.content ?? '',
83	      ].filter(x => x !== null).join('\n'))
84	      .join('\n\n' + '─'.repeat(60) + '\n\n')
85	    : 'No RAG corpus available for this session. Base your analysis on the entity signal data only and be explicit about this limitation.'
86	
87	  const signalLegend = [
88	    'cl1 = Unexplained Production Spike (USGS / EITI)',
89	    'cl2 = Regional Child Labour Prevalence (ILO ILOSTAT)',
90	    'cl3 = Low School Enrolment + Literacy (UNESCO)',
91	    'cl4 = Nighttime Luminosity Anomaly (NASA VIIRS)',
92	    'fl1 = Armed Group / Mine Proximity (IPIS / ACLED)',
93	    'fl2 = ILO Forced Labour Indicator (ILO 2022)',
94	    'fl3 = Debt Bondage / Wage Withholding (Amnesty Intl)',
95	    'fl4 = AIS Dark Shipping Events (MarineTraffic)',
96	    'df1 = Comtrade Mirror-Trade Gap (UN Comtrade)',
97	    'df2 = AIS Dark Events at Transit Port (MarineTraffic)',
98	    'df3 = Certificate Temporal Fraud (RMI RMAP / EITI)',
99	    'df4 = Mine Area vs Concession Mismatch (Sentinel-2)',
100	    'df5 = Invoice Price Below Market Floor (LME / Fastmarkets)',
101	    'mr1 = RMI AMRT Non-Compliance (RMI 2024)',
102	    'mr2 = GISTM Tailings Extreme/Very High (Global Tailings Portal 2025)',
103	    'mr3 = BHRRC Allegation Density (BHRRC TMT 2024)',
104	    'mr4 = Global Witness Investigative Flag (GW 2024)',
105	    'mr5 = BMI Carbon & Waste ESG Below Median (BMI 2024)',
106	  ].join('\n  ')
107	
108	  const preset = entity.signalPreset ?? {}
109	  const signalLines = Object.entries(preset)
110	    .map(([k, v]) => `  ${k}: ${v.toFixed(2)}`)
111	    .join('\n')
112	
113	  return `You are REEtrieve Intelligence, an investigative AI embedded in the REEtrieve Compliance Platform. You specialise in critical mineral supply chains — specifically rare earth elements (REE), cobalt, nickel, tin, and tungsten.
114	
115	Your audience is compliance officers, regulators, journalists, and auditors who need rigorous, cited, probabilistic analysis — not reassurance.
116	
117	${'═'.repeat(65)}
118	ENTITY UNDER REVIEW
119	${'═'.repeat(65)}
120	Name:            ${entity.name}
121	Country:         ${entity.flag ?? ''} ${entity.country}
122	Industry:        ${entity.industry}
123	Risk Score:      ${entity.riskScore}/100  (Bayesian composite, 4 dimensions)
124	Cert Status:     ${entity.certStatus ?? 'unknown'}
125	Last Audit:      ${entity.lastAudit ?? 'never'}
126	Risk Flags:      ${(entity.riskFlags ?? []).join(' | ') || 'none'}
127	Intel Note:      ${entity.note ?? ''}
128	
129	SIGNAL ACTIVATIONS (0 = no signal, 1 = fully active):
130	${signalLines}
131	
132	SIGNAL LEGEND:
133	  ${signalLegend}
134	
135	${'═'.repeat(65)}
136	RELEVANT DOCUMENT EXCERPTS  (ranked by relevance to your query)
137	${'═'.repeat(65)}
138	${chunkBlock}
139	
140	${'═'.repeat(65)}
141	ANALYTICAL RULES — APPLY TO EVERY RESPONSE
142	${'═'.repeat(65)}
143	1. CITATION REQUIRED: Every factual claim must end with its source in parentheses.
144	   Use exact names: (USGS MCS 2024), (UN Comtrade 2023), (ILO ILOSTAT 2022),
145	   (RMI 2024), (BHRRC TMT 2024), (Global Witness 2024), (BMI 2024),
146	   or (REEtrieve signal data) for values derived from signal activations above.
147	
148	2. TWO-SIDED ANALYSIS: After each risk finding, state the strongest
149	   counter-argument or benign alternative interpretation, then explain
150	   why you accept or reject it given the available evidence.
151	
152	3. RELATIVIZE SCORES: Interpret numbers in context.
153	   The 13-entity database range is roughly 38–95. Quartile breakpoints:
154	   ≥82 = top quartile (critical), 68–81 = high, 52–67 = elevated, <52 = lower.
155	   Always say where this entity sits.
156	
157	4. PROBABILISTIC FRAMING: Use "the evidence is consistent with...",
158	   "with high/moderate/low confidence...", "this cannot be confirmed but...".
159	   Never state certainties. Supply chains are inherently uncertain.
160	
161	5. MISSING DATA IS A SIGNAL: If a dataset doesn't cover this entity,
162	   name the gap explicitly and explain what inference is still possible.
163	   E.g., absence from GISTM disclosures after the 2025 mandatory deadline
164	   is itself non-compliance, not a neutral absence.
165	
166	6. TEMPORAL LAG: Materials extracted today appear in products in 2–3 years.
167	   A battery sold in 2025 contains ore from 2022–2023. Always anchor
168	   downstream product claims to the correct extraction window.
169	
170	7. DOWNSTREAM TRACING: Trace material flows step by step using the document
171	   corpus: mine → smelter → refiner → manufacturer → brand. State each
172	   inference link explicitly. Only name companies that appear in cited documents.
173	   If a company cannot be confirmed, say so rather than speculating.
174	
175	8. SCOPE GUARD: You cover REE, cobalt, nickel, tin, tungsten only.
176	   If asked about other minerals, note this is outside your current dataset.
177	
178	9. ADVERSARIAL LENS: Always ask — could this data have been falsified?
179	   Who benefits from the official narrative? Flag where document fraud
180	   signals (df1–df5) suggest the numbers themselves may be unreliable.`
181	}
182	
183	// ── Chat endpoint ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
184	app.post('/api/chat', async (req, res) => {
185	  const { entity, messages } = req.body
186	
187	  if (!entity || !Array.isArray(messages) || !messages.length) {
188	    return res.status(400).json({ error: 'entity and messages[] required' })
189	  }
190	
191	  // Extract last user message for retrieval
192	  const lastUser = [...messages].reverse().find(m => m.role === 'user')?.content ?? ''
193	  const chunks   = retrieveChunks(entity.name, lastUser)
194	
195	  const systemPrompt = buildSystemPrompt(entity, chunks)
196	
197	  // SSE headers
198	  res.setHeader('Content-Type', 'text/event-stream')
199	  res.setHeader('Cache-Control', 'no-cache')
200	  res.setHeader('Connection', 'keep-alive')
201	  res.setHeader('X-Accel-Buffering', 'no')
202	  res.flushHeaders()
203	
204	  try {
205	    // Ollama OpenAI-compatible chat endpoint with streaming
206	    const model = req.body.model ?? OLLAMA_MODEL
207	    const ollamaRes = await fetch(`${OLLAMA_URL}/api/chat`, {
208	      method: 'POST',
209	      headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
210	      body: JSON.stringify({
211	        model,
212	        stream: true,
213	        options: { num_predict: 1500, temperature: 0.3 },
214	        messages: [
215	          { role: 'system', content: systemPrompt },
216	          ...messages.map(m => ({ role: m.role, content: m.content })),
217	        ],
218	      }),
219	    })
220	
221	    if (!ollamaRes.ok) {
222	      const err = await ollamaRes.text()
223	      throw new Error(`Ollama ${ollamaRes.status}: ${err}`)
224	    }
225	
226	    const reader  = ollamaRes.body.getReader()
227	    const decoder = new TextDecoder()
228	    let   buffer  = ''
229	
230	    while (true) {
231	      const { done, value } = await reader.read()
232	      if (done) break
233	      buffer += decoder.decode(value, { stream: true })
234	      const lines = buffer.split('\n')
235	      buffer = lines.pop()
236	      for (const line of lines) {
237	        if (!line.trim()) continue
238	        try {
239	          const json = JSON.parse(line)
240	          const text = json.message?.content
241	          if (text) res.write(`data: ${JSON.stringify({ text })}\n\n`)
242	          if (json.done) {
243	            res.write('data: [DONE]\n\n')
244	            res.end()
245	            return
246	          }
247	        } catch { /* partial JSON — skip */ }
248	      }
249	    }
250	
251	    res.write('data: [DONE]\n\n')
252	    res.end()
253	  } catch (err) {
254	    console.error('Ollama error:', err.message)
255	    if (!res.headersSent) {
256	      res.status(500).json({ error: err.message })
257	    } else {
258	      res.write(`data: ${JSON.stringify({ error: err.message })}\n\n`)
259	      res.end()
260	    }
261	  }
262	})
263	
264	app.listen(PORT, () => {
265	  console.log(`\nREEtrieve chat API  →  http://localhost:${PORT}`)
266	  console.log(`Ollama:              ${OLLAMA_URL}`)
267	  console.log(`Model:               ${OLLAMA_MODEL}`)
268	  console.log(`RAG corpus:          ${CHUNKS.length} chunks loaded\n`)
269	})
270

original_content:
1	# REEtrieve — Critical Mineral Supply Chain Intelligence
2	
3	> *Can a battery's "ethically sourced" claim survive a 10-second check?*
4	
5	REEtrieve is an open-data PoC that traces rare earth, cobalt, nickel, tin, and tungsten supply chains — flags laundering hubs, mass-balance gaps, and moral risk — and lets auditors interrogate the evidence via an on-device AI chat grounded in real documents.
6	
7	---
8	
9	## What it does
10	
11	| Layer | What it does |
12	|---|---|
13	| **Geo-Forensic Map** | Plots 200+ mines, refineries, and transit hubs. Infers missing coordinates at state level. Flags AIS dark-shipping corridors. |
14	| **Bayesian Risk Engine** | 18 signals across 4 dimensions: child labour, forced labour, document fraud, moral risk. Non-independence product model — no hardcoded scores, everything derived from public data. |
15	| **High-Risk Audit Dashboard** | 13 flagged entities across 6 continents. OECD 5-step compliance checklist. Grade assignment (A–F) with OECD §4.1–4.4 citations. Inspector workflow with file upload and audit history. |
16	| **RAG + Local AI Chat** | Investigators ask natural-language questions grounded in USGS, UN Comtrade, OECD, and RMI documents. Two-sided analysis enforced. All claims cited. Runs 100% on-device — no API key. |
17	
18	---
19	
20	## Data sources
21	
22	- **USGS** Mineral Commodity Summaries 2019–2024 + element fact sheets
23	- **UN Comtrade** trade flow API — HS codes for REE, Co, Ni, Sn, W (2018–2024)
24	- **OECD** Due Diligence Guidance 3rd Ed. — checklist and grade multipliers
25	- **RMI AMRT** — smelter/refiner audit lists
26	- **GISTM** Global Tailings Portal — TSF consequence classifications
27	- **BHRRC** Transition Minerals Tracker — allegation density
28	- **Global Witness** — "The Sacrifice Zone" 2024 hyperspectral satellite investigation
29	- **Benchmark Mineral Intelligence** — REE carbon & waste ESG scores
30	
31	---
32	
33	## Risk signals (18 total)
34	
35	| Dimension | Signals |
36	|---|---|
37	| Child Labour | Production spike (USGS/EITI), ILO child labour rate, UNESCO enrolment gap, NASA VIIRS night luminosity |
38	| Forced Labour | Armed group proximity (IPIS/ACLED), ILO forced labour indicator, debt bondage (Amnesty), AIS dark shipping |
39	| Document & Trade Fraud | Comtrade mirror-trade gap, AIS dark port events, certificate temporal fraud, satellite concession mismatch, invoice below LME floor |
40	| Moral Risk | RMI AMRT absence, GISTM tailings class, BHRRC allegation density, Global Witness investigation flag, BMI ESG below median |
41	
42	---
43	
44	## Tech stack
45	
46	- **Frontend** — React 18 + Vite, MapLibre GL, Deck.gl
47	- **Risk engine** — Bayesian non-independence product (`scoring.js`) — pure JS, no library
48	- **RAG pipeline** — Python: `pdfplumber` → sliding-window chunker → JSONL corpus
49	- **AI chat** — Ollama (local, free, no API key) · Express SSE proxy · keyword-ranked chunk retrieval
50	- **Models** — Llama 3.2 (fast) / Mistral (stronger reasoning) — switchable in UI
51	
52	---
53	
54	## Run it
55	
56	```bash
57	# 1. Install Ollama and pull models (one-time, ~4 GB total)
58	brew install ollama
59	ollama serve
60	ollama pull llama3.2    # fast
61	ollama pull mistral     # stronger reasoning — switchable in UI
62	
63	# 2. Start the app
64	cd webapp
65	cp .env.example .env

1	# REEtrieve — Critical Mineral Supply Chain Intelligence
2	
3	> *Can a battery's "ethically sourced" claim survive a 10-second check?*
4	
5	REEtrieve is an open-data PoC that traces rare earth, cobalt, nickel, tin, and tungsten supply chains — flags laundering hubs, mass-balance gaps, and moral risk — and lets auditors interrogate the evidence via an on-device AI chat grounded in real documents.
6	
7	---
8	
9	## What it does
10	
11	| Layer | What it does |
12	|---|---|
13	| **Geo-Forensic Map** | Plots 200+ mines, refineries, and transit hubs. Infers missing coordinates at state level. Flags AIS dark-shipping corridors. |
14	| **Bayesian Risk Engine** | 18 signals across 4 dimensions: child labour, forced labour, document fraud, moral risk. Non-independence product model — no hardcoded scores, everything derived from public data. |
15	| **High-Risk Audit Dashboard** | 13 flagged entities across 6 continents. OECD 5-step compliance checklist. Grade assignment (A–F) with OECD §4.1–4.4 citations. Inspector workflow with file upload and audit history. |
16	| **RAG + Local AI Chat** | Investigators ask natural-language questions grounded in USGS, UN Comtrade, OECD, and RMI documents. Two-sided analysis enforced. All claims cited. Runs 100% on-device — no API key. |
17	
18	---
19	
20	## Data sources
21	
22	- **USGS** Mineral Commodity Summaries 2019–2024 + element fact sheets
23	- **UN Comtrade** trade flow API — HS codes for REE, Co, Ni, Sn, W (2018–2024)
24	- **OECD** Due Diligence Guidance 3rd Ed. — checklist and grade multipliers
25	- **RMI AMRT** — smelter/refiner audit lists
26	- **GISTM** Global Tailings Portal — TSF consequence classifications
27	- **BHRRC** Transition Minerals Tracker — allegation density
28	- **Global Witness** — "The Sacrifice Zone" 2024 hyperspectral satellite investigation
29	- **Benchmark Mineral Intelligence** — REE carbon & waste ESG scores
30	
31	---
32	
33	## Risk signals (18 total)
34	
35	| Dimension | Signals |
36	|---|---|
37	| Child Labour | Production spike (USGS/EITI), ILO child labour rate, UNESCO enrolment gap, NASA VIIRS night luminosity |
38	| Forced Labour | Armed group proximity (IPIS/ACLED), ILO forced labour indicator, debt bondage (Amnesty), AIS dark shipping |
39	| Document & Trade Fraud | Comtrade mirror-trade gap, AIS dark port events, certificate temporal fraud, satellite concession mismatch, invoice below LME floor |
40	| Moral Risk | RMI AMRT absence, GISTM tailings class, BHRRC allegation density, Global Witness investigation flag, BMI ESG below median |
41	
42	---
43	
44	## Tech stack
45	
46	- **Frontend** — React 18 + Vite, MapLibre GL, Deck.gl
47	- **Risk engine** — Bayesian non-independence product (`scoring.js`) — pure JS, no library
48	- **RAG pipeline** — Python: `pdfplumber` → sliding-window chunker → JSONL corpus
49	- **AI chat** — Ollama (local, free, no API key) · Express SSE proxy · keyword-ranked chunk retrieval
50	- **Models** — Llama 3.2 (fast) / Mistral (stronger reasoning) — switchable in UI
51	
52	---
53	
54	## Run it
[4 lines cut]
59	ollama serve
60	ollama pull llama3.2    # fast
61	ollama pull mistral     # stronger reasoning — switchable in UI
62	
63	# 2. Start the app
64	cd webapp
65	cp .env.example .env
66	npm install
67	npm run dev:all          # starts Vite + Express API concurrently
68	
69	# 3. Build the RAG corpus (one-time, ~15 min)
70	cd rag_prep
71	pip install pdfplumber tqdm requests

75	python 03_scrape_oecd_sec.py    # OECD guidance + SEC CMR exhibits

77	```

1	"""
2	Step 3 — Embedding.
3	Reads data/chunks/chunks.jsonl, embeds with nomic-embed-text-v1.5 via transformers
4	(no sentence-transformers / datasets / pyarrow dependency), writes FAISS index.
5	"""
6	
7	import json, pathlib, sys
8	import numpy as np
9	
10	# ── config ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
11	CHUNKS_FILE = pathlib.Path(__file__).parent / "data/chunks/chunks.jsonl"
12	OUT_DIR     = pathlib.Path(__file__).parent / "data/embeddings"
13	OUT_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
14	INDEX_FILE  = OUT_DIR / "index.faiss"
15	META_FILE   = OUT_DIR / "metadata.jsonl"
16	
17	MODEL_NAME  = "nomic-ai/nomic-embed-text-v1.5"
18	BATCH_SIZE  = 64
19	DOC_PREFIX  = "search_document: "
20	
21	
22	def mean_pool(token_embeddings, attention_mask):
23	    mask = attention_mask.unsqueeze(-1).expand(token_embeddings.size()).float()
24	    return (token_embeddings * mask).sum(1) / mask.sum(1).clamp(min=1e-9)
25	
26	
27	def encode_batch(model, tokenizer, texts, device):
28	    import torch
29	    import torch.nn.functional as F
30	    enc = tokenizer(texts, padding=True, truncation=True,
31	                    max_length=512, return_tensors="pt").to(device)
32	    with torch.no_grad():
33	        out = model(**enc)
34	    vecs = mean_pool(out.last_hidden_state, enc["attention_mask"])
35	    return F.normalize(vecs, p=2, dim=1).cpu().numpy().astype(np.float32)
36	
37	
38	def main():
39	    try:
40	        import torch
41	        from transformers import AutoTokenizer, AutoModel
42	        import faiss
43	    except ImportError:
44	        print("Missing deps. Run:  pip install torch transformers faiss-cpu")
45	        sys.exit(1)
46	
47	    # ── device ──
48	    if torch.backends.mps.is_available():
49	        device = "mps"
50	    elif torch.cuda.is_available():
51	        device = "cuda"
52	    else:
53	        device = "cpu"
54	    print(f"Device: {device}\n")
55	
56	    # ── load chunks ──
57	    if not CHUNKS_FILE.exists():
58	        print(f"✗ {CHUNKS_FILE} not found — run 05_chunk.py first")
59	        sys.exit(1)
60	
61	    print("Loading chunks...", flush=True)
62	    chunks = []
63	    with open(CHUNKS_FILE, encoding="utf-8") as f:
64	        for line in f:
65	            line = line.strip()
66	            if line:
67	                chunks.append(json.loads(line))
68	    n = len(chunks)
69	    print(f"✓ {n} chunks\n", flush=True)
70	
71	    # ── load model ──
72	    print(f"Loading {MODEL_NAME}...", flush=True)
73	    tokenizer = AutoTokenizer.from_pretrained(MODEL_NAME, trust_remote_code=True)
74	    model     = AutoModel.from_pretrained(MODEL_NAME, trust_remote_code=True).to(device)
75	    model.eval()
76	    print("✓ Model loaded\n", flush=True)
77	
78	    # ── embed ──
79	    texts = [DOC_PREFIX + c["text"] for c in chunks]
80	    all_vecs = []
81	    total_batches = (n + BATCH_SIZE - 1) // BATCH_SIZE
82	
83	    for i in range(0, n, BATCH_SIZE):
84	        batch = texts[i : i + BATCH_SIZE]
85	        vecs  = encode_batch(model, tokenizer, batch, device)
86	        all_vecs.append(vecs)
87	        done = min(i + BATCH_SIZE, n)
88	        pct  = int(done / n * 100)
89	        print(f"  [{done:>{len(str(n))}}/{n}] {pct:>3}%", flush=True)
90	
91	    embeddings = np.vstack(all_vecs)
92	
93	    # ── FAISS index ──
94	    dim   = embeddings.shape[1]
95	    index = faiss.IndexFlatIP(dim)  # cosine sim (vecs are L2-normalised)
96	    index.add(embeddings)
97	    faiss.write_index(index, str(INDEX_FILE))
98	    print(f"\n✓ FAISS index  → {INDEX_FILE}  ({dim}-dim, {index.ntotal} vectors)")
99	
100	    # ── metadata ──
101	    with open(META_FILE, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
102	        for chunk in chunks:
103	            f.write(json.dumps(chunk, ensure_ascii=False) + "\n")
104	    print(f"✓ Metadata     → {META_FILE}")
105	    print(f"\nTo query: embed with prefix 'search_query: ', then index.search(vec, k)")
106	
107	
108	if __name__ == "__main__":
109	    main()
110

The following skills are available for use with the Skill tool:

- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- savings: Show Context Gateway compression savings report
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch
</system-reminder>

1	/**
2	 * REEtrieve Intelligence — chat API server
3	 * POST /api/chat  →  SSE stream of Ollama responses grounded in RAG corpus
4	 *
5	 * Run alongside Vite:  npm run dev:all
6	 * Requires:  Ollama running locally (ollama serve) with a model pulled.
7	 *
8	 * Quick setup:
9	 *   brew install ollama
10	 *   ollama serve          # or open Ollama.app
11	 *   ollama pull llama3.2  # ~2 GB — good quality/speed balance
12	 *
13	 * Override model or URL via .env:
14	 *   OLLAMA_MODEL=mistral
15	 *   OLLAMA_URL=http://localhost:11434
16	 */
17	
18	import express from 'express'
19	import { readFileSync, existsSync } from 'fs'
20	import path from 'path'
21	import { fileURLToPath } from 'url'
22	import 'dotenv/config'
23	
24	const __dirname = path.dirname(fileURLToPath(import.meta.url))
25	const app = express()
26	app.use(express.json({ limit: '1mb' }))
27	
28	const PORT         = process.env.PORT        ?? 3001
29	const OLLAMA_URL   = process.env.OLLAMA_URL  ?? 'http://localhost:11434'
30	const OLLAMA_MODEL = process.env.OLLAMA_MODEL ?? 'llama3.2'
31	
32	// ── Load RAG corpus once at startup ──────────────────────────────────────────
33	const CHUNKS_PATH = process.env.CHUNKS_PATH
34	  ?? path.resolve(__dirname, '../../rag_prep/data/chunks/chunks.jsonl')
35	
36	let CHUNKS = []
37	if (existsSync(CHUNKS_PATH)) {
38	  const lines = readFileSync(CHUNKS_PATH, 'utf8').trim().split('\n').filter(Boolean)
39	  CHUNKS = lines.map(l => { try { return JSON.parse(l) } catch { return null } }).filter(Boolean)
40	  console.log(`✓ Loaded ${CHUNKS.length} RAG chunks from ${path.basename(path.dirname(CHUNKS_PATH))}/chunks.jsonl`)
41	} else {
42	  console.warn(`⚠ chunks.jsonl not found at:\n  ${CHUNKS_PATH}\n  Running in entity-data-only mode (RAG disabled).\n  Run: cd rag_prep && python 05_chunk.py`)
43	}
44	
45	// ── Keyword-based chunk retrieval ─────────────────────────────────────────────
46	const STOPWORDS = new Set([
47	  'the','and','for','that','this','with','are','from','into','what',
48	  'which','have','been','will','when','where','how','does','can','its',
49	  'their','about','used','also','over','more','than','such',
50	])
51	
52	function retrieveChunks(entityName, query, topK = 7) {
53	  if (!CHUNKS.length) return []
54	
55	  // Build token set: entity name tokens + meaningful query tokens
56	  const entityTokens = entityName.toLowerCase().split(/\W+/).filter(t => t.length > 2)
57	  const queryTokens  = query.toLowerCase().split(/\W+/)
58	    .filter(t => t.length > 3 && !STOPWORDS.has(t))
59	  const allTokens    = [...new Set([...entityTokens, ...queryTokens])]
60	
61	  return CHUNKS
62	    .map(chunk => {
63	      const haystack = (chunk.text || chunk.content || '').toLowerCase()
64	      // Entity tokens worth 2 pts each (high precision), query tokens 1 pt
65	      const score =
66	        entityTokens.reduce((s, t) => s + (haystack.includes(t) ? 2 : 0), 0) +
67	        queryTokens.reduce((s, t)  => s + (haystack.includes(t) ? 1 : 0), 0)
68	      return { ...chunk, _score: score }
69	    })
70	    .filter(c => c._score > 0)
71	    .sort((a, b) => b._score - a._score)
72	    .slice(0, topK)
73	}
74	
75	// ── System prompt ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
76	function buildSystemPrompt(entity, chunks) {
77	  const chunkBlock = chunks.length
78	    ? chunks.map((c, i) => [
79	        `[DOC ${i + 1}] ${c.source ?? c.source_type ?? 'Unknown source'} | Year: ${c.year ?? '?'} | Page: ${c.page ?? '?'}`,
80	        c.url ? `URL: ${c.url}` : null,
81	        '',
82	        c.text ?? c.content ?? '',
83	      ].filter(x => x !== null).join('\n'))
84	      .join('\n\n' + '─'.repeat(60) + '\n\n')
85	    : 'No RAG corpus available for this session. Base your analysis on the entity signal data only and be explicit about this limitation.'
86	
87	  const signalLegend = [
88	    'cl1 = Unexplained Production Spike (USGS / EITI)',
89	    'cl2 = Regional Child Labour Prevalence (ILO ILOSTAT)',
90	    'cl3 = Low School Enrolment + Literacy (UNESCO)',
91	    'cl4 = Nighttime Luminosity Anomaly (NASA VIIRS)',
92	    'fl1 = Armed Group / Mine Proximity (IPIS / ACLED)',
93	    'fl2 = ILO Forced Labour Indicator (ILO 2022)',
94	    'fl3 = Debt Bondage / Wage Withholding (Amnesty Intl)',
95	    'fl4 = AIS Dark Shipping Events (MarineTraffic)',
96	    'df1 = Comtrade Mirror-Trade Gap (UN Comtrade)',
97	    'df2 = AIS Dark Events at Transit Port (MarineTraffic)',
98	    'df3 = Certificate Temporal Fraud (RMI RMAP / EITI)',
99	    'df4 = Mine Area vs Concession Mismatch (Sentinel-2)',
100	    'df5 = Invoice Price Below Market Floor (LME / Fastmarkets)',
101	    'mr1 = RMI AMRT Non-Compliance (RMI 2024)',
102	    'mr2 = GISTM Tailings Extreme/Very High (Global Tailings Portal 2025)',
103	    'mr3 = BHRRC Allegation Density (BHRRC TMT 2024)',
104	    'mr4 = Global Witness Investigative Flag (GW 2024)',
105	    'mr5 = BMI Carbon & Waste ESG Below Median (BMI 2024)',
106	  ].join('\n  ')
107	
108	  const preset = entity.signalPreset ?? {}
109	  const signalLines = Object.entries(preset)
110	    .map(([k, v]) => `  ${k}: ${v.toFixed(2)}`)
111	    .join('\n')
112	
113	  return `You are REEtrieve Intelligence, an investigative AI embedded in the REEtrieve Compliance Platform. You specialise in critical mineral supply chains — specifically rare earth elements (REE), cobalt, nickel, tin, and tungsten.
114	
115	Your audience is compliance officers, regulators, journalists, and auditors who need rigorous, cited, probabilistic analysis — not reassurance.
116	
117	${'═'.repeat(65)}
118	ENTITY UNDER REVIEW
119	${'═'.repeat(65)}
120	Name:            ${entity.name}
121	Country:         ${entity.flag ?? ''} ${entity.country}
122	Industry:        ${entity.industry}
123	Risk Score:      ${entity.riskScore}/100  (Bayesian composite, 4 dimensions)
124	Cert Status:     ${entity.certStatus ?? 'unknown'}
125	Last Audit:      ${entity.lastAudit ?? 'never'}
126	Risk Flags:      ${(entity.riskFlags ?? []).join(' | ') || 'none'}
127	Intel Note:      ${entity.note ?? ''}
128	
129	SIGNAL ACTIVATIONS (0 = no signal, 1 = fully active):
130	${signalLines}
131	
132	SIGNAL LEGEND:
133	  ${signalLegend}
134	
135	${'═'.repeat(65)}
136	RELEVANT DOCUMENT EXCERPTS  (ranked by relevance to your query)
137	${'═'.repeat(65)}
138	${chunkBlock}
139	
140	${'═'.repeat(65)}
141	ANALYTICAL RULES — APPLY TO EVERY RESPONSE
142	${'═'.repeat(65)}
143	1. CITATION REQUIRED: Every factual claim must end with its source in parentheses.
144	   Use exact names: (USGS MCS 2024), (UN Comtrade 2023), (ILO ILOSTAT 2022),
145	   (RMI 2024), (BHRRC TMT 2024), (Global Witness 2024), (BMI 2024),
146	   or (REEtrieve signal data) for values derived from signal activations above.
147	
148	2. TWO-SIDED ANALYSIS: After each risk finding, state the strongest
149	   counter-argument or benign alternative interpretation, then explain
150	   why you accept or reject it given the available evidence.
151	
152	3. RELATIVIZE SCORES: Interpret numbers in context.
153	   The 13-entity database range is roughly 38–95. Quartile breakpoints:
154	   ≥82 = top quartile (critical), 68–81 = high, 52–67 = elevated, <52 = lower.
155	   Always say where this entity sits.
156	
157	4. PROBABILISTIC FRAMING: Use "the evidence is consistent with...",
158	   "with high/moderate/low confidence...", "this cannot be confirmed but...".
159	   Never state certainties. Supply chains are inherently uncertain.
160	
161	5. MISSING DATA IS A SIGNAL: If a dataset doesn't cover this entity,
162	   name the gap explicitly and explain what inference is still possible.
163	   E.g., absence from GISTM disclosures after the 2025 mandatory deadline
164	   is itself non-compliance, not a neutral absence.
165	
166	6. TEMPORAL LAG: Materials extracted today appear in products in 2–3 years.
167	   A battery sold in 2025 contains ore from 2022–2023. Always anchor
168	   downstream product claims to the correct extraction window.
169	
170	7. DOWNSTREAM TRACING: Trace material flows step by step using the document
171	   corpus: mine → smelter → refiner → manufacturer → brand. State each
172	   inference link explicitly. Only name companies that appear in cited documents.
173	   If a company cannot be confirmed, say so rather than speculating.
174	
175	8. SCOPE GUARD: You cover REE, cobalt, nickel, tin, tungsten only.
176	   If asked about other minerals, note this is outside your current dataset.
177	
178	9. ADVERSARIAL LENS: Always ask — could this data have been falsified?
179	   Who benefits from the official narrative? Flag where document fraud
180	   signals (df1–df5) suggest the numbers themselves may be unreliable.`
181	}
182	
183	// ── Chat endpoint ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
184	app.post('/api/chat', async (req, res) => {
185	  const { entity, messages } = req.body
186	
187	  if (!entity || !Array.isArray(messages) || !messages.length) {
188	    return res.status(400).json({ error: 'entity and messages[] required' })
189	  }
190	
191	  // Extract last user message for retrieval
192	  const lastUser = [...messages].reverse().find(m => m.role === 'user')?.content ?? ''
193	  const chunks   = retrieveChunks(entity.name, lastUser)
194	
195	  const systemPrompt = buildSystemPrompt(entity, chunks)
196	
197	  // SSE headers
198	  res.setHeader('Content-Type', 'text/event-stream')
199	  res.setHeader('Cache-Control', 'no-cache')
200	  res.setHeader('Connection', 'keep-alive')
201	  res.setHeader('X-Accel-Buffering', 'no')
202	  res.flushHeaders()
203	
204	  try {
205	    // Ollama OpenAI-compatible chat endpoint with streaming
206	    const model = req.body.model ?? OLLAMA_MODEL
207	    const ollamaRes = await fetch(`${OLLAMA_URL}/api/chat`, {
208	      method: 'POST',
209	      headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
210	      body: JSON.stringify({
211	        model,
212	        stream: true,
213	        options: { num_predict: 1500, temperature: 0.3 },
214	        messages: [
215	          { role: 'system', content: systemPrompt },
216	          ...messages.map(m => ({ role: m.role, content: m.content })),
217	        ],
218	      }),
219	    })
220	
221	    if (!ollamaRes.ok) {
222	      const err = await ollamaRes.text()
223	      throw new Error(`Ollama ${ollamaRes.status}: ${err}`)
224	    }
225	
226	    const reader  = ollamaRes.body.getReader()
227	    const decoder = new TextDecoder()
228	    let   buffer  = ''
229	
230	    while (true) {
231	      const { done, value } = await reader.read()
232	      if (done) break
233	      buffer += decoder.decode(value, { stream: true })
234	      const lines = buffer.split('\n')
235	      buffer = lines.pop()
236	      for (const line of lines) {
237	        if (!line.trim()) continue
238	        try {
239	          const json = JSON.parse(line)
240	          const text = json.message?.content
241	          if (text) res.write(`data: ${JSON.stringify({ text })}\n\n`)
242	          if (json.done) {
243	            res.write('data: [DONE]\n\n')
244	            res.end()
245	            return
246	          }
247	        } catch { /* partial JSON — skip */ }
248	      }
249	    }
250	
251	    res.write('data: [DONE]\n\n')
252	    res.end()
253	  } catch (err) {
254	    console.error('Ollama error:', err.message)
255	    if (!res.headersSent) {
256	      res.status(500).json({ error: err.message })
257	    } else {
258	      res.write(`data: ${JSON.stringify({ error: err.message })}\n\n`)
259	      res.end()
260	    }
261	  }
262	})
263	
264	app.listen(PORT, () => {
265	  console.log(`\nREEtrieve chat API  →  http://localhost:${PORT}`)
266	  console.log(`Ollama:              ${OLLAMA_URL}`)
267	  console.log(`Model:               ${OLLAMA_MODEL}`)
268	  console.log(`RAG corpus:          ${CHUNKS.length} chunks loaded\n`)
269	})
270

original_content:
1	# REEtrieve — Critical Mineral Supply Chain Intelligence
2	
3	> *Can a battery's "ethically sourced" claim survive a 10-second check?*
4	
5	REEtrieve is an open-data PoC that traces rare earth, cobalt, nickel, tin, and tungsten supply chains — flags laundering hubs, mass-balance gaps, and moral risk — and lets auditors interrogate the evidence via an on-device AI chat grounded in real documents.
6	
7	---
8	
9	## What it does
10	
11	| Layer | What it does |
12	|---|---|
13	| **Geo-Forensic Map** | Plots 200+ mines, refineries, and transit hubs. Infers missing coordinates at state level. Flags AIS dark-shipping corridors. |
14	| **Bayesian Risk Engine** | 18 signals across 4 dimensions: child labour, forced labour, document fraud, moral risk. Non-independence product model — no hardcoded scores, everything derived from public data. |
15	| **High-Risk Audit Dashboard** | 13 flagged entities across 6 continents. OECD 5-step compliance checklist. Grade assignment (A–F) with OECD §4.1–4.4 citations. Inspector workflow with file upload and audit history. |
16	| **RAG + Local AI Chat** | Investigators ask natural-language questions grounded in USGS, UN Comtrade, OECD, and RMI documents. Two-sided analysis enforced. All claims cited. Runs 100% on-device — no API key. |
17	
18	---
19	
20	## Data sources
21	
22	- **USGS** Mineral Commodity Summaries 2019–2024 + element fact sheets
23	- **UN Comtrade** trade flow API — HS codes for REE, Co, Ni, Sn, W (2018–2024)
24	- **OECD** Due Diligence Guidance 3rd Ed. — checklist and grade multipliers
25	- **RMI AMRT** — smelter/refiner audit lists
26	- **GISTM** Global Tailings Portal — TSF consequence classifications
27	- **BHRRC** Transition Minerals Tracker — allegation density
28	- **Global Witness** — "The Sacrifice Zone" 2024 hyperspectral satellite investigation
29	- **Benchmark Mineral Intelligence** — REE carbon & waste ESG scores
30	
31	---
32	
33	## Risk signals (18 total)
34	
35	| Dimension | Signals |
36	|---|---|
37	| Child Labour | Production spike (USGS/EITI), ILO child labour rate, UNESCO enrolment gap, NASA VIIRS night luminosity |
38	| Forced Labour | Armed group proximity (IPIS/ACLED), ILO forced labour indicator, debt bondage (Amnesty), AIS dark shipping |
39	| Document & Trade Fraud | Comtrade mirror-trade gap, AIS dark port events, certificate temporal fraud, satellite concession mismatch, invoice below LME floor |
40	| Moral Risk | RMI AMRT absence, GISTM tailings class, BHRRC allegation density, Global Witness investigation flag, BMI ESG below median |
41	
42	---
43	
44	## Tech stack
45	
46	- **Frontend** — React 18 + Vite, MapLibre GL, Deck.gl
47	- **Risk engine** — Bayesian non-independence product (`scoring.js`) — pure JS, no library
48	- **RAG pipeline** — Python: `pdfplumber` → sliding-window chunker → JSONL corpus
49	- **AI chat** — Ollama (local, free, no API key) · Express SSE proxy · keyword-ranked chunk retrieval
50	- **Models** — Llama 3.2 (fast) / Mistral (stronger reasoning) — switchable in UI
51	
52	---
53	
54	## Run it
55	
56	```bash
57	# 1. Install Ollama and pull models (one-time, ~4 GB total)
58	brew install ollama
59	ollama serve
60	ollama pull llama3.2    # fast
61	ollama pull mistral     # stronger reasoning — switchable in UI
62	
63	# 2. Start the app
64	cd webapp
65	cp .env.example .env
66	npm install
67	npm run dev:all          # starts Vite + Express API concurrently
68	
69	# 3. Build the RAG corpus (one-time, ~15 min)
70	cd rag_prep
71	pip install pdfplumber tqdm requests
72	python 01_scrape_usgs.py        # USGS PDFs (26 documents)
73	python 01b_extract_usgs_text.py # PDF → inspectable text
74	python 02_scrape_comtrade.py    # UN Comtrade trade flows
75	python 03_scrape_oecd_sec.py    # OECD guidance + SEC CMR exhibits
76	python 05_chunk.py              # sliding-window chunker → chunks.jsonl
77	```
78	
79	Open `http://localhost:5173` → Audit Dashboard → click any entity → **Ask AI**
80	
81	---
82	
83	## Uninstall
84	
85	### Remove npm packages added for this project
86	```bash
87	cd webapp
88	npm uninstall @anthropic-ai/sdk express dotenv
89	npm uninstall --save-dev concurrently
90	```
91	
92	### Remove Ollama models
93	```bash
94	ollama rm llama3.2
95	ollama rm mistral
96	```
97	
98	### Remove Ollama itself
99	```bash
100	# Stop the service
101	pkill ollama
102	
103	# Homebrew install
104	brew uninstall ollama
105	
106	# Remove model data (~4 GB)
107	rm -rf ~/.ollama
108	
109	# Remove the app if you installed via .dmg
110	rm -rf /Applications/Ollama.app
111	```
112	
113	### Remove Python RAG dependencies
114	```bash
115	pip uninstall pdfplumber tqdm requests ocrmypdf pytesseract -y
116	```
117

But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.

12	|---|---|
13	| **Geo-Forensic Map** | Plots 200+ mines, refineries, and transit hubs. Infers missing coordinates at state level. Flags AIS dark-shipping corridors. |
14	| **Bayesian Risk Engine** | 18 signals across 4 dimensions: child labour, forced labour, document fraud, moral risk. Non-independence product model — no hardcoded scores, everything derived from public data. |
15	| **High-Risk Audit Dashboard** | 13 flagged entities across 6 continents. OECD 5-step compliance checklist. Grade assignment (A–F) with OECD §4.1–4.4 citations. Inspector workflow with file upload and audit history. |
16	| **RAG + Local AI Chat** | Investigators ask natural-language questions grounded in USGS, UN Comtrade, OECD, and RMI documents. Two-sided analysis enforced. All claims cited. Runs 100% on-device — no API key. |
17	
18	---
19	
20	## Data sources
21	
22	- **USGS** Mineral Commodity Summaries 2019–2024 + element fact sheets
23	- **UN Comtrade** trade flow API — HS codes for REE, Co, Ni, Sn, W (2018–2024)
24	- **OECD** Due Diligence Guidance 3rd Ed. — checklist and grade multipliers
25	- **RMI AMRT** — smelter/refiner audit lists
26	- **GISTM** Global Tailings Portal — TSF consequence classifications
27	- **BHRRC** Transition Minerals Tracker — allegation density
28	- **Global Witness** — "The Sacrifice Zone" 2024 hyperspectral satellite investigation
29	- **Benchmark Mineral Intelligence** — REE carbon & waste ESG scores
30	
31	---
32	
33	## Risk signals (18 total)
34	
35	| Dimension | Signals |
36	|---|---|
37	| Child Labour | Production spike (USGS/EITI), ILO child labour rate, UNESCO enrolment gap, NASA VIIRS night luminosity |
38	| Forced Labour | Armed group proximity (IPIS/ACLED), ILO forced labour indicator, debt bondage (Amnesty), AIS dark shipping |
39	| Document & Trade Fraud | Comtrade mirror-trade gap, AIS dark port events, certificate temporal fraud, satellite concession mismatch, invoice below LME floor |
40	| Moral Risk | RMI AMRT absence, GISTM tailings class, BHRRC allegation density, Global Witness investigation flag, BMI ESG below median |
41	
42	---
43	
44	## Tech stack
45	
46	- **Frontend** — React 18 + Vite, MapLibre GL, Deck.gl
47	- **Risk engine** — Bayesian non-independence product (`scoring.js`) — pure JS, no library
48	- **RAG pipeline** — Python: `pdfplumber` → sliding-window chunker → JSONL corpus
49	- **AI chat** — Ollama (local, free, no API key) · Express SSE proxy · keyword-ranked chunk retrieval
50	- **Models** — Llama 3.2 (fast) / Mistral (stronger reasoning) — switchable in UI
51	
52	---
53	
54	## Run it
[4 lines cut]
59	ollama serve
60	ollama pull llama3.2    # fast
61	ollama pull mistral     # stronger reasoning — switchable in UI
62	
63	# 2. Start the app
64	cd webapp
65	cp .env.example .env
66	npm install
67	npm run dev:all          # starts Vite + Express API concurrently
68	
69	# 3. Build the RAG corpus (one-time, ~15 min)
70	cd rag_prep
71	pip install pdfplumber tqdm requests
72	python 01_scrape_usgs.py        # USGS PDFs (26 documents)
73	python 01b_extract_usgs_text.py # PDF → inspectable text
74	python 02_scrape_comtrade.py    # UN Comtrade trade flows
75	python 03_scrape_oecd_sec.py    # OECD guidance + SEC CMR exhibits
76	python 05_chunk.py              # sliding-window chunker → chunks.jsonl
77	```
78	
79	Open `http://localhost:5173` → Audit Dashboard → click any entity → **Ask AI**
80	
81	---
82

1	"""
2	Step 3 — Embedding.
3	Reads data/chunks/chunks.jsonl, embeds with nomic-embed-text-v1.5 via transformers
4	(no sentence-transformers / datasets / pyarrow dependency), writes FAISS index.
5	"""
6	
7	import json, pathlib, sys
8	import numpy as np
9	
10	# ── config ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
11	CHUNKS_FILE = pathlib.Path(__file__).parent / "data/chunks/chunks.jsonl"
12	OUT_DIR     = pathlib.Path(__file__).parent / "data/embeddings"
13	OUT_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
14	INDEX_FILE  = OUT_DIR / "index.faiss"
15	META_FILE   = OUT_DIR / "metadata.jsonl"
16	
17	MODEL_NAME  = "nomic-ai/nomic-embed-text-v1.5"
18	BATCH_SIZE  = 64
19	DOC_PREFIX  = "search_document: "
20	
21	
22	def mean_pool(token_embeddings, attention_mask):
23	    mask = attention_mask.unsqueeze(-1).expand(token_embeddings.size()).float()
24	    return (token_embeddings * mask).sum(1) / mask.sum(1).clamp(min=1e-9)
25	
26	
27	def encode_batch(model, tokenizer, texts, device):
28	    import torch
29	    import torch.nn.functional as F
30	    enc = tokenizer(texts, padding=True, truncation=True,
31	                    max_length=512, return_tensors="pt").to(device)
32	    with torch.no_grad():
33	        out = model(**enc)
34	    vecs = mean_pool(out.last_hidden_state, enc["attention_mask"])
35	    return F.normalize(vecs, p=2, dim=1).cpu().numpy().astype(np.float32)
36	
37	
38	def main():
39	    try:
40	        import torch
41	        from transformers import AutoTokenizer, AutoModel
42	        import faiss
43	    except ImportError:
44	        print("Missing deps. Run:  pip install torch transformers faiss-cpu")
45	        sys.exit(1)
46	
47	    # ── device ──
48	    if torch.backends.mps.is_available():
49	        device = "mps"
50	    elif torch.cuda.is_available():
51	        device = "cuda"
52	    else:
53	        device = "cpu"
54	    print(f"Device: {device}\n")
55	
56	    # ── load chunks ──
57	    if not CHUNKS_FILE.exists():
58	        print(f"✗ {CHUNKS_FILE} not found — run 05_chunk.py first")
59	        sys.exit(1)
60	
61	    print("Loading chunks...", flush=True)
62	    chunks = []
63	    with open(CHUNKS_FILE, encoding="utf-8") as f:
64	        for line in f:
65	            line = line.strip()
66	            if line:
67	                chunks.append(json.loads(line))
68	    n = len(chunks)
69	    print(f"✓ {n} chunks\n", flush=True)
70	
71	    # ── load model ──
72	    print(f"Loading {MODEL_NAME}...", flush=True)
73	    tokenizer = AutoTokenizer.from_pretrained(MODEL_NAME, trust_remote_code=True)
74	    model     = AutoModel.from_pretrained(MODEL_NAME, trust_remote_code=True).to(device)
75	    model.eval()
76	    print("✓ Model loaded\n", flush=True)
77	
78	    # ── embed ──
79	    texts = [DOC_PREFIX + c["text"] for c in chunks]
80	    all_vecs = []
81	    total_batches = (n + BATCH_SIZE - 1) // BATCH_SIZE
82	
83	    for i in range(0, n, BATCH_SIZE):
84	        batch = texts[i : i + BATCH_SIZE]
85	        vecs  = encode_batch(model, tokenizer, batch, device)
86	        all_vecs.append(vecs)
87	        done = min(i + BATCH_SIZE, n)
88	        pct  = int(done / n * 100)
89	        print(f"  [{done:>{len(str(n))}}/{n}] {pct:>3}%", flush=True)
90	
91	    embeddings = np.vstack(all_vecs)
92	
93	    # ── FAISS index ──
94	    dim   = embeddings.shape[1]
95	    index = faiss.IndexFlatIP(dim)  # cosine sim (vecs are L2-normalised)
96	    index.add(embeddings)
97	    faiss.write_index(index, str(INDEX_FILE))
98	    print(f"\n✓ FAISS index  → {INDEX_FILE}  ({dim}-dim, {index.ntotal} vectors)")
99	
100	    # ── metadata ──
101	    with open(META_FILE, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
102	        for chunk in chunks:
103	            f.write(json.dumps(chunk, ensure_ascii=False) + "\n")
104	    print(f"✓ Metadata     → {META_FILE}")
105	    print(f"\nTo query: embed with prefix 'search_query: ', then index.search(vec, k)")
106	
107	
108	if __name__ == "__main__":
109	    main()
110

original_content:
1	/**
2	 * REEtrieve Intelligence — chat API server
3	 * POST /api/chat  →  SSE stream of Ollama responses grounded in RAG corpus
4	 *
5	 * Run alongside Vite:  npm run dev:all
6	 * Requires:  Ollama running locally (ollama serve) with a model pulled.
7	 *
8	 * Quick setup:
9	 *   brew install ollama
10	 *   ollama serve          # or open Ollama.app
11	 *   ollama pull llama3.2  # ~2 GB — good quality/speed balance
12	 *
13	 * Override model or URL via .env:
14	 *   OLLAMA_MODEL=mistral
15	 *   OLLAMA_URL=http://localhost:11434
16	 */
17	
18	import express from 'express'
19	import { readFileSync, existsSync } from 'fs'
20	import path from 'path'
21	import { fileURLToPath } from 'url'
22	import 'dotenv/config'
23	
24	const __dirname = path.dirname(fileURLToPath(import.meta.url))
25	const app = express()
26	app.use(express.json({ limit: '1mb' }))
27	
28	const PORT         = process.env.PORT        ?? 3001
29	const OLLAMA_URL   = process.env.OLLAMA_URL  ?? 'http://localhost:11434'
30	const OLLAMA_MODEL = process.env.OLLAMA_MODEL ?? 'llama3.2'
31	
32	// ── Load RAG corpus once at startup ──────────────────────────────────────────
33	const CHUNKS_PATH = process.env.CHUNKS_PATH
34	  ?? path.resolve(__dirname, '../../rag_prep/data/chunks/chunks.jsonl')
35	
36	let CHUNKS = []
37	if (existsSync(CHUNKS_PATH)) {
38	  const lines = readFileSync(CHUNKS_PATH, 'utf8').trim().split('\n').filter(Boolean)
39	  CHUNKS = lines.map(l => { try { return JSON.parse(l) } catch { return null } }).filter(Boolean)
40	  console.log(`✓ Loaded ${CHUNKS.length} RAG chunks from ${path.basename(path.dirname(CHUNKS_PATH))}/chunks.jsonl`)
41	} else {
42	  console.warn(`⚠ chunks.jsonl not found at:\n  ${CHUNKS_PATH}\n  Running in entity-data-only mode (RAG disabled).\n  Run: cd rag_prep && python 05_chunk.py`)
43	}
44	
45	// ── Keyword-based chunk retrieval ─────────────────────────────────────────────
46	const STOPWORDS = new Set([
47	  'the','and','for','that','this','with','are','from','into','what',
48	  'which','have','been','will','when','where','how','does','can','its',
49	  'their','about','used','also','over','more','than','such',
50	])
51	
52	function retrieveChunks(entityName, query, topK = 7) {
53	  if (!CHUNKS.length) return []
54	
55	  // Build token set: entity name tokens + meaningful query tokens
56	  const entityTokens = entityName.toLowerCase().split(/\W+/).filter(t => t.length > 2)
57	  const queryTokens  = query.toLowerCase().split(/\W+/)
58	    .filter(t => t.length > 3 && !STOPWORDS.has(t))
59	  const allTokens    = [...new Set([...entityTokens, ...queryTokens])]
60	
61	  return CHUNKS
62	    .map(chunk => {
63	      const haystack = (chunk.text || chunk.content || '').toLowerCase()
64	      // Entity tokens worth 2 pts each (high precision), query tokens 1 pt
65	      const score =
66	        entityTokens.reduce((s, t) => s + (haystack.includes(t) ? 2 : 0), 0) +
67	        queryTokens.reduce((s, t)  => s + (haystack.includes(t) ? 1 : 0), 0)
68	      return { ...chunk, _score: score }
69	    })
70	    .filter(c => c._score > 0)
71	    .sort((a, b) => b._score - a._score)
72	    .slice(0, topK)
73	}
74	
75	// ── System prompt ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
76	function buildSystemPrompt(entity, chunks) {
77	  const chunkBlock = chunks.length
78	    ? chunks.map((c, i) => [
79	        `[DOC ${i + 1}] ${c.source ?? c.source_type ?? 'Unknown source'} | Year: ${c.year ?? '?'} | Page: ${c.page ?? '?'}`,
80	        c.url ? `URL: ${c.url}` : null,
81	        '',
82	        c.text ?? c.content ?? '',
83	      ].filter(x => x !== null).join('\n'))
84	      .join('\n\n' + '─'.repeat(60) + '\n\n')
85	    : 'No RAG corpus available for this session. Base your analysis on the entity signal data only and be explicit about this limitation.'
86	
87	  const signalLegend = [
88	    'cl1 = Unexplained Production Spike (USGS / EITI)',
89	    'cl2 = Regional Child Labour Prevalence (ILO ILOSTAT)',
90	    'cl3 = Low School Enrolment + Literacy (UNESCO)',

113	  return `You are REEtrieve Intelligence, an investigative AI embedded in the REEtrieve Compliance Platform. You specialise in critical mineral supply chains — specifically rare earth elements (REE), cobalt, nickel, tin, and tungsten.

115	Your audience is compliance officers, regulators, journalists, and auditors who need rigorous, cited, probabilistic analysis — not reassurance.
116	
117	${'═'.repeat(65)}
118	ENTITY UNDER REVIEW
119	${'═'.repeat(65)}
120	Name:            ${entity.name}
121	Country:         ${entity.flag ?? ''} ${entity.country}
122	Industry:        ${entity.industry}
123	Risk Score:      ${entity.riskScore}/100  (Bayesian composite, 4 dimensions)
124	Cert Status:     ${entity.certStatus ?? 'unknown'}
125	Last Audit:      ${entity.lastAudit ?? 'never'}
126	Risk Flags:      ${(entity.riskFlags ?? []).join(' | ') || 'none'}
127	Intel Note:      ${entity.note ?? ''}
128

130	${signalLines}

134	
135	${'═'.repeat(65)}
136	RELEVANT DOCUMENT EXCERPTS  (ranked by relevance to your query)
137	${'═'.repeat(65)}
138	${chunkBlock}

140	${'═'.repeat(65)}
141	ANALYTICAL RULES — APPLY TO EVERY RESPONSE
142	${'═'.repeat(65)}
143	1. CITATION REQUIRED: Every factual claim must end with its source in parentheses.
144	   Use exact names: (USGS MCS 2024), (UN Comtrade 2023), (ILO ILOSTAT 2022),
145	   (RMI 2024), (BHRRC TMT 2024), (Global Witness 2024), (BMI 2024),
146	   or (REEtrieve signal data) for values derived from signal activations above.
147	
148	2. TWO-SIDED ANALYSIS: After each risk finding, state the strongest
149	   counter-argument or benign alternative interpretation, then explain
150	   why you accept or reject it given the available evidence.
151	
152	3. RELATIVIZE SCORES: Interpret numbers in context.
153	   The 13-entity database range is roughly 38–95. Quartile breakpoints:
154	   ≥82 = top quartile (critical), 68–81 = high, 52–67 = elevated, <52 = lower.
155	   Always say where this entity sits.
156	
157	4. PROBABILISTIC FRAMING: Use "the evidence is consistent with...",
158	   "with high/moderate/low confidence...", "this cannot be confirmed but...".
159	   Never state certainties. Supply chains are inherently uncertain.
160	
161	5. MISSING DATA IS A SIGNAL: If a dataset doesn't cover this entity,
162	   name the gap explicitly and explain what inference is still possible.
163	   E.g., absence from GISTM disclosures after the 2025 mandatory deadline
164	   is itself non-compliance, not a neutral absence.
165	
166	6. TEMPORAL LAG: Materials extracted today appear in products in 2–3 years.
167	   A battery sold in 2025 contains ore from 2022–2023. Always anchor
168	   downstream product claims to the correct extraction window.
169	
170	7. DOWNSTREAM TRACING: Trace material flows step by step using the document
171	   corpus: mine → smelter → refiner → manufacturer → brand. State each
172	   inference link explicitly. Only name companies that appear in cited documents.
173	   If a company cannot be confirmed, say so rather than speculating.
174	
175	8. SCOPE GUARD: You cover REE, cobalt, nickel, tin, tungsten only.
176	   If asked about other minerals, note this is outside your current dataset.
177	
178	9. ADVERSARIAL LENS: Always ask — could this data have been falsified?

257	    } else {

259	      res.end()
260	    }
261	  }
262	})

47	  'the','and','for','that','this','with','are','from','into','what',
48	  'which','have','been','will','when','where','how','does','can','its',
49	  'their','about','used','also','over','more','than','such',
50	])
51	
52	function retrieveChunks(entityName, query, topK = 7) {
53	  if (!CHUNKS.length) return []
54	
55	  // Build token set: entity name tokens + meaningful query tokens
56	  const entityTokens = entityName.toLowerCase().split(/\W+/).filter(t => t.length > 2)
57	  const queryTokens  = query.toLowerCase().split(/\W+/)
58	    .filter(t => t.length > 3 && !STOPWORDS.has(t))
59	  const allTokens    = [...new Set([...entityTokens, ...queryTokens])]
60	
61	  return CHUNKS
62	    .map(chunk => {
63	      const haystack = (chunk.text || chunk.content || '').toLowerCase()
64	      // Entity tokens worth 2 pts each (high precision), query tokens 1 pt
65	      const score =
66	        entityTokens.reduce((s, t) => s + (haystack.includes(t) ? 2 : 0), 0) +
67	        queryTokens.reduce((s, t)  => s + (haystack.includes(t) ? 1 : 0), 0)
68	      return { ...chunk, _score: score }
69	    })
70	    .filter(c => c._score > 0)
71	    .sort((a, b) => b._score - a._score)
72	    .slice(0, topK)
73	}
