query: `importlib.metadata.version("pandas")` returns `None` — conda-installed pandas has no pip metadata. This is a conda/pip conflict that's hard to fix cleanly. Let me just rewrite the script to avoid `sentence-transformers` and its `datasets` dependency entirely:
original_content:
1	"""
2	Step 2 — Chunking & metadata tagging.
3	Converts all raw documents into data/chunks/chunks.jsonl
4	Uses multiprocessing to parallelize across files.
5	"""
6	
7	import json, pathlib, re, hashlib, sys
8	from multiprocessing import Pool, cpu_count
9	from typing import Iterator
10	
11	# ── config ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
12	CHUNK_SIZE  = 1_600
13	OVERLAP     = 200
14	MIN_CHUNK   = 120
15	WORKERS     = min(4, max(1, cpu_count() - 3))
16	
17	RAW_ROOT    = pathlib.Path(__file__).parent / "data/raw"
18	TEXT_ROOT   = pathlib.Path(__file__).parent / "data/text"
19	OUT_DIR     = pathlib.Path(__file__).parent / "data/chunks"
20	OUT_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
21	CHUNKS_FILE = OUT_DIR / "chunks.jsonl"
22	
23	# Pre-compiled regexes
24	_RE_NULL   = re.compile(r'\x00')
25	_RE_SPACES = re.compile(r'[ \t]{3,}')
26	_RE_LINES  = re.compile(r'\n{4,}')
27	_RE_HYPHEN = re.compile(r'([a-z])-\n([a-z])')
28	
29	
30	# ── helpers ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
31	
32	def tokens_approx(text: str) -> int:
33	    return max(1, len(text) // 4)
34	
35	def make_id(source_type: str, doc_name: str, page, chunk_idx: int) -> str:
36	    return f"{source_type}__{doc_name}__p{page}__c{chunk_idx}"
37	
38	def clean_text(text: str) -> str:
39	    text = _RE_NULL.sub('', text)
40	    text = _RE_SPACES.sub('  ', text)
41	    text = _RE_LINES.sub('\n\n\n', text)
42	    text = _RE_HYPHEN.sub(r'\1\2', text)
43	    return text.strip()
44	
45	def sliding_window(text: str) -> Iterator[str]:
46	    text = text.strip()
47	    if not text:
48	        return
49	    if len(text) <= CHUNK_SIZE:
50	        if len(text) >= MIN_CHUNK:
51	            yield text
52	        return
53	    start = 0
54	    while start < len(text):
55	        end   = start + CHUNK_SIZE
56	        chunk = text[start:end]
57	        if end < len(text):
58	            bp = max(chunk.rfind(". ", len(chunk) - 200),
59	                     chunk.rfind("\n",  len(chunk) - 200))
60	            if bp > len(chunk) // 2:
61	                chunk = chunk[:bp + 1]
62	        chunk = chunk.strip()
63	        if len(chunk) >= MIN_CHUNK:
64	            yield chunk
65	        start += len(chunk) - OVERLAP
66	
67	
68	# ── metadata builders ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
69	
70	def usgs_meta(p: pathlib.Path) -> dict:
71	    m    = re.search(r'(\d{4})', p.stem)
72	    year = int(m.group(1)) if m else None
73	    el   = next((e for e in ["lithium","cobalt","rare-earths","nickel",
74	                              "graphite","manganese","tungsten","tin"]
75	                 if e.replace("-","_") in p.stem or e in p.stem), None)
76	    label = f"USGS Mineral Commodity Summary {year}" + (f" — {el.title()}" if el else "")
77	    return {"source": label, "source_type": "usgs", "year": year, "element": el,
78	            "url": f"https://pubs.usgs.gov/periodicals/mcs{year}/mcs{year}.pdf",
79	            "doc_path": str(p.relative_to(RAW_ROOT))}
80	
81	def usgs_text_meta(p: pathlib.Path) -> dict:
82	    return usgs_meta(pathlib.Path(str(p).replace("data/text/", "data/raw/").replace(".txt", ".pdf")))
83	
84	def sec_meta(p: pathlib.Path) -> dict:
85	    company = p.parent.name
86	    return {"source": f"SEC Form SD — Conflict Minerals Disclosure ({company.upper()})",
87	            "source_type": "sec_edgar", "year": None, "company": company,
88	            "url": "https://www.sec.gov/cgi-bin/browse-edgar",
89	            "doc_path": str(p.relative_to(RAW_ROOT))}
90	
91	def comtrade_meta(p: pathlib.Path) -> dict:
92	    parts = p.stem.split("_")
93	    year  = int(parts[-1]) if parts[-1].isdigit() else None
94	    flow  = "Exports" if "X" in parts else "Imports"
95	    return {"source": f"UN Comtrade — HS {parts[0]} {flow} {year}",
96	            "source_type": "comtrade", "year": year, "hs_code": parts[0], "flow": flow,
97	            "url": "https://comtradeplus.un.org/",
98	            "doc_path": str(p.relative_to(RAW_ROOT))}
99	
100	def misc_meta(p: pathlib.Path) -> dict:
101	    raw_str = str(p).replace("/data/text/misc/", "/data/raw/misc/").replace(".txt", ".pdf")
102	    try:
103	        doc_path = str(pathlib.Path(raw_str).relative_to(RAW_ROOT))
104	    except ValueError:
105	        doc_path = p.name
106	    return {"source": f"External Report — {p.stem.replace('_',' ').title()}",
107	            "source_type": "misc", "year": None, "url": "", "doc_path": doc_path}
108	
109	
110	# ── worker (runs in subprocess) ───────────────────────────────────────────────
111	
112	def process_file(args: tuple) -> list[dict]:
113	    path_str, meta_fn_name, is_pdf = args
114	    path     = pathlib.Path(path_str)
115	    meta_fn  = globals()[meta_fn_name]
116	    meta     = meta_fn(path)
117	
118	    if is_pdf:
119	        # should not happen — all PDFs pre-extracted, but keep as safety net
120	        try:
121	            import pdfplumber
122	            chunks = []
123	            with pdfplumber.open(path) as pdf:
124	                for pg_num, pg in enumerate(pdf.pages, 1):
125	                    raw = pg.extract_text()
126	                    if not raw:
127	                        continue
128	                    text = clean_text(raw)
129	                    for idx, chunk in enumerate(sliding_window(text)):
130	                        chunks.append({**meta,
131	                            "id": make_id(meta["source_type"], path.stem, pg_num, idx),
132	                            "text": chunk, "page": pg_num, "chunk_index": idx,
133	                            "tokens_approx": tokens_approx(chunk)})
134	            return chunks
135	        except Exception as e:
136	            print(f"  ✗ PDF {path.name}: {e}", flush=True)
137	            return []
138	
139	    try:
140	        raw  = path.read_text(encoding="utf-8", errors="ignore")
141	        text = clean_text(raw)
142	        return [{**meta,
143	                 "id": make_id(meta["source_type"], path.stem, 0, idx),
144	                 "text": chunk, "page": None, "chunk_index": idx,
145	                 "tokens_approx": tokens_approx(chunk)}
146	                for idx, chunk in enumerate(sliding_window(text))]
147	    except Exception as e:
148	        print(f"  ✗ {path.name}: {e}", flush=True)
149	        return []
150	
151	
152	# ── file collection ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
153	
154	_misc_pre = {p.stem for p in (TEXT_ROOT/"misc").glob("*.txt")} \
155	            if (TEXT_ROOT/"misc").exists() else set()
156	
157	SOURCES = [
158	    (TEXT_ROOT/"usgs",        "*.txt",    False, "usgs_text_meta"),
159	    (TEXT_ROOT/"misc",        "*.txt",    False, "misc_meta"),
160	    (RAW_ROOT/"sec_edgar",    "**/*.txt", False, "sec_meta"),
161	    (RAW_ROOT/"comtrade_text","*.txt",    False, "comtrade_meta"),
162	    (RAW_ROOT/"misc",         "**/*.txt", False, "misc_meta"),
163	]
164	
165	def collect_files() -> list[tuple]:
166	    files = []
167	    seen  = set()
168	    for folder, glob, is_pdf, fn_name in SOURCES:
169	        if not folder.exists():
170	            continue
171	        for p in sorted(folder.glob(glob)):
172	            if not p.is_file() or p.name == "_manifest.json":
173	                continue
174	            key = p.stem
175	            if key in seen:          # skip raw/misc txt if already in text/misc
176	                continue
177	            seen.add(key)
178	            files.append((str(p), fn_name, is_pdf))
179	    # any misc PDFs not yet pre-extracted
180	    misc_pdf = RAW_ROOT / "misc"
181	    if misc_pdf.exists():
182	        for p in sorted(misc_pdf.glob("**/*.pdf")):
183	            if p.stem not in _misc_pre:
184	                files.append((str(p), "misc_meta", True))
185	    return files
186	
187	
188	# ── main ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
189	
190	def main():
191	    files = collect_files()
192	    n     = len(files)
193	    print(f"\n✓ {n} source files  •  {WORKERS} workers\n")
194	
195	    seen_hashes : set[str] = set()
196	    total_chunks = total_tokens = skipped = 0
197	
198	    with open(CHUNKS_FILE, "w", encoding="utf-8") as out, \
199	         Pool(processes=WORKERS) as pool:
200	
201	        for i, chunks in enumerate(pool.imap_unordered(process_file, files, chunksize=1), 1):
202	            name = pathlib.Path(files[i-1][0]).name if i <= n else "?"
203	            pct  = int(i / n * 100)
204	            print(f"  [{i:>3}/{n}] {pct:>3}%  +{len(chunks):>4} chunks  {name}", flush=True)
205	
206	            for chunk in chunks:
207	                h = hashlib.md5(chunk["text"].encode()).hexdigest()[:8]
208	                if h in seen_hashes:
209	                    skipped += 1
210	                    continue
211	                seen_hashes.add(h)
212	                out.write(json.dumps(chunk, ensure_ascii=False) + "\n")
213	                total_chunks += 1
214	                total_tokens += chunk["tokens_approx"]
215	
216	    # breakdown
217	    by_type: dict[str, int] = {}
218	    with open(CHUNKS_FILE, encoding="utf-8") as f:
219	        for line in f:

222	
223	    mx = max(by_type.values()) if by_type else 1
224	    print(f"\n{'═'*55}")

227	    print(f"  Duplicates     : {skipped:,}")
228	    print(f"\n  By source type:")
229	    for st, c in sorted(by_type.items(), key=lambda x: -x[1]):
230	        print(f"    {st:<18} {c:>6}  {'█' * (c // max(1, mx // 30))}")
231	    print(f"{'═'*55}")
232	    print(f"\n  ✓ Ready →  python 06_embed.py")
233	
234	
235	if __name__ == "__main__":
236	    main()

query: `importlib.metadata.version("pandas")` returns `None` — conda-installed pandas has no pip metadata. This is a conda/pip conflict that's hard to fix cleanly. Let me just rewrite the script to avoid `sentence-transformers` and its `datasets` dependency entirely:
original_content:
1	"""
2	Step 2 — Chunking & metadata tagging.
3	Converts all raw documents into data/chunks/chunks.jsonl
4	"""
5	
6	import json, pathlib, re, hashlib, sys
7	from typing import Iterator
8	
9	# ── config ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
10	CHUNK_SIZE  = 1_600
11	OVERLAP     = 200
12	MIN_CHUNK   = 120
13	
14	RAW_ROOT    = pathlib.Path(__file__).parent / "data/raw"
15	TEXT_ROOT   = pathlib.Path(__file__).parent / "data/text"
16	OUT_DIR     = pathlib.Path(__file__).parent / "data/chunks"
17	OUT_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
18	CHUNKS_FILE = OUT_DIR / "chunks.jsonl"
19	
20	# Pre-compiled regexes
21	_RE_NULL   = re.compile(r'\x00')
22	_RE_SPACES = re.compile(r'[ \t]{3,}')
23	_RE_LINES  = re.compile(r'\n{4,}')
24	_RE_HYPHEN = re.compile(r'([a-z])-\n([a-z])')
25	
26	
27	# ── helpers ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
28	
29	def tokens_approx(text: str) -> int:
30	    return max(1, len(text) // 4)
31	
32	def make_id(source_type: str, doc_name: str, page, chunk_idx: int) -> str:
33	    return f"{source_type}__{doc_name}__p{page}__c{chunk_idx}"
34	
35	def clean_text(text: str) -> str:
36	    text = _RE_NULL.sub('', text)
37	    text = _RE_SPACES.sub('  ', text)
38	    text = _RE_LINES.sub('\n\n\n', text)
39	    text = _RE_HYPHEN.sub(r'\1\2', text)
40	    return text.strip()
41	
42	def sliding_window(text: str) -> Iterator[str]:
43	    text = text.strip()
44	    if not text:
45	        return
46	    if len(text) <= CHUNK_SIZE:
47	        if len(text) >= MIN_CHUNK:
48	            yield text
49	        return
50	    start = 0
51	    while start < len(text):
52	        end   = start + CHUNK_SIZE
53	        chunk = text[start:end]
54	        if end < len(text):
55	            bp = max(chunk.rfind(". ", len(chunk) - 200),
56	                     chunk.rfind("\n",  len(chunk) - 200))
57	            if bp > len(chunk) // 2:
58	                chunk = chunk[:bp + 1]
59	        chunk = chunk.strip()
60	        if len(chunk) >= MIN_CHUNK:
61	            yield chunk
62	        start += len(chunk) - OVERLAP
63	
64	
65	# ── metadata builders ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
66	
67	def usgs_meta(p: pathlib.Path) -> dict:
68	    m    = re.search(r'(\d{4})', p.stem)
69	    year = int(m.group(1)) if m else None
70	    el   = next((e for e in ["lithium","cobalt","rare-earths","nickel",
71	                              "graphite","manganese","tungsten","tin"]
72	                 if e.replace("-","_") in p.stem or e in p.stem), None)
73	    label = f"USGS Mineral Commodity Summary {year}" + (f" — {el.title()}" if el else "")
74	    return {"source": label, "source_type": "usgs", "year": year, "element": el,
75	            "url": f"https://pubs.usgs.gov/periodicals/mcs{year}/mcs{year}.pdf",
76	            "doc_path": str(p.relative_to(RAW_ROOT))}
77	
78	def usgs_text_meta(p: pathlib.Path) -> dict:
79	    return usgs_meta(pathlib.Path(str(p).replace("data/text/", "data/raw/").replace(".txt", ".pdf")))
80	
81	def sec_meta(p: pathlib.Path) -> dict:
82	    company = p.parent.name
83	    return {"source": f"SEC Form SD — Conflict Minerals Disclosure ({company.upper()})",
84	            "source_type": "sec_edgar", "year": None, "company": company,
85	            "url": "https://www.sec.gov/cgi-bin/browse-edgar",
86	            "doc_path": str(p.relative_to(RAW_ROOT))}
87	
88	def comtrade_meta(p: pathlib.Path) -> dict:
89	    parts = p.stem.split("_")
90	    year  = int(parts[-1]) if parts[-1].isdigit() else None
91	    flow  = "Exports" if "X" in parts else "Imports"
92	    return {"source": f"UN Comtrade — HS {parts[0]} {flow} {year}",
93	            "source_type": "comtrade", "year": year, "hs_code": parts[0], "flow": flow,
94	            "url": "https://comtradeplus.un.org/",
95	            "doc_path": str(p.relative_to(RAW_ROOT))}
96	
97	def misc_meta(p: pathlib.Path) -> dict:
98	    raw_str = str(p).replace("/data/text/misc/", "/data/raw/misc/").replace(".txt", ".pdf")
99	    try:
100	        doc_path = str(pathlib.Path(raw_str).relative_to(RAW_ROOT))
101	    except ValueError:
102	        doc_path = p.name
103	    return {"source": f"External Report — {p.stem.replace('_',' ').title()}",
104	            "source_type": "misc", "year": None, "url": "", "doc_path": doc_path}
105	
106	
107	# ── worker (runs in subprocess) ───────────────────────────────────────────────
108	
109	def iter_file_chunks(args: tuple):
110	    path_str, meta_fn_name, is_pdf = args
111	    path    = pathlib.Path(path_str)
112	    meta_fn = globals()[meta_fn_name]
113	    meta    = meta_fn(path)
114	
115	    if is_pdf:
116	        try:
117	            import pdfplumber
118	            with pdfplumber.open(path) as pdf:
119	                for pg_num, pg in enumerate(pdf.pages, 1):
120	                    raw = pg.extract_text()
121	                    if not raw:
122	                        continue
123	                    text = clean_text(raw)
124	                    del raw
125	                    for idx, chunk in enumerate(sliding_window(text)):
126	                        yield {**meta,
127	                               "id": make_id(meta["source_type"], path.stem, pg_num, idx),
128	                               "text": chunk, "page": pg_num, "chunk_index": idx,
129	                               "tokens_approx": tokens_approx(chunk)}
130	        except Exception as e:
131	            print(f"  ✗ PDF {path.name}: {e}", flush=True)
132	        return
133	
134	    try:
135	        raw  = path.read_text(encoding="utf-8", errors="ignore")
136	        text = clean_text(raw)
137	        del raw
138	        for idx, chunk in enumerate(sliding_window(text)):
139	            yield {**meta,
140	                   "id": make_id(meta["source_type"], path.stem, 0, idx),
141	                   "text": chunk, "page": None, "chunk_index": idx,
142	                   "tokens_approx": tokens_approx(chunk)}
143	    except Exception as e:
144	        print(f"  ✗ {path.name}: {e}", flush=True)
145	
146	
147	# ── file collection ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
148	
149	_misc_pre = {p.stem for p in (TEXT_ROOT/"misc").glob("*.txt")} \
150	            if (TEXT_ROOT/"misc").exists() else set()
151	
152	SOURCES = [
153	    (TEXT_ROOT/"usgs",        "*.txt",    False, "usgs_text_meta"),
154	    (TEXT_ROOT/"misc",        "*.txt",    False, "misc_meta"),
155	    (RAW_ROOT/"sec_edgar",    "**/*.txt", False, "sec_meta"),
156	    (RAW_ROOT/"comtrade_text","*.txt",    False, "comtrade_meta"),
157	    (RAW_ROOT/"misc",         "**/*.txt", False, "misc_meta"),
158	]
159	
160	def collect_files() -> list[tuple]:
161	    files = []
162	    seen  = set()
163	    for folder, glob, is_pdf, fn_name in SOURCES:
164	        if not folder.exists():
165	            continue
166	        for p in sorted(folder.glob(glob)):
167	            if not p.is_file() or p.name == "_manifest.json":
168	                continue
169	            key = p.stem
170	            if key in seen:          # skip raw/misc txt if already in text/misc
171	                continue
172	            seen.add(key)
173	            files.append((str(p), fn_name, is_pdf))
174	    # any misc PDFs not yet pre-extracted
175	    misc_pdf = RAW_ROOT / "misc"
176	    if misc_pdf.exists():
177	        for p in sorted(misc_pdf.glob("**/*.pdf")):
178	            if p.stem not in _misc_pre:
179	                files.append((str(p), "misc_meta", True))
180	    return files
181	
182	
183	# ── main ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
184	
185	def main():
186	    print("Collecting files...", flush=True)
187	    files = collect_files()
188	    n     = len(files)
189	    print(f"✓ {n} source files\n", flush=True)
190	
191	    seen_hashes : set[str] = set()
192	    total_chunks = total_tokens = skipped = 0
193	
194	    with open(CHUNKS_FILE, "w", encoding="utf-8") as out:
195	        for i, f in enumerate(files, 1):
196	            name = pathlib.Path(f[0]).name
197	            pct  = int(i / n * 100)
198	            file_chunks = 0
199	            for chunk in iter_file_chunks(f):
200	                h = hashlib.md5(chunk["text"].encode()).hexdigest()[:8]
201	                if h in seen_hashes:
202	                    skipped += 1
203	                    continue
204	                seen_hashes.add(h)
205	                out.write(json.dumps(chunk, ensure_ascii=False) + "\n")
206	                total_chunks += 1
207	                total_tokens += chunk["tokens_approx"]
208	                file_chunks += 1
209	            print(f"  [{i:>3}/{n}] {pct:>3}%  +{file_chunks:>4} chunks  {name}", flush=True)
210	
211	    # breakdown
212	    by_type: dict[str, int] = {}
213	    with open(CHUNKS_FILE, encoding="utf-8") as f:
214	        for line in f:
215	            st = json.loads(line).get("source_type", "?")
216	            by_type[st] = by_type.get(st, 0) + 1
217	
218	    mx = max(by_type.values()) if by_type else 1
219	    print(f"\n{'═'*55}")
220	    print(f"  Chunks written : {total_chunks:,}  →  {CHUNKS_FILE}")
221	    print(f"  Approx tokens  : {total_tokens:,}  (~{total_tokens//1000}k)")
222	    print(f"  Duplicates     : {skipped:,}")
223	    print(f"\n  By source type:")
224	    for st, c in sorted(by_type.items(), key=lambda x: -x[1]):
225	        print(f"    {st:<18} {c:>6}  {'█' * (c // max(1, mx // 30))}")
226	    print(f"{'═'*55}")
227	    print(f"\n  ✓ Ready →  python 06_embed.py")
228	
229	
230	if __name__ == "__main__":
231	    main()
232

1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
2	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
3	#  REEtrieve RAG — Step 1 & 2 pipeline runner
4	#
5	#  Usage:
6	#    chmod +x run_pipeline.sh
7	#    ./run_pipeline.sh
8	#
9	#  Or run individual steps:
10	#    python 01_scrape_usgs.py
11	#    python 02_scrape_comtrade.py
12	#    python 03_scrape_oecd_sec.py
13	#    python 04_inventory.py        ← quality check (run after all scrapers)
14	#    python 05_chunk.py            ← produces data/chunks/chunks.jsonl
15	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
16	
17	set -e
18	cd "$(dirname "$0")"
19	
20	echo ""
21	echo "═══════════════════════════════════════════════════"
22	echo "  REEtrieve RAG Pipeline — Steps 1 & 2"
23	echo "═══════════════════════════════════════════════════"
24	
25	# Check deps
26	python -c "import pdfplumber, requests, tqdm" 2>/dev/null || {
27	    echo "  Installing dependencies..."
28	    pip install -r requirements.txt -q
29	}
30	
31	echo ""
32	echo "── Step 1a: USGS ──────────────────────────────────"
33	python 01_scrape_usgs.py
34	
35	echo ""
36	echo "── Step 1b: UN Comtrade ───────────────────────────"
37	python 02_scrape_comtrade.py
38	
39	echo ""
40	echo "── Step 1c: OECD + SEC EDGAR ──────────────────────"
41	python 03_scrape_oecd_sec.py
42	
43	echo ""
44	echo "── Step 1d: Inventory & QA ────────────────────────"
45	python 04_inventory.py
46	
47	echo ""
48	echo "── Step 1e: OCR scan PDFs ─────────────────────────"
49	python 06_ocr_pdfs.py
50	
51	echo ""
52	echo "── Step 2: Chunk & tag ────────────────────────────"
53	python 05_chunk.py
54	
55	echo ""
56	echo "═══════════════════════════════════════════════════"
57	echo "  Pipeline complete."
58	echo "  Next: python 07_embed.py   (Step 3 — embeddings)"
59	echo "═══════════════════════════════════════════════════"
60

1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
2	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
3	#  REEtrieve RAG — Step 1 & 2 pipeline runner
4	#
5	#  Usage:
6	#    chmod +x run_pipeline.sh
7	#    ./run_pipeline.sh
8	#
9	#  Or run individual steps:
10	#    python 01_scrape_usgs.py
11	#    python 02_scrape_comtrade.py
12	#    python 03_scrape_oecd_sec.py
13	#    python 04_inventory.py        ← quality check (run after all scrapers)
14	#    python 05_chunk.py            ← produces data/chunks/chunks.jsonl
15	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
16	
17	set -e
18	cd "$(dirname "$0")"
19	
20	echo ""
21	echo "═══════════════════════════════════════════════════"
22	echo "  REEtrieve RAG Pipeline — Steps 1 & 2"
23	echo "═══════════════════════════════════════════════════"
24	
25	# Check deps
26	python -c "import pdfplumber, requests, tqdm" 2>/dev/null || {
27	    echo "  Installing dependencies..."
28	    pip install -r requirements.txt -q
29	}
30	
31	echo ""
32	echo "── Step 1a: USGS ──────────────────────────────────"
33	python 01_scrape_usgs.py
34	
35	echo ""
36	echo "── Step 1b: UN Comtrade ───────────────────────────"
37	python 02_scrape_comtrade.py
38	
39	echo ""
40	echo "── Step 1c: OECD + SEC EDGAR ──────────────────────"
41	python 03_scrape_oecd_sec.py
42	
43	echo ""
44	echo "── Step 1d: Inventory & QA ────────────────────────"
45	python 04_inventory.py
46	
47	echo ""
48	echo "── Step 1e: OCR scan PDFs ─────────────────────────"
49	python 06_ocr_pdfs.py
50	
51	echo ""
52	echo "── Step 2: Chunk & tag ────────────────────────────"
53	python 05_chunk.py
54	
55	echo ""
56	echo "═══════════════════════════════════════════════════"
57	echo "  Pipeline complete."
58	echo "  Next: python 07_embed.py   (Step 3 — embeddings)"
59	echo "═══════════════════════════════════════════════════"

query: `importlib.metadata.version("pandas")` returns `None` — conda-installed pandas has no pip metadata. This is a conda/pip conflict that's hard to fix cleanly. Let me just rewrite the script to avoid `sentence-transformers` and its `datasets` dependency entirely:
original_content:
1	"""
2	Step 3 — Embedding.
3	Reads data/chunks/chunks.jsonl, embeds each chunk with nomic-embed-text-v1.5 on MPS,
4	and writes a FAISS index + metadata to data/embeddings/.
5	"""
6	
7	import json, pathlib, sys
8	import numpy as np
9	
10	# ── config ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
11	CHUNKS_FILE = pathlib.Path(__file__).parent / "data/chunks/chunks.jsonl"
12	OUT_DIR     = pathlib.Path(__file__).parent / "data/embeddings"
13	OUT_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
14	INDEX_FILE  = OUT_DIR / "index.faiss"
15	META_FILE   = OUT_DIR / "metadata.jsonl"
16	
17	MODEL_NAME  = "nomic-ai/nomic-embed-text-v1.5"
18	BATCH_SIZE  = 64
19	DOC_PREFIX  = "search_document: "
20	
21	
22	def main():
23	    try:
24	        from sentence_transformers import SentenceTransformer
25	        import faiss
26	        import torch
27	    except ImportError:
28	        print("Missing deps. Run:  pip install sentence-transformers faiss-cpu torch")
29	        sys.exit(1)
30	
31	    # ── device ──
32	    if torch.backends.mps.is_available():
33	        device = "mps"
34	    elif torch.cuda.is_available():
35	        device = "cuda"
36	    else:
37	        device = "cpu"
38	    print(f"Device: {device}\n")
39	
40	    # ── load chunks ──
41	    if not CHUNKS_FILE.exists():
42	        print(f"✗ {CHUNKS_FILE} not found — run 05_chunk.py first")
43	        sys.exit(1)
44	
45	    print("Loading chunks...", flush=True)
46	    chunks = []
47	    with open(CHUNKS_FILE, encoding="utf-8") as f:
48	        for line in f:
49	            line = line.strip()
50	            if line:
51	                chunks.append(json.loads(line))
52	    n = len(chunks)
53	    print(f"✓ {n} chunks\n", flush=True)
54	
55	    # ── load model ──
56	    print(f"Loading {MODEL_NAME}...", flush=True)
57	    model = SentenceTransformer(MODEL_NAME, device=device, trust_remote_code=True)
58	
59	    # ── embed ──
60	    texts = [DOC_PREFIX + c["text"] for c in chunks]
61	    print(f"Embedding {n} chunks  •  batch={BATCH_SIZE}  •  device={device}\n", flush=True)
62	
63	    try:
64	        from tqdm import tqdm
65	        show_progress = True
66	    except ImportError:
67	        show_progress = False
68	
69	    embeddings = model.encode(
70	        texts,
71	        batch_size=BATCH_SIZE,
72	        normalize_embeddings=True,
73	        show_progress_bar=show_progress,
74	        convert_to_numpy=True,
75	    )
76	
77	    # ── FAISS index ──
78	    dim   = embeddings.shape[1]
79	    index = faiss.IndexFlatIP(dim)   # inner product on L2-normed vecs = cosine similarity
80	    index.add(embeddings.astype(np.float32))
81	    faiss.write_index(index, str(INDEX_FILE))
82	    print(f"\n✓ FAISS index  → {INDEX_FILE}  ({dim}-dim, {index.ntotal} vectors)")
83	
84	    # ── metadata ──
85	    with open(META_FILE, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
86	        for chunk in chunks:
87	            f.write(json.dumps(chunk, ensure_ascii=False) + "\n")
88	    print(f"✓ Metadata     → {META_FILE}")
89	
90	    print(f"\nTo query: embed with prefix 'search_query: ', then index.search(vec, k)")
91	
92	
93	if __name__ == "__main__":
94	    main()
95

query: `importlib.metadata.version("pandas")` returns `None` — conda-installed pandas has no pip metadata. This is a conda/pip conflict that's hard to fix cleanly. Let me just rewrite the script to avoid `sentence-transformers` and its `datasets` dependency entirely:
original_content:
1	"""
2	Step 2 — Chunking & metadata tagging.
3	Converts all raw documents into data/chunks/chunks.jsonl
4	Uses multiprocessing to parallelize across files.
5	"""
6	
7	import json, pathlib, re, hashlib, sys
8	from multiprocessing import Pool, cpu_count
9	from typing import Iterator
10	
11	# ── config ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
12	CHUNK_SIZE  = 1_600
13	OVERLAP     = 200
14	MIN_CHUNK   = 120
15	WORKERS     = min(4, max(1, cpu_count() - 3))
16	
17	RAW_ROOT    = pathlib.Path(__file__).parent / "data/raw"
18	TEXT_ROOT   = pathlib.Path(__file__).parent / "data/text"
19	OUT_DIR     = pathlib.Path(__file__).parent / "data/chunks"
20	OUT_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
21	CHUNKS_FILE = OUT_DIR / "chunks.jsonl"
22	
23	# Pre-compiled regexes
24	_RE_NULL   = re.compile(r'\x00')
25	_RE_SPACES = re.compile(r'[ \t]{3,}')
26	_RE_LINES  = re.compile(r'\n{4,}')
27	_RE_HYPHEN = re.compile(r'([a-z])-\n([a-z])')
28	
29	
30	# ── helpers ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
31	
32	def tokens_approx(text: str) -> int:
33	    return max(1, len(text) // 4)
34	
35	def make_id(source_type: str, doc_name: str, page, chunk_idx: int) -> str:
36	    return f"{source_type}__{doc_name}__p{page}__c{chunk_idx}"
37	
38	def clean_text(text: str) -> str:
39	    text = _RE_NULL.sub('', text)
40	    text = _RE_SPACES.sub('  ', text)
41	    text = _RE_LINES.sub('\n\n\n', text)
42	    text = _RE_HYPHEN.sub(r'\1\2', text)
43	    return text.strip()
44	
45	def sliding_window(text: str) -> Iterator[str]:
46	    text = text.strip()
47	    if not text:
48	        return
49	    if len(text) <= CHUNK_SIZE:
50	        if len(text) >= MIN_CHUNK:
51	            yield text
52	        return
53	    start = 0
54	    while start < len(text):
55	        end   = start + CHUNK_SIZE
56	        chunk = text[start:end]
57	        if end < len(text):
58	            bp = max(chunk.rfind(". ", len(chunk) - 200),
59	                     chunk.rfind("\n",  len(chunk) - 200))
60	            if bp > len(chunk) // 2:
61	                chunk = chunk[:bp + 1]
62	        chunk = chunk.strip()
63	        if len(chunk) >= MIN_CHUNK:
64	            yield chunk
65	        start += len(chunk) - OVERLAP
66	
67	
68	# ── metadata builders ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
69	
70	def usgs_meta(p: pathlib.Path) -> dict:
71	    m    = re.search(r'(\d{4})', p.stem)
72	    year = int(m.group(1)) if m else None
73	    el   = next((e for e in ["lithium","cobalt","rare-earths","nickel",
74	                              "graphite","manganese","tungsten","tin"]
75	                 if e.replace("-","_") in p.stem or e in p.stem), None)
76	    label = f"USGS Mineral Commodity Summary {year}" + (f" — {el.title()}" if el else "")
77	    return {"source": label, "source_type": "usgs", "year": year, "element": el,
78	            "url": f"https://pubs.usgs.gov/periodicals/mcs{year}/mcs{year}.pdf",
79	            "doc_path": str(p.relative_to(RAW_ROOT))}
80	
81	def usgs_text_meta(p: pathlib.Path) -> dict:
82	    return usgs_meta(pathlib.Path(str(p).replace("data/text/", "data/raw/").replace(".txt", ".pdf")))
83	
84	def sec_meta(p: pathlib.Path) -> dict:
85	    company = p.parent.name
86	    return {"source": f"SEC Form SD — Conflict Minerals Disclosure ({company.upper()})",
87	            "source_type": "sec_edgar", "year": None, "company": company,
88	            "url": "https://www.sec.gov/cgi-bin/browse-edgar",
89	            "doc_path": str(p.relative_to(RAW_ROOT))}
90	
91	def comtrade_meta(p: pathlib.Path) -> dict:
92	    parts = p.stem.split("_")
93	    year  = int(parts[-1]) if parts[-1].isdigit() else None
94	    flow  = "Exports" if "X" in parts else "Imports"
95	    return {"source": f"UN Comtrade — HS {parts[0]} {flow} {year}",
96	            "source_type": "comtrade", "year": year, "hs_code": parts[0], "flow": flow,
97	            "url": "https://comtradeplus.un.org/",
98	            "doc_path": str(p.relative_to(RAW_ROOT))}
99	
100	def misc_meta(p: pathlib.Path) -> dict:
101	    raw_str = str(p).replace("/data/text/misc/", "/data/raw/misc/").replace(".txt", ".pdf")
102	    try:
103	        doc_path = str(pathlib.Path(raw_str).relative_to(RAW_ROOT))
104	    except ValueError:
105	        doc_path = p.name
106	    return {"source": f"External Report — {p.stem.replace('_',' ').title()}",
107	            "source_type": "misc", "year": None, "url": "", "doc_path": doc_path}
108	
109	
110	# ── worker (runs in subprocess) ───────────────────────────────────────────────
111	
112	def process_file(args: tuple) -> list[dict]:
113	    path_str, meta_fn_name, is_pdf = args
114	    path     = pathlib.Path(path_str)
115	    meta_fn  = globals()[meta_fn_name]
116	    meta     = meta_fn(path)
117	
118	    if is_pdf:
119	        # should not happen — all PDFs pre-extracted, but keep as safety net
120	        try:
121	            import pdfplumber
122	            chunks = []
123	            with pdfplumber.open(path) as pdf:
124	                for pg_num, pg in enumerate(pdf.pages, 1):
125	                    raw = pg.extract_text()
126	                    if not raw:
127	                        continue
128	                    text = clean_text(raw)
129	                    for idx, chunk in enumerate(sliding_window(text)):
130	                        chunks.append({**meta,
131	                            "id": make_id(meta["source_type"], path.stem, pg_num, idx),
132	                            "text": chunk, "page": pg_num, "chunk_index": idx,
133	                            "tokens_approx": tokens_approx(chunk)})
134	            return chunks
135	        except Exception as e:
136	            print(f"  ✗ PDF {path.name}: {e}", flush=True)
137	            return []
138	
139	    try:
140	        raw  = path.read_text(encoding="utf-8", errors="ignore")
141	        text = clean_text(raw)
142	        return [{**meta,
143	                 "id": make_id(meta["source_type"], path.stem, 0, idx),
144	                 "text": chunk, "page": None, "chunk_index": idx,
145	                 "tokens_approx": tokens_approx(chunk)}
146	                for idx, chunk in enumerate(sliding_window(text))]
147	    except Exception as e:
148	        print(f"  ✗ {path.name}: {e}", flush=True)
149	        return []
150	
151	
152	# ── file collection ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
153	
154	_misc_pre = {p.stem for p in (TEXT_ROOT/"misc").glob("*.txt")} \
155	            if (TEXT_ROOT/"misc").exists() else set()
156	
157	SOURCES = [
158	    (TEXT_ROOT/"usgs",        "*.txt",    False, "usgs_text_meta"),
159	    (TEXT_ROOT/"misc",        "*.txt",    False, "misc_meta"),
160	    (RAW_ROOT/"sec_edgar",    "**/*.txt", False, "sec_meta"),
161	    (RAW_ROOT/"comtrade_text","*.txt",    False, "comtrade_meta"),
162	    (RAW_ROOT/"misc",         "**/*.txt", False, "misc_meta"),
163	]
164	
165	def collect_files() -> list[tuple]:
166	    files = []
167	    seen  = set()
168	    for folder, glob, is_pdf, fn_name in SOURCES:
169	        if not folder.exists():
170	            continue
171	        for p in sorted(folder.glob(glob)):
172	            if not p.is_file() or p.name == "_manifest.json":
173	                continue
174	            key = p.stem
175	            if key in seen:          # skip raw/misc txt if already in text/misc
176	                continue
177	            seen.add(key)
178	            files.append((str(p), fn_name, is_pdf))
179	    # any misc PDFs not yet pre-extracted
180	    misc_pdf = RAW_ROOT / "misc"
181	    if misc_pdf.exists():
182	        for p in sorted(misc_pdf.glob("**/*.pdf")):
183	            if p.stem not in _misc_pre:
184	                files.append((str(p), "misc_meta", True))
185	    return files
186	
187	
188	# ── main ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
189	
190	def main():
191	    files = collect_files()
192	    n     = len(files)
193	    print(f"\n✓ {n} source files  •  {WORKERS} workers\n")
194	
195	    seen_hashes : set[str] = set()
196	    total_chunks = total_tokens = skipped = 0
197	
198	    with open(CHUNKS_FILE, "w", encoding="utf-8") as out, \
199	         Pool(processes=WORKERS) as pool:
200	
201	        for i, chunks in enumerate(pool.imap_unordered(process_file, files, chunksize=1), 1):
202	            name = pathlib.Path(files[i-1][0]).name if i <= n else "?"
203	            pct  = int(i / n * 100)
204	            print(f"  [{i:>3}/{n}] {pct:>3}%  +{len(chunks):>4} chunks  {name}", flush=True)
205	
206	            for chunk in chunks:
207	                h = hashlib.md5(chunk["text"].encode()).hexdigest()[:8]
208	                if h in seen_hashes:
209	                    skipped += 1
210	                    continue
211	                seen_hashes.add(h)
212	                out.write(json.dumps(chunk, ensure_ascii=False) + "\n")
213	                total_chunks += 1
214	                total_tokens += chunk["tokens_approx"]
215	
216	    # breakdown
217	    by_type: dict[str, int] = {}
218	    with open(CHUNKS_FILE, encoding="utf-8") as f:
219	        for line in f:
220	            st = json.loads(line).get("source_type", "?")
221	            by_type[st] = by_type.get(st, 0) + 1
222	
223	    mx = max(by_type.values()) if by_type else 1
224	    print(f"\n{'═'*55}")
225	    print(f"  Chunks written : {total_chunks:,}  →  {CHUNKS_FILE}")
226	    print(f"  Approx tokens  : {total_tokens:,}  (~{total_tokens//1000}k)")
227	    print(f"  Duplicates     : {skipped:,}")
228	    print(f"\n  By source type:")
229	    for st, c in sorted(by_type.items(), key=lambda x: -x[1]):
230	        print(f"    {st:<18} {c:>6}  {'█' * (c // max(1, mx // 30))}")
231	    print(f"{'═'*55}")
232	    print(f"\n  ✓ Ready →  python 06_embed.py")
233	
234	
235	if __name__ == "__main__":
236	    main()
237

1	"""
2	Step 2 — Chunking & metadata tagging.
3	Converts all raw documents into data/chunks/chunks.jsonl
4	"""
5	
6	import json, pathlib, re, hashlib, sys
7	from typing import Iterator
8	
9	# ── config ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
10	CHUNK_SIZE  = 1_600
11	OVERLAP     = 200
12	MIN_CHUNK   = 120
13	
14	RAW_ROOT    = pathlib.Path(__file__).parent / "data/raw"
15	TEXT_ROOT   = pathlib.Path(__file__).parent / "data/text"
16	OUT_DIR     = pathlib.Path(__file__).parent / "data/chunks"
17	OUT_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
18	CHUNKS_FILE = OUT_DIR / "chunks.jsonl"
19	
20	# Pre-compiled regexes
21	_RE_NULL   = re.compile(r'\x00')
22	_RE_SPACES = re.compile(r'[ \t]{3,}')
23	_RE_LINES  = re.compile(r'\n{4,}')
24	_RE_HYPHEN = re.compile(r'([a-z])-\n([a-z])')
25	
26	
27	# ── helpers ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
28	
29	def tokens_approx(text: str) -> int:
30	    return max(1, len(text) // 4)
31	
32	def make_id(source_type: str, doc_name: str, page, chunk_idx: int) -> str:
33	    return f"{source_type}__{doc_name}__p{page}__c{chunk_idx}"
34	
35	def clean_text(text: str) -> str:
36	    text = _RE_NULL.sub('', text)
37	    text = _RE_SPACES.sub('  ', text)
38	    text = _RE_LINES.sub('\n\n\n', text)
39	    text = _RE_HYPHEN.sub(r'\1\2', text)
40	    return text.strip()
41	
42	def sliding_window(text: str) -> Iterator[str]:
43	    text = text.strip()
44	    if not text:
45	        return
46	    if len(text) <= CHUNK_SIZE:
47	        if len(text) >= MIN_CHUNK:
48	            yield text
49	        return
50	    start = 0
51	    while start < len(text):
52	        end   = start + CHUNK_SIZE
53	        chunk = text[start:end]
54	        if end < len(text):
55	            bp = max(chunk.rfind(". ", len(chunk) - 200),
56	                     chunk.rfind("\n",  len(chunk) - 200))
57	            if bp > len(chunk) // 2:
58	                chunk = chunk[:bp + 1]
59	        chunk = chunk.strip()
60	        if len(chunk) >= MIN_CHUNK:
61	            yield chunk
62	        start += len(chunk) - OVERLAP
63	
64	
65	# ── metadata builders ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
66	
67	def usgs_meta(p: pathlib.Path) -> dict:
68	    m    = re.search(r'(\d{4})', p.stem)
69	    year = int(m.group(1)) if m else None
70	    el   = next((e for e in ["lithium","cobalt","rare-earths","nickel",
71	                              "graphite","manganese","tungsten","tin"]
72	                 if e.replace("-","_") in p.stem or e in p.stem), None)
73	    label = f"USGS Mineral Commodity Summary {year}" + (f" — {el.title()}" if el else "")
74	    return {"source": label, "source_type": "usgs", "year": year, "element": el,
75	            "url": f"https://pubs.usgs.gov/periodicals/mcs{year}/mcs{year}.pdf",
76	            "doc_path": str(p.relative_to(RAW_ROOT))}
77	
78	def usgs_text_meta(p: pathlib.Path) -> dict:
79	    return usgs_meta(pathlib.Path(str(p).replace("data/text/", "data/raw/").replace(".txt", ".pdf")))
80	
81	def sec_meta(p: pathlib.Path) -> dict:
82	    company = p.parent.name
83	    return {"source": f"SEC Form SD — Conflict Minerals Disclosure ({company.upper()})",
84	            "source_type": "sec_edgar", "year": None, "company": company,
85	            "url": "https://www.sec.gov/cgi-bin/browse-edgar",
86	            "doc_path": str(p.relative_to(RAW_ROOT))}
87	
88	def comtrade_meta(p: pathlib.Path) -> dict:
89	    parts = p.stem.split("_")
90	    year  = int(parts[-1]) if parts[-1].isdigit() else None
91	    flow  = "Exports" if "X" in parts else "Imports"
92	    return {"source": f"UN Comtrade — HS {parts[0]} {flow} {year}",
93	            "source_type": "comtrade", "year": year, "hs_code": parts[0], "flow": flow,
94	            "url": "https://comtradeplus.un.org/",
95	            "doc_path": str(p.relative_to(RAW_ROOT))}
96	
97	def misc_meta(p: pathlib.Path) -> dict:
98	    raw_str = str(p).replace("/data/text/misc/", "/data/raw/misc/").replace(".txt", ".pdf")
99	    try:
100	        doc_path = str(pathlib.Path(raw_str).relative_to(RAW_ROOT))
101	    except ValueError:
102	        doc_path = p.name
103	    return {"source": f"External Report — {p.stem.replace('_',' ').title()}",
104	            "source_type": "misc", "year": None, "url": "", "doc_path": doc_path}
105	
106	
107	# ── worker (runs in subprocess) ───────────────────────────────────────────────
108	
109	def iter_file_chunks(args: tuple):
110	    path_str, meta_fn_name, is_pdf = args
111	    path    = pathlib.Path(path_str)
112	    meta_fn = globals()[meta_fn_name]
113	    meta    = meta_fn(path)
114	
115	    if is_pdf:
116	        try:
117	            import pdfplumber
118	            with pdfplumber.open(path) as pdf:
119	                for pg_num, pg in enumerate(pdf.pages, 1):
120	                    raw = pg.extract_text()
121	                    if not raw:
122	                        continue
123	                    text = clean_text(raw)
124	                    del raw
125	                    for idx, chunk in enumerate(sliding_window(text)):
126	                        yield {**meta,
127	                               "id": make_id(meta["source_type"], path.stem, pg_num, idx),
128	                               "text": chunk, "page": pg_num, "chunk_index": idx,
129	                               "tokens_approx": tokens_approx(chunk)}
130	        except Exception as e:
131	            print(f"  ✗ PDF {path.name}: {e}", flush=True)
132	        return
133	
134	    try:
135	        raw  = path.read_text(encoding="utf-8", errors="ignore")
136	        text = clean_text(raw)
137	        del raw
138	        for idx, chunk in enumerate(sliding_window(text)):
139	            yield {**meta,
140	                   "id": make_id(meta["source_type"], path.stem, 0, idx),
141	                   "text": chunk, "page": None, "chunk_index": idx,
142	                   "tokens_approx": tokens_approx(chunk)}
143	    except Exception as e:
144	        print(f"  ✗ {path.name}: {e}", flush=True)
145	
146	
147	# ── file collection ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
148	
149	_misc_pre = {p.stem for p in (TEXT_ROOT/"misc").glob("*.txt")} \
150	            if (TEXT_ROOT/"misc").exists() else set()
151	
152	SOURCES = [
153	    (TEXT_ROOT/"usgs",        "*.txt",    False, "usgs_text_meta"),
154	    (TEXT_ROOT/"misc",        "*.txt",    False, "misc_meta"),
155	    (RAW_ROOT/"sec_edgar",    "**/*.txt", False, "sec_meta"),
156	    (RAW_ROOT/"comtrade_text","*.txt",    False, "comtrade_meta"),
157	    (RAW_ROOT/"misc",         "**/*.txt", False, "misc_meta"),
158	]
159	
160	def collect_files() -> list[tuple]:
161	    files = []
162	    seen  = set()
163	    for folder, glob, is_pdf, fn_name in SOURCES:
164	        if not folder.exists():
165	            continue
166	        for p in sorted(folder.glob(glob)):
167	            if not p.is_file() or p.name == "_manifest.json":
168	                continue
169	            key = p.stem
170	            if key in seen:          # skip raw/misc txt if already in text/misc
171	                continue
172	            seen.add(key)
173	            files.append((str(p), fn_name, is_pdf))
174	    # any misc PDFs not yet pre-extracted
175	    misc_pdf = RAW_ROOT / "misc"
176	    if misc_pdf.exists():
177	        for p in sorted(misc_pdf.glob("**/*.pdf")):
178	            if p.stem not in _misc_pre:
179	                files.append((str(p), "misc_meta", True))
180	    return files
181	
182	
183	# ── main ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
184	
185	def main():
186	    print("Collecting files...", flush=True)
187	    files = collect_files()
188	    n     = len(files)
189	    print(f"✓ {n} source files\n", flush=True)
190	
191	    seen_hashes : set[str] = set()
192	    total_chunks = total_tokens = skipped = 0
193	
194	    with open(CHUNKS_FILE, "w", encoding="utf-8") as out:
195	        for i, f in enumerate(files, 1):
196	            name = pathlib.Path(f[0]).name
197	            pct  = int(i / n * 100)
198	            file_chunks = 0
199	            for chunk in iter_file_chunks(f):
200	                h = hashlib.md5(chunk["text"].encode()).hexdigest()[:8]
201	                if h in seen_hashes:
202	                    skipped += 1
203	                    continue
204	                seen_hashes.add(h)
205	                out.write(json.dumps(chunk, ensure_ascii=False) + "\n")
206	                total_chunks += 1
207	                total_tokens += chunk["tokens_approx"]
208	                file_chunks += 1
209	            print(f"  [{i:>3}/{n}] {pct:>3}%  +{file_chunks:>4} chunks  {name}", flush=True)
210	
211	    # breakdown
212	    by_type: dict[str, int] = {}
213	    with open(CHUNKS_FILE, encoding="utf-8") as f:
214	        for line in f:
215	            st = json.loads(line).get("source_type", "?")
216	            by_type[st] = by_type.get(st, 0) + 1
217	
218	    mx = max(by_type.values()) if by_type else 1
219	    print(f"\n{'═'*55}")
220	    print(f"  Chunks written : {total_chunks:,}  →  {CHUNKS_FILE}")
221	    print(f"  Approx tokens  : {total_tokens:,}  (~{total_tokens//1000}k)")
222	    print(f"  Duplicates     : {skipped:,}")
223	    print(f"\n  By source type:")
224	    for st, c in sorted(by_type.items(), key=lambda x: -x[1]):
225	        print(f"    {st:<18} {c:>6}  {'█' * (c // max(1, mx // 30))}")
226	    print(f"{'═'*55}")
227	    print(f"\n  ✓ Ready →  python 06_embed.py")
228	
229	
230	if __name__ == "__main__":
231	    main()
232

1	"""
2	Step 3 — Embedding.
3	Reads data/chunks/chunks.jsonl, embeds each chunk with nomic-embed-text-v1.5 on MPS,
4	and writes a FAISS index + metadata to data/embeddings/.
5	"""
6	
7	import json, pathlib, sys
8	import numpy as np
9	
10	# ── config ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
11	CHUNKS_FILE = pathlib.Path(__file__).parent / "data/chunks/chunks.jsonl"
12	OUT_DIR     = pathlib.Path(__file__).parent / "data/embeddings"
13	OUT_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
14	INDEX_FILE  = OUT_DIR / "index.faiss"
15	META_FILE   = OUT_DIR / "metadata.jsonl"
16	
17	MODEL_NAME  = "nomic-ai/nomic-embed-text-v1.5"
18	BATCH_SIZE  = 64
19	DOC_PREFIX  = "search_document: "
20	
21	
22	def main():
23	    try:
24	        from sentence_transformers import SentenceTransformer
25	        import faiss
26	        import torch
27	    except ImportError:
28	        print("Missing deps. Run:  pip install sentence-transformers faiss-cpu torch")
29	        sys.exit(1)
30	
31	    # ── device ──
32	    if torch.backends.mps.is_available():
33	        device = "mps"
34	    elif torch.cuda.is_available():
35	        device = "cuda"
36	    else:
37	        device = "cpu"
38	    print(f"Device: {device}\n")
39	
40	    # ── load chunks ──
41	    if not CHUNKS_FILE.exists():
42	        print(f"✗ {CHUNKS_FILE} not found — run 05_chunk.py first")
43	        sys.exit(1)
44	
45	    print("Loading chunks...", flush=True)
46	    chunks = []
47	    with open(CHUNKS_FILE, encoding="utf-8") as f:
48	        for line in f:
49	            line = line.strip()
50	            if line:
51	                chunks.append(json.loads(line))
52	    n = len(chunks)
53	    print(f"✓ {n} chunks\n", flush=True)
54	
55	    # ── load model ──
56	    print(f"Loading {MODEL_NAME}...", flush=True)
57	    model = SentenceTransformer(MODEL_NAME, device=device, trust_remote_code=True)
58	
59	    # ── embed ──
60	    texts = [DOC_PREFIX + c["text"] for c in chunks]
61	    print(f"Embedding {n} chunks  •  batch={BATCH_SIZE}  •  device={device}\n", flush=True)
62	
63	    try:
64	        from tqdm import tqdm
65	        show_progress = True
66	    except ImportError:
67	        show_progress = False
68	
69	    embeddings = model.encode(
70	        texts,
71	        batch_size=BATCH_SIZE,
72	        normalize_embeddings=True,
73	        show_progress_bar=show_progress,
74	        convert_to_numpy=True,
75	    )
76	
77	    # ── FAISS index ──
78	    dim   = embeddings.shape[1]
79	    index = faiss.IndexFlatIP(dim)   # inner product on L2-normed vecs = cosine similarity
80	    index.add(embeddings.astype(np.float32))
81	    faiss.write_index(index, str(INDEX_FILE))
82	    print(f"\n✓ FAISS index  → {INDEX_FILE}  ({dim}-dim, {index.ntotal} vectors)")
83	
84	    # ── metadata ──
85	    with open(META_FILE, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
86	        for chunk in chunks:
87	            f.write(json.dumps(chunk, ensure_ascii=False) + "\n")
88	    print(f"✓ Metadata     → {META_FILE}")
89	
90	    print(f"\nTo query: embed with prefix 'search_query: ', then index.search(vec, k)")
91	
92	
93	if __name__ == "__main__":
94	    main()
95

1	/**
2	 * REEtrieve Intelligence — chat API server
3	 * POST /api/chat  →  SSE stream of Ollama responses grounded in RAG corpus
4	 *
5	 * Run alongside Vite:  npm run dev:all
6	 * Requires:  Ollama running locally (ollama serve) with a model pulled.
7	 *
8	 * Quick setup:
9	 *   brew install ollama
10	 *   ollama serve          # or open Ollama.app
11	 *   ollama pull llama3.2  # ~2 GB — good quality/speed balance
12	 *
13	 * Override model or URL via .env:
14	 *   OLLAMA_MODEL=mistral
15	 *   OLLAMA_URL=http://localhost:11434
16	 */
17	
18	import express from 'express'
19	import { readFileSync, existsSync } from 'fs'
20	import path from 'path'
21	import { fileURLToPath } from 'url'
22	import 'dotenv/config'
23	
24	const __dirname = path.dirname(fileURLToPath(import.meta.url))
25	const app = express()
26	app.use(express.json({ limit: '1mb' }))
27	
28	const PORT         = process.env.PORT        ?? 3001
29	const OLLAMA_URL   = process.env.OLLAMA_URL  ?? 'http://localhost:11434'
30	const OLLAMA_MODEL = process.env.OLLAMA_MODEL ?? 'llama3.2'
31	
32	// ── Load RAG corpus once at startup ──────────────────────────────────────────
33	const CHUNKS_PATH = process.env.CHUNKS_PATH
34	  ?? path.resolve(__dirname, '../../rag_prep/data/chunks/chunks.jsonl')
35	
36	let CHUNKS = []
37	if (existsSync(CHUNKS_PATH)) {
38	  const lines = readFileSync(CHUNKS_PATH, 'utf8').trim().split('\n').filter(Boolean)
39	  CHUNKS = lines.map(l => { try { return JSON.parse(l) } catch { return null } }).filter(Boolean)
40	  console.log(`✓ Loaded ${CHUNKS.length} RAG chunks from ${path.basename(path.dirname(CHUNKS_PATH))}/chunks.jsonl`)
41	} else {
42	  console.warn(`⚠ chunks.jsonl not found at:\n  ${CHUNKS_PATH}\n  Running in entity-data-only mode (RAG disabled).\n  Run: cd rag_prep && python 05_chunk.py`)
43	}
44	
45	// ── Keyword-based chunk retrieval ─────────────────────────────────────────────
46	const STOPWORDS = new Set([
47	  'the','and','for','that','this','with','are','from','into','what',
48	  'which','have','been','will','when','where','how','does','can','its',
49	  'their','about','used','also','over','more','than','such',
50	])
51	
52	function retrieveChunks(entityName, query, topK = 7) {
53	  if (!CHUNKS.length) return []
54	
55	  // Build token set: entity name tokens + meaningful query tokens
56	  const entityTokens = entityName.toLowerCase().split(/\W+/).filter(t => t.length > 2)
57	  const queryTokens  = query.toLowerCase().split(/\W+/)
58	    .filter(t => t.length > 3 && !STOPWORDS.has(t))
59	  const allTokens    = [...new Set([...entityTokens, ...queryTokens])]
60	
61	  return CHUNKS
62	    .map(chunk => {
63	      const haystack = (chunk.text || chunk.content || '').toLowerCase()
64	      // Entity tokens worth 2 pts each (high precision), query tokens 1 pt
65	      const score =
66	        entityTokens.reduce((s, t) => s + (haystack.includes(t) ? 2 : 0), 0) +
67	        queryTokens.reduce((s, t)  => s + (haystack.includes(t) ? 1 : 0), 0)
68	      return { ...chunk, _score: score }
69	    })
70	    .filter(c => c._score > 0)
71	    .sort((a, b) => b._score - a._score)
72	    .slice(0, topK)
73	}
74	
75	// ── System prompt ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
76	function buildSystemPrompt(entity, chunks) {
77	  const chunkBlock = chunks.length
78	    ? chunks.map((c, i) => [
79	        `[DOC ${i + 1}] ${c.source ?? c.source_type ?? 'Unknown source'} | Year: ${c.year ?? '?'} | Page: ${c.page ?? '?'}`,
80	        c.url ? `URL: ${c.url}` : null,
81	        '',
82	        c.text ?? c.content ?? '',
83	      ].filter(x => x !== null).join('\n'))
84	      .join('\n\n' + '─'.repeat(60) + '\n\n')
85	    : 'No RAG corpus available for this session. Base your analysis on the entity signal data only and be explicit about this limitation.'
86	
87	  const signalLegend = [
88	    'cl1 = Unexplained Production Spike (USGS / EITI)',
89	    'cl2 = Regional Child Labour Prevalence (ILO ILOSTAT)',
90	    'cl3 = Low School Enrolment + Literacy (UNESCO)',
91	    'cl4 = Nighttime Luminosity Anomaly (NASA VIIRS)',
92	    'fl1 = Armed Group / Mine Proximity (IPIS / ACLED)',
93	    'fl2 = ILO Forced Labour Indicator (ILO 2022)',
94	    'fl3 = Debt Bondage / Wage Withholding (Amnesty Intl)',
95	    'fl4 = AIS Dark Shipping Events (MarineTraffic)',
96	    'df1 = Comtrade Mirror-Trade Gap (UN Comtrade)',
97	    'df2 = AIS Dark Events at Transit Port (MarineTraffic)',
98	    'df3 = Certificate Temporal Fraud (RMI RMAP / EITI)',
99	    'df4 = Mine Area vs Concession Mismatch (Sentinel-2)',
100	    'df5 = Invoice Price Below Market Floor (LME / Fastmarkets)',
101	    'mr1 = RMI AMRT Non-Compliance (RMI 2024)',
102	    'mr2 = GISTM Tailings Extreme/Very High (Global Tailings Portal 2025)',
103	    'mr3 = BHRRC Allegation Density (BHRRC TMT 2024)',
104	    'mr4 = Global Witness Investigative Flag (GW 2024)',
105	    'mr5 = BMI Carbon & Waste ESG Below Median (BMI 2024)',
106	  ].join('\n  ')
107	
108	  const preset = entity.signalPreset ?? {}
109	  const signalLines = Object.entries(preset)
110	    .map(([k, v]) => `  ${k}: ${v.toFixed(2)}`)
111	    .join('\n')
112	
113	  return `You are REEtrieve Intelligence, an investigative AI embedded in the REEtrieve Compliance Platform. You specialise in critical mineral supply chains — specifically rare earth elements (REE), cobalt, nickel, tin, and tungsten.
114	
115	Your audience is compliance officers, regulators, journalists, and auditors who need rigorous, cited, probabilistic analysis — not reassurance.
116	
117	${'═'.repeat(65)}
118	ENTITY UNDER REVIEW
119	${'═'.repeat(65)}
120	Name:            ${entity.name}
121	Country:         ${entity.flag ?? ''} ${entity.country}
122	Industry:        ${entity.industry}
123	Risk Score:      ${entity.riskScore}/100  (Bayesian composite, 4 dimensions)
124	Cert Status:     ${entity.certStatus ?? 'unknown'}
125	Last Audit:      ${entity.lastAudit ?? 'never'}
126	Risk Flags:      ${(entity.riskFlags ?? []).join(' | ') || 'none'}
127	Intel Note:      ${entity.note ?? ''}
128	
129	SIGNAL ACTIVATIONS (0 = no signal, 1 = fully active):
130	${signalLines}
131	
132	SIGNAL LEGEND:
133	  ${signalLegend}
134	
135	${'═'.repeat(65)}
136	RELEVANT DOCUMENT EXCERPTS  (ranked by relevance to your query)
137	${'═'.repeat(65)}
138	${chunkBlock}
139	
140	${'═'.repeat(65)}
141	ANALYTICAL RULES — APPLY TO EVERY RESPONSE
142	${'═'.repeat(65)}
143	1. CITATION REQUIRED: Every factual claim must end with its source in parentheses.
144	   Use exact names: (USGS MCS 2024), (UN Comtrade 2023), (ILO ILOSTAT 2022),
145	   (RMI 2024), (BHRRC TMT 2024), (Global Witness 2024), (BMI 2024),
146	   or (REEtrieve signal data) for values derived from signal activations above.
147	
148	2. TWO-SIDED ANALYSIS: After each risk finding, state the strongest
149	   counter-argument or benign alternative interpretation, then explain
150	   why you accept or reject it given the available evidence.
151	
152	3. RELATIVIZE SCORES: Interpret numbers in context.
153	   The 13-entity database range is roughly 38–95. Quartile breakpoints:
154	   ≥82 = top quartile (critical), 68–81 = high, 52–67 = elevated, <52 = lower.
155	   Always say where this entity sits.
156	
157	4. PROBABILISTIC FRAMING: Use "the evidence is consistent with...",
158	   "with high/moderate/low confidence...", "this cannot be confirmed but...".
159	   Never state certainties. Supply chains are inherently uncertain.
160	
161	5. MISSING DATA IS A SIGNAL: If a dataset doesn't cover this entity,
162	   name the gap explicitly and explain what inference is still possible.
163	   E.g., absence from GISTM disclosures after the 2025 mandatory deadline
164	   is itself non-compliance, not a neutral absence.
165	
166	6. TEMPORAL LAG: Materials extracted today appear in products in 2–3 years.
167	   A battery sold in 2025 contains ore from 2022–2023. Always anchor
168	   downstream product claims to the correct extraction window.
169	
170	7. DOWNSTREAM TRACING: Trace material flows step by step using the document
171	   corpus: mine → smelter → refiner → manufacturer → brand. State each
172	   inference link explicitly. Only name companies that appear in cited documents.
173	   If a company cannot be confirmed, say so rather than speculating.
174	
175	8. SCOPE GUARD: You cover REE, cobalt, nickel, tin, tungsten only.
176	   If asked about other minerals, note this is outside your current dataset.
177	
178	9. ADVERSARIAL LENS: Always ask — could this data have been falsified?
179	   Who benefits from the official narrative? Flag where document fraud
180	   signals (df1–df5) suggest the numbers themselves may be unreliable.`
181	}
182	
183	// ── Chat endpoint ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
184	app.post('/api/chat', async (req, res) => {
185	  const { entity, messages } = req.body
186	
187	  if (!entity || !Array.isArray(messages) || !messages.length) {
188	    return res.status(400).json({ error: 'entity and messages[] required' })
189	  }
190	
191	  // Extract last user message for retrieval
192	  const lastUser = [...messages].reverse().find(m => m.role === 'user')?.content ?? ''
193	  const chunks   = retrieveChunks(entity.name, lastUser)
194	
195	  const systemPrompt = buildSystemPrompt(entity, chunks)
196	
197	  // SSE headers
198	  res.setHeader('Content-Type', 'text/event-stream')
199	  res.setHeader('Cache-Control', 'no-cache')
200	  res.setHeader('Connection', 'keep-alive')
201	  res.setHeader('X-Accel-Buffering', 'no')
202	  res.flushHeaders()
203	
204	  try {
205	    // Ollama OpenAI-compatible chat endpoint with streaming
206	    const model = req.body.model ?? OLLAMA_MODEL
207	    const ollamaRes = await fetch(`${OLLAMA_URL}/api/chat`, {
208	      method: 'POST',
209	      headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
210	      body: JSON.stringify({
211	        model,
212	        stream: true,
213	        options: { num_predict: 1500, temperature: 0.3 },
214	        messages: [
215	          { role: 'system', content: systemPrompt },
216	          ...messages.map(m => ({ role: m.role, content: m.content })),
217	        ],
218	      }),
219	    })
220	
221	    if (!ollamaRes.ok) {
222	      const err = await ollamaRes.text()
223	      throw new Error(`Ollama ${ollamaRes.status}: ${err}`)
224	    }
225	
226	    const reader  = ollamaRes.body.getReader()
227	    const decoder = new TextDecoder()
228	    let   buffer  = ''
229	
230	    while (true) {
231	      const { done, value } = await reader.read()
232	      if (done) break
233	      buffer += decoder.decode(value, { stream: true })
234	      const lines = buffer.split('\n')
235	      buffer = lines.pop()
236	      for (const line of lines) {
237	        if (!line.trim()) continue
238	        try {
239	          const json = JSON.parse(line)
240	          const text = json.message?.content
241	          if (text) res.write(`data: ${JSON.stringify({ text })}\n\n`)
242	          if (json.done) {
243	            res.write('data: [DONE]\n\n')
244	            res.end()
245	            return
246	          }
247	        } catch { /* partial JSON — skip */ }
248	      }
249	    }
250	
251	    res.write('data: [DONE]\n\n')
252	    res.end()
253	  } catch (err) {
254	    console.error('Ollama error:', err.message)
255	    if (!res.headersSent) {
256	      res.status(500).json({ error: err.message })
257	    } else {
258	      res.write(`data: ${JSON.stringify({ error: err.message })}\n\n`)
259	      res.end()
260	    }
261	  }
262	})
263	
264	app.listen(PORT, () => {
265	  console.log(`\nREEtrieve chat API  →  http://localhost:${PORT}`)
266	  console.log(`Ollama:              ${OLLAMA_URL}`)
267	  console.log(`Model:               ${OLLAMA_MODEL}`)
268	  console.log(`RAG corpus:          ${CHUNKS.length} chunks loaded\n`)
269	})

query: `importlib.metadata.version("pandas")` returns `None` — conda-installed pandas has no pip metadata. This is a conda/pip conflict that's hard to fix cleanly. Let me just rewrite the script to avoid `sentence-transformers` and its `datasets` dependency entirely:
original_content:
1	"""
2	Step 2 — Chunking & metadata tagging.
3	Converts all raw documents into data/chunks/chunks.jsonl
4	Uses multiprocessing to parallelize across files.
5	"""
6	
7	import json, pathlib, re, hashlib, sys
8	from multiprocessing import Pool, cpu_count
9	from typing import Iterator
10	
11	# ── config ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
12	CHUNK_SIZE  = 1_600
13	OVERLAP     = 200
14	MIN_CHUNK   = 120
15	WORKERS     = min(4, max(1, cpu_count() - 3))
16	
17	RAW_ROOT    = pathlib.Path(__file__).parent / "data/raw"
18	TEXT_ROOT   = pathlib.Path(__file__).parent / "data/text"
19	OUT_DIR     = pathlib.Path(__file__).parent / "data/chunks"
20	OUT_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
21	CHUNKS_FILE = OUT_DIR / "chunks.jsonl"
22	
23	# Pre-compiled regexes
24	_RE_NULL   = re.compile(r'\x00')
25	_RE_SPACES = re.compile(r'[ \t]{3,}')
26	_RE_LINES  = re.compile(r'\n{4,}')
27	_RE_HYPHEN = re.compile(r'([a-z])-\n([a-z])')
28	
29	
30	# ── helpers ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
31	
32	def tokens_approx(text: str) -> int:
33	    return max(1, len(text) // 4)
34	
35	def make_id(source_type: str, doc_name: str, page, chunk_idx: int) -> str:
36	    return f"{source_type}__{doc_name}__p{page}__c{chunk_idx}"
37	
38	def clean_text(text: str) -> str:
39	    text = _RE_NULL.sub('', text)
40	    text = _RE_SPACES.sub('  ', text)
41	    text = _RE_LINES.sub('\n\n\n', text)
42	    text = _RE_HYPHEN.sub(r'\1\2', text)
43	    return text.strip()
44	
45	def sliding_window(text: str) -> Iterator[str]:
46	    text = text.strip()
47	    if not text:
48	        return
49	    if len(text) <= CHUNK_SIZE:
50	        if len(text) >= MIN_CHUNK:
51	            yield text
52	        return
53	    start = 0
54	    while start < len(text):
55	        end   = start + CHUNK_SIZE
56	        chunk = text[start:end]
57	        if end < len(text):
58	            bp = max(chunk.rfind(". ", len(chunk) - 200),
59	                     chunk.rfind("\n",  len(chunk) - 200))
60	            if bp > len(chunk) // 2:
61	                chunk = chunk[:bp + 1]
62	        chunk = chunk.strip()
63	        if len(chunk) >= MIN_CHUNK:
64	            yield chunk
65	        start += len(chunk) - OVERLAP
66	
67	
68	# ── metadata builders ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
69	
70	def usgs_meta(p: pathlib.Path) -> dict:
71	    m    = re.search(r'(\d{4})', p.stem)
72	    year = int(m.group(1)) if m else None
73	    el   = next((e for e in ["lithium","cobalt","rare-earths","nickel",
74	                              "graphite","manganese","tungsten","tin"]
75	                 if e.replace("-","_") in p.stem or e in p.stem), None)
76	    label = f"USGS Mineral Commodity Summary {year}" + (f" — {el.title()}" if el else "")
77	    return {"source": label, "source_type": "usgs", "year": year, "element": el,
78	            "url": f"https://pubs.usgs.gov/periodicals/mcs{year}/mcs{year}.pdf",
79	            "doc_path": str(p.relative_to(RAW_ROOT))}
80	
81	def usgs_text_meta(p: pathlib.Path) -> dict:
82	    return usgs_meta(pathlib.Path(str(p).replace("data/text/", "data/raw/").replace(".txt", ".pdf")))
83	
84	def sec_meta(p: pathlib.Path) -> dict:
85	    company = p.parent.name
86	    return {"source": f"SEC Form SD — Conflict Minerals Disclosure ({company.upper()})",
87	            "source_type": "sec_edgar", "year": None, "company": company,
88	            "url": "https://www.sec.gov/cgi-bin/browse-edgar",
89	            "doc_path": str(p.relative_to(RAW_ROOT))}
90	
91	def comtrade_meta(p: pathlib.Path) -> dict:
92	    parts = p.stem.split("_")
93	    year  = int(parts[-1]) if parts[-1].isdigit() else None
94	    flow  = "Exports" if "X" in parts else "Imports"
95	    return {"source": f"UN Comtrade — HS {parts[0]} {flow} {year}",
96	            "source_type": "comtrade", "year": year, "hs_code": parts[0], "flow": flow,
97	            "url": "https://comtradeplus.un.org/",
98	            "doc_path": str(p.relative_to(RAW_ROOT))}
99	
100	def misc_meta(p: pathlib.Path) -> dict:
101	    raw_str = str(p).replace("/data/text/misc/", "/data/raw/misc/").replace(".txt", ".pdf")
102	    try:
103	        doc_path = str(pathlib.Path(raw_str).relative_to(RAW_ROOT))
104	    except ValueError:
105	        doc_path = p.name
106	    return {"source": f"External Report — {p.stem.replace('_',' ').title()}",
107	            "source_type": "misc", "year": None, "url": "", "doc_path": doc_path}
108	
109	
110	# ── worker (runs in subprocess) ───────────────────────────────────────────────
111	
112	def process_file(args: tuple) -> list[dict]:
113	    path_str, meta_fn_name, is_pdf = args
114	    path     = pathlib.Path(path_str)
115	    meta_fn  = globals()[meta_fn_name]
116	    meta     = meta_fn(path)
117	
118	    if is_pdf:
119	        # should not happen — all PDFs pre-extracted, but keep as safety net
120	        try:
121	            import pdfplumber
122	            chunks = []
123	            with pdfplumber.open(path) as pdf:
124	                for pg_num, pg in enumerate(pdf.pages, 1):
125	                    raw = pg.extract_text()
126	                    if not raw:
127	                        continue
128	                    text = clean_text(raw)
129	                    for idx, chunk in enumerate(sliding_window(text)):
130	                        chunks.append({**meta,
131	                            "id": make_id(meta["source_type"], path.stem, pg_num, idx),
132	                            "text": chunk, "page": pg_num, "chunk_index": idx,
133	                            "tokens_approx": tokens_approx(chunk)})
134	            return chunks
135	        except Exception as e:
136	            print(f"  ✗ PDF {path.name}: {e}", flush=True)
137	            return []
138	
139	    try:
140	        raw  = path.read_text(encoding="utf-8", errors="ignore")
141	        text = clean_text(raw)
142	        return [{**meta,
143	                 "id": make_id(meta["source_type"], path.stem, 0, idx),
144	                 "text": chunk, "page": None, "chunk_index": idx,
145	                 "tokens_approx": tokens_approx(chunk)}
146	                for idx, chunk in enumerate(sliding_window(text))]
147	    except Exception as e:
148	        print(f"  ✗ {path.name}: {e}", flush=True)
149	        return []
150	
151	
152	# ── file collection ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
153	
154	_misc_pre = {p.stem for p in (TEXT_ROOT/"misc").glob("*.txt")} \
155	            if (TEXT_ROOT/"misc").exists() else set()
156	
157	SOURCES = [
158	    (TEXT_ROOT/"usgs",        "*.txt",    False, "usgs_text_meta"),
159	    (TEXT_ROOT/"misc",        "*.txt",    False, "misc_meta"),
160	    (RAW_ROOT/"sec_edgar",    "**/*.txt", False, "sec_meta"),
161	    (RAW_ROOT/"comtrade_text","*.txt",    False, "comtrade_meta"),
162	    (RAW_ROOT/"misc",         "**/*.txt", False, "misc_meta"),
163	]
164	
165	def collect_files() -> list[tuple]:
166	    files = []
167	    seen  = set()
168	    for folder, glob, is_pdf, fn_name in SOURCES:
169	        if not folder.exists():
170	            continue
171	        for p in sorted(folder.glob(glob)):
172	            if not p.is_file() or p.name == "_manifest.json":
173	                continue
174	            key = p.stem
175	            if key in seen:          # skip raw/misc txt if already in text/misc
176	                continue
177	            seen.add(key)
178	            files.append((str(p), fn_name, is_pdf))
179	    # any misc PDFs not yet pre-extracted
180	    misc_pdf = RAW_ROOT / "misc"
181	    if misc_pdf.exists():
182	        for p in sorted(misc_pdf.glob("**/*.pdf")):
183	            if p.stem not in _misc_pre:
184	                files.append((str(p), "misc_meta", True))
185	    return files
186	
187	
188	# ── main ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
189	
190	def main():
191	    files = collect_files()
192	    n     = len(files)
193	    print(f"\n✓ {n} source files  •  {WORKERS} workers\n")
194	
195	    seen_hashes : set[str] = set()
196	    total_chunks = total_tokens = skipped = 0
197	
198	    with open(CHUNKS_FILE, "w", encoding="utf-8") as out, \
199	         Pool(processes=WORKERS) as pool:
200	
201	        for i, chunks in enumerate(pool.imap_unordered(process_file, files, chunksize=1), 1):
202	            name = pathlib.Path(files[i-1][0]).name if i <= n else "?"
203	            pct  = int(i / n * 100)
204	            print(f"  [{i:>3}/{n}] {pct:>3}%  +{len(chunks):>4} chunks  {name}", flush=True)
205	
206	            for chunk in chunks:
207	                h = hashlib.md5(chunk["text"].encode()).hexdigest()[:8]
208	                if h in seen_hashes:
209	                    skipped += 1
210	                    continue
211	                seen_hashes.add(h)

217	    by_type: dict[str, int] = {}

221	            by_type[st] = by_type.get(st, 0) + 1
222	
223	    mx = max(by_type.values()) if by_type else 1
224	    print(f"\n{'═'*55}")
225	    print(f"  Chunks written : {total_chunks:,}  →  {CHUNKS_FILE}")
226	    print(f"  Approx tokens  : {total_tokens:,}  (~{total_tokens//1000}k)")
227	    print(f"  Duplicates     : {skipped:,}")
228	    print(f"\n  By source type:")
229	    for st, c in sorted(by_type.items(), key=lambda x: -x[1]):
230	        print(f"    {st:<18} {c:>6}  {'█' * (c // max(1, mx // 30))}")
231	    print(f"{'═'*55}")
232	    print(f"\n  ✓ Ready →  python 06_embed.py")
233	
234	
235	if __name__ == "__main__":
236	    main()
